themes.name: Semiconductor Wafer Foundry & Advanced IC Manufacturing
themes.description: The move this week is being driven mainly by a broad bid into semiconductor equipment on growing expectations for a sustained AI-related capex upcycle at foundries and memory makers, following recent bullish commentary and orders from leading chip producers. That’s being reinforced by rotation into cyclical growth/tech ahead of upcoming earnings, with investors positioning for stronger WFE and advanced-node tooling demand in 2H. Additionally, easing concerns about export controls and supply-chain disruptions have helped sentiment across semi-cap equipment, lifting AMAT, LRCX, VECO, UMC, KLAC, and ACMR together.

themes.name: Inflammatory Disease & Immunology Biologics
themes.description: The main catalyst lifting ORKA and SYRE this week is read-through from AbbVie’s $10.9B acquisition of Apogee, which has triggered a re-rating of immunology and Paragon spinout peers. Sell-side commentary has explicitly flagged JBIO, ORKA, SYRE, and CBIO as beneficiaries of this deal-driven scramble ahead of the patent cliff. There is also incremental attention from EULAR-related pipeline coverage, but the AbbVie/Apogee deal read-through is the dominant driver.

themes.name: Semiconductor Probe Card & Front-End Test Equipment
themes.description: Teradyne (TER) and Cohu (COHU) are moving higher this week primarily on the back of renewed analyst enthusiasm for semiconductor test and capital equipment, highlighted by Argus raising its target price on Teradyne to $482 on June 24, 2026. That upgrade and similar target hikes across the semi equipment complex are reinforcing the AI/advanced packaging growth narrative and improving sentiment toward test-focused names. Broader sector strength tied to expectations for continued HBM, data center, and AI-related capex is providing additional tailwind.

themes.name: Liquid Biopsy & Multi-Cancer Early Detection
themes.description: GH is up this week mainly on renewed enthusiasm around cancer diagnostics following GRAIL’s recent progress on its Galleri multi-cancer screening program and PMA filing, which benefits sentiment for peers in blood-based early detection. GRAL itself is moving on anticipation of mid‑2026 detailed data from PATHFINDER 2 and the full NHS-Galleri study plus the February completion of the final PMA module for Galleri, which positions the test for FDA approval and broader commercialization. The broader bid into oncology diagnostics and screening names on these regulatory and data milestones is lifting both tickers.

themes.name: Targeted Protein Degradation Oncology
themes.description: KYMR is being driven higher by the recent positive Phase 1 data for its oral STAT6 degrader KT-621 in atopic dermatitis, which has led to multiple bullish headlines and renewed enthusiasm around its protein degradation platform. In addition, the strong Q1 2026 earnings beat on both revenue and EPS and subsequent analyst target raises (e.g., B. Riley lifting its PT to 155) are reinforcing the move. GLUE’s strength this week is more tied to follow-through from its earlier 300% surge and recent capital raise, plus insider activity (CEO share sale) highlighting the stock’s sharp re-rating in the molecular glue space alongside KYMR.

themes.name: Peptide & Hormone Therapies for Metabolic & Endocrine Disorders
themes.description: I don’t have enough reliable result coverage here to identify the specific catalyst for ASND, MBX, XERS, VKTX, and CORT from the provided data. The most likely driver is a sector-wide biotech/clinical-data move, but I can’t verify a single shared news item from these results.

themes.name: Casual Dining Restaurant Turnaround
themes.description: CBRL is trading higher on follow‑through from its blockbuster Q3 FY26 print and significantly raised full‑year guidance (notably the big step‑up in EBITDA outlook), which reset expectations for the earnings and margin trajectory. In addition, its high short‑interest profile is making it a favored squeeze/mean‑reversion candidate as sentiment flips from deeply bearish to more constructive. For EAT, the move is being driven by read‑through from stronger casual‑dining prints and guidance (including CBRL’s), ahead of its own upcoming earnings and on positioning for continued traffic and margin improvement across the space.

themes.name: Inflammatory Disease & Immunology Biologics
themes.description: The main catalyst lifting ORKA and SYRE this week is read-through from AbbVie’s $10.9B acquisition of Apogee, which has triggered a re-rating of immunology and Paragon spinout peers. Sell-side commentary has explicitly flagged JBIO, ORKA, SYRE, and CBIO as beneficiaries of this deal-driven scramble ahead of the patent cliff. There is also incremental attention from EULAR-related pipeline coverage, but the AbbVie/Apogee deal read-through is the dominant driver.

themes.name: U.S. Passenger Airlines
themes.description: The move across UAL, ALGT, ALK, SKYW, DAL, and LUV is being driven primarily by a sector-wide bid into airlines on strong summer travel demand and improving cash generation and fundamentals highlighted in recent corporate updates and analyst commentary. Several names, notably UAL and SKYW, have also seen fresh analyst target hikes and Buy reiterations, reinforcing the bullish tone and pulling the group higher together. There doesn’t appear to be a single company-specific headline; it’s a broad “airlines momentum”/earnings and demand setup that’s getting repriced higher this week.

themes.name: Golf Equipment & Apparel
themes.description: The main catalyst this week is a fresh UBS rating action on Callaway (CALY), with the firm maintaining a Neutral rating but raising its price target from $15 to $19, which has improved sentiment and drawn in momentum buyers. That upgrade comes on the back of investors reassessing Callaway’s heavy Topgolf-related investment as potentially turning into more sustainable returns, helping the stock catch a bid despite prior concerns about muted revenue growth and weak free cash flow.

themes.name: AI Data Center Power Infrastructure
themes.description: The main catalyst is continued enthusiasm for data center and grid infrastructure plays tied to AI buildout and next‑gen power demands, which directly benefits Vertiv (VRT), Bloom Energy (BE), Powrtec/related power plays (PWR), AGX, and fuel‑cell names like FCEL as investors rotate into “picks and shovels” for AI and electrification. VRT and AGX are being highlighted in thematic pieces as critical providers of cooling, power, and gas plant infrastructure for AI racks and tech‑heavy grids, while BE and FCEL are catching sympathy bids as investors look for alternative and clean‑energy power solutions that could support data centers and industrial loads.

themes.name: Satellite Communications & Space Data Services
themes.description: The main catalyst is a sector-wide rerating of space and satellite names after Rocket Lab’s agreement to acquire Iridium and the sharp move higher in the Tema Space Innovators ETF, which concentrates flows into holdings like VSAT, MDA, SPIR, and ASTS. Alongside that, investors are positioning ahead of SpaceX IPO chatter and direct‑to‑device / space‑based broadband deployment milestones (e.g., ASTS BlueBird constellation), which is lifting the whole “new space” basket. There is no single company‑specific headline this week for those four; they are moving primarily on thematic inflows and momentum across space infrastructure and communications.

themes.name: Specialty Catastrophe Property Insurance Underwriters
themes.description: There is no clear, stock-specific news catalyst pushing NP/SLDE higher this week; the move appears to be driven mainly by speculative interest and momentum rather than a fresh fundamental development. Recent filings and company news do not show any major new contracts, regulatory decisions, or M&A that would explain the spike. In this context, traders are likely treating it as a technical/low-float play in a mixed broader market tape.

themes.name: Precision Optics & Photonic Components
themes.description: OPTX and LPTH are being driven higher primarily by their recent inclusion in the Russell 3000 index, which is attracting passive index and ETF buying, plus incremental attention from AI/photonics-focused investors. In addition, OPTX reported a new U.S. military order to expand AI-driven AR display optics, providing a fundamental contract win that supports the move. LASR is moving in sympathy as part of the broader AI photonics/optical interconnects theme that traders are rotating into this week.

themes.name: European Bank Stocks
themes.description: The move in European and UK banks this week is being driven mainly by rising market expectations for a more prolonged higher-rate environment in Europe and the UK, which improves net interest margins and earnings leverage for the sector. In addition, better-than-feared macro data in the eurozone and UK, plus ongoing rotation into value/cyclicals after recent underperformance, is supporting large-cap banks such as SAN, NWG, BCS, LYG, BBVA, and ING. There is no single stock-specific headline; it is a sector-wide macro/rates trade.

themes.name: Property & Casualty Insurance Underwriters
themes.description: The main catalyst is a sector-wide bid into U.S. property & casualty insurers as investors rotate into defensive, cash-generative financials ahead of the upcoming earnings season and potential Fed rate path inflection. Higher short rates and still-firm commercial pricing support margin expansion screens for names like PGR, CINF, AFG, HCI, SKWD, and AXS, drawing in both fundamental and quant flows. There is no stock-specific news driving these individually; it is a group move tied to macro (rates, credit conditions) and insurance pricing expectations.

themes.name: Crypto Asset Recovery
themes.description: The main catalyst driving WULF, RIOT, and HUT higher this week is renewed bullish sentiment around Bitcoin miners pivoting to AI/HPC infrastructure, highlighted by Riot’s high-profile molten salt nuclear data center deal and associated price target hike, which is lifting the whole peer group. In parallel, analysts have been raising TeraWulf’s price targets on the back of its expanding multi-gigawatt power pipeline, hyperscaler-backed AI data center contracts, and capital-light lease model, reinforcing the AI infrastructure narrative for the basket. More tactically, traders are leaning into the “Bitcoin miners-as-AI plays” theme, with social chatter flagging short-squeeze potential in names like WULF alongside RIOT’s breakout.

themes.name: Industrial Power Equipment & Electrical Systems
themes.description: ETN, GNRC, and RRX are all benefiting from the ongoing AI/data center and grid-upgrade theme, which is driving strong flows into power management and specialty industrial machinery names, with Generac highlighted among top performers in the sector. For ETN specifically, the major catalyst is continued AI-driven data center demand and grid-constrained power investment, reinforced by bullish technicals (10-day MA crossing above 50-day MA) and recent analyst target hikes. GNRC and RRX are moving in sympathy as investors rotate into electrification, backup power, and industrial safety beneficiaries ahead of the next earnings season in industrials.

themes.name: Life Insurance & Annuity Providers
themes.description: The main catalyst pushing PRU, MET, PFG and UNM higher this week is sector-wide rotation into defensive financials and value, as investors reassess U.S. insurance names in a backdrop of elevated rates, strong equity markets and resilient earnings expectations. Large life and retirement insurers are benefiting from improved spread income, higher investment yields and renewed interest in dividend-paying value stocks as growth/AI leaders consolidate and volatility picks up. There is no single company-specific headline driving the move; it is a broad insurance/financials bid tied to macro positioning and the relative attractiveness of their earnings and valuations.

themes.name: Private Mortgage Insurance
themes.description: The move in RDN, MTG, ESNT, and NMIH this week is primarily tied to renewed bullish sentiment on private mortgage insurers as recurring premium income, strong cash flow, and underwriting profitability are being highlighted in recent analyst and media coverage. Positive write-ups emphasizing long-term earnings growth and resilient housing-credit fundamentals for Radian, NMI Holdings, and MGIC are reinforcing the sector’s defensive growth narrative and attracting incremental long-only and yield-focused flows. There is no single company-specific headline; this is a group move driven by favorable fundamentals and rotation into financials/housing-related plays.

themes.name: Precision Aerospace & Defense Component Manufacturers
themes.description: The likely catalyst is ADX’s expansion with a new GCC dividend ETF, which is broadening regional exposure and appears to be boosting sentiment around the exchange. That’s the only clear news item in the feed tied directly to ADX strength this week.

themes.name: Senior Living & Housing Operators
themes.description: BKD is moving higher on continued positive sentiment from its CEO transition and activist involvement, with the company recently initiating a search for a new CEO and having its director slate elected following a proxy fight with Ortelius. In addition, fundamentals are improving, with Brookdale highlighting higher occupancy trends and incremental growth from recently announced community acquisitions, which supports the upside in the January calls.

themes.name: Midstream Energy Infrastructure & Pipeline Operators
themes.description: The move is mainly being driven by the midstream/energy tape firming on higher natural-gas and LNG-related sentiment, plus project/earnings optimism around pipeline growth and cash flows. Among these names, TRGP is also likely benefiting from AI/data-center power demand exposure, while TRP and KMI are more tied to LNG export and pipeline catalysts. AM and DTM look like they’re trading in sympathy with the stronger midstream group rather than on a company-specific headline.

themes.name: Multifamily Apartment REITs
themes.description: The move in CPT, MAA, and UDR this week is being driven less by stock‑specific news and more by a sector-wide bid into multifamily REITs on expectations of lower rates and stabilizing Sunbelt apartment fundamentals. Apartment REITs have been trading tightly correlated, and UDR’s recent positive narrative around easing new supply and operational improvements has been bleeding over into peers like CPT and MAA via that high correlation. In short, it’s a macro/sector re‑rating story (rates, supply, demand) rather than a new company-specific catalyst for any one of the three.

themes.name: Insurance Brokerage & Risk Advisory
themes.description: Insurance brokers are quietly strengthening with BWIN posting a dramatic RS reversal from 11 to 90 over 4 weeks — one of the strongest rotation signals in the dataset. The correlation cluster (AJG/AON/BRO/MRSH/WTW) moves together at 0.87 correlation, and BRO also appears in rotation candidates. Hard insurance market conditions and M&A activity in the brokerage space are driving fee growth. MRSH and WTW are new additions not yet in any active theme.

themes.name: U.S. Petroleum Refining & Downstream Processing
themes.description: The move is being driven by a broad rally in U.S. refiners, likely tied to stronger crack spreads and improving refining margins, which lifts names like CVI, DINO, DK, MPC, PBF, and PSX together. I don’t see a company-specific catalyst in the provided results, so this looks more like an industry rotation than isolated news.

themes.name: U.S. Regional & Mid-Cap Commercial Banks
themes.description: Regional banks like EWBC, WTFC, WAL, BOKF, HBAN, and FITB are trading higher this week primarily on improving interest-rate expectations, with markets increasingly pricing in a soft-landing scenario and a more benign rate-cut path that supports net interest margins rather than compressing them abruptly. The group is also benefiting from a rebound in risk appetite toward financials after recent stress-test results and capital plans reinforced confidence in bank balance sheets and dividend/buyback capacity. Additionally, reduced concerns about near-term credit deterioration and commercial real estate exposures are supporting a relief bid across mid-cap and regional bank names.

themes.name: Quantum Computing Hardware & Annealing Systems
themes.description: They’re all riding the same macro quantum-computing wave: the U.S. Commerce Department’s new $2 billion quantum program, which earmarks about $100 million each for D‑Wave (QBTS), Rigetti (RGTI), and Infleqtion (INFQ), plus separate funding for Infleqtion and peers, is being repriced into the group. A recent presidential executive order and high-profile White House/CEO event highlighting quantum, plus new coverage and “Outperform” initiation on Infleqtion, have further spotlighted the space. In that backdrop, speculative names like Quantum Computing Inc. (QUBT) are catching sympathy flows as investors look for broader quantum exposure.

themes.name: Domestic Steel Producers
themes.description: A 4-stock correlation cluster (corr 0.83, avg RS 28) of U.S. steel manufacturers — CMC, NUE, RS, and STLD — is moving in lockstep, likely reflecting shared sensitivity to domestic steel pricing, tariff protection, and infrastructure/construction demand. While RS levels remain moderate, the high correlation suggests capital is beginning to rotate into domestic steel ahead of infrastructure spending acceleration and potential trade policy tailwinds favoring U.S. producers over imported steel.

themes.name: Edge Cloud & Developer CDN Platforms
themes.description: DOCN and FSLY are moving higher this week largely on continued momentum in high‑growth, AI‑adjacent infrastructure names, as investors rotate back into small/mid-cap cloud and edge-compute plays leveraged to rising AI and bandwidth demand. There is no single stock-specific headline driving the move; it’s more a sector-wide rerating in software/infrastructure tied to AI, developers, and edge workloads. Some traders are also positioning ahead of upcoming earnings/guidance resets in the group, expecting improving usage trends and operating leverage.

themes.name: Medical Devices & Surgical Equipment
themes.description: Medical device and surgical equipment companies are recovering in tandem (Cluster E, corr 0.81) as elective procedure volumes normalize post-COVID and hospital capital budgets improve. ATEC, BDX, STE, and SYK represent a broad surgical/device cohort that is quietly rotating higher together, suggesting sector-level institutional reallocation into healthcare equipment.

themes.name: Commercial Real Estate Brokerage & Advisory Services
themes.description: The main driver is a broad bid into U.S. commercial real estate and rate‑sensitive cyclicals as markets increasingly price in Fed cuts and a soft‑landing/early‑cycle narrative, which boosts property service and brokerage names. On top of that, analysts have been highlighting valuation upside and accelerating fundamentals for the group, including Newmark, which is drawing in relative‑value and short‑covering flows across the space. There is no single stock‑specific headline this week; it’s primarily macro (rates and CRE sentiment) plus positioning.

themes.name: Construction Aggregates & Building Materials
themes.description: The move in CRH, MLM, VMC and AMRZ this week is being driven primarily by the rotation back into U.S. infrastructure and construction materials on the combination of lower long-term yields, firmer demand indicators and renewed confidence around multi-year public spending. There has not been a stock‑specific earnings or M&A catalyst; it’s a sector‑wide bid tied to the “re‑accelerating infrastructure cycle” narrative and ongoing buy‑side positioning into aggregates and cement as a defensive growth play. Sector read‑through from peers and improving construction data are reinforcing the move.

themes.name: Insurance Brokerage & Distribution Services
themes.description: The move is largely sector-wide, driven by renewed interest in insurance brokers as defensive, cash-generative plays amid rising rate expectations and macro uncertainty, rather than a single company-specific headline. For AJG, MRSH, AON, BRO and WTW, investors are leaning into the robust Q1 prints, margin expansion narratives, and ongoing M&A/AI productivity themes that support mid‑teens EPS growth even in a softer pricing environment. There is no major discrete news item this week; it’s a rotation back into the broker group on relative earnings quality and resilience.

themes.name: Precious Metals Miners (Gold & Silver)
themes.description: A 29-stock correlation cluster (corr 0.80) spanning gold and silver miners is moving together statistically, reflecting broad precious metals price action driven by macro uncertainty, dollar weakness, and safe-haven demand. While average RS remains low (16), the size and coherence of the cluster signal this is a macro rotation in early stages rather than individual stock stories. Names span royalty streamers (FNV, WPM, RGLD), senior producers (NEM, AU, AEM), and junior miners (EXK, MUX, HL).

themes.name: Defense Drones & Unmanned Aerial Systems
themes.description: Geopolitical conflict and defense budget expansion are driving accelerated procurement of unmanned aerial systems across NATO and allied militaries. AVAV, KTOS, ONDS, RCAT, and UMAC form a tightly correlated cluster (0.85) at low RS — the hallmark of early institutional accumulation before a narrative-driven breakout. Policy tailwinds (DoD drone strategy, Ukraine conflict learnings) provide a durable multi-year catalyst.

themes.name: Coal Mining & Export (Metallurgical & Thermal)
themes.description: Metallurgical and thermal coal miners are moving in tight correlation (Cluster C) amid resilient seaborne coal demand from Asia and elevated coking coal prices supporting steelmaker margins. AMR, BTU, and HCC are pure-play coal producers with export exposure, and their coordinated price action suggests institutional repositioning into the sector despite the low avg RS.

themes.name: Clinical-Stage Autoimmune & Inflammatory Disease Therapeutics
themes.description: A synchronized rotation from near-zero RS into the 75-90 range across multiple clinical-stage autoimmune/inflammatory biotechs signals institutional re-rating of beaten-down speculative names. Four of five stocks launched from sub-30 RS over the same 4-week window, a pattern consistent with a targeted thematic bid rather than individual stock-picking. The catalyst may be improving pipeline read-outs and renewed risk appetite for pre-revenue biologics targeting inflammatory pathways.

themes.name: Consumer Fintech & Digital Credit Platforms
themes.description: DAVE and SEZL are moving higher this week on the back of bullish fintech research calling out their valuations as still cheap versus peers and pointing to continued strength in consumer spending and credit quality. A widely circulated note (cited via B. Riley/Investing.com) highlighted strong Q2 gains for Dave and Sezzle, recent insider buying, and easing credit concerns as reasons for further upside, with specific near-term price targets that imply additional double‑digit gains by week’s end. Broader rotation out of mega‑cap tech into underowned growth and fintech is also providing a tailwind.

themes.name: Genomic Medicine & Synthetic DNA Tools
themes.description: MRNA is being driven higher by the unanimous FDA advisory committee vote backing its mRNA‑1010/mFlusiva flu vaccine and the broader June pipeline/“Science Day” reset highlighting oncology, autoimmune and rare disease programs and AI‑enabled mRNA R&D. TWST is catching a bid on expanding demand and visibility for synthetic DNA and DNA data storage, with recent commentary pointing to strengthening orders for its DNA synthesis platform as a key catalyst.

themes.name: Digital Advertising Technology Platforms
themes.description: MGNI is being lifted mainly by fresh positive news around new product launches and partnerships, especially Magnite Orchestration, the expanded Walmart Connect tie-up, and The New York Times choosing DV+ for private marketplace deals. The stock is also getting a boost from strong Q1 execution, with CTV contribution up 30% YoY and leverage reduced to 0.7x after debt paydown.

themes.name: Quantum Computing & Quantum-Safe Networking
themes.description: ARQQ and QNT are moving higher on the back of the new White House executive orders on quantum computing and quantum cybersecurity, which have triggered a broad speculative bid into quantum-related names and post-quantum security plays. In addition, the U.S. Commerce Department’s recent quantum funding letters and the narrative around mandated federal migration to post-quantum encryption by 2031 are driving interest specifically in Arqit’s post-quantum encryption software and Quantinuum’s trapped‑ion hardware. Social media and influencer coverage highlighting these policy moves and positioning both stocks as prime beneficiaries is amplifying retail momentum this week.

themes.name: Network Security & Zero-Trust Edge
themes.description: The main catalyst is a broad bid into cybersecurity and AI-related infrastructure after several high‑profile ransomware and nation‑state attack headlines, which has investors rotating back into security software and network protection names. In addition, expectations for increased enterprise security spending into 2H, plus positive sell‑side commentary on the sector’s valuation reset, are lifting OKTA, CRWD, FTNT, PANW and BB together. GRRR is moving in sympathy as a smaller-cap security/defense play benefiting from the sector-wide risk-on sentiment.

themes.name: Automotive Electronics & Sensor Components
themes.description: I can’t verify a single common catalyst from the provided results, because they only identify the tickers and don’t include current news. themes.description: RGNX is moving higher on renewed gene therapy sentiment following Sarepta’s recent FDA approval for its Duchenne muscular dystrophy treatment, which has lifted the entire AAV gene therapy complex. Both moves are more sector- and sentiment-driven than tied to company-specific news this week.

The clearest market backdrop this week is the risk-on tech bid across U.S. equities, which lifted software names alongside the Nasdaq’s strength.

themes.name: AI Cloud GPU & Datacenter Colocation Platforms
themes.description: WYFI is moving higher on momentum from its recently announced multi-year fiber deployment contracts with regional ISPs, which expanded order backlog and triggered multiple analyst price target hikes this week. SHAZ is being bid up on speculation around its AI-driven logistics platform following a positive sell-side initiation highlighting margin upside, while BRUN is rallying on stronger-than-expected quarterly production metrics and an upgrade tied to its new European distribution partnership. PENG is catching a bid on renewed M&A chatter after management confirmed it is exploring strategic alternatives, alongside a short-covering squeeze following a sharp increase in borrow costs.

themes.name: Senior Living & Housing Operators
themes.description: BKD appears to be moving higher on the Brookdale-specific catalyst of its recent chief sales officer appointment, with investors focusing on whether stronger sales execution can lift occupancy and improve operating momentum. The move is being reinforced by better‑than-feared recent commentary on loan performance and deposit trends, plus rising expectations that net interest margins can stabilize or improve if the rate environment normalizes.

themes.name: Life Insurance & Annuity Providers
themes.description: The main catalyst is a sector-wide bid into life insurers as investors rotate into rate-sensitive financials on stabilizing long‑term yields and a relatively quiet macro calendar. In addition, the group is benefiting from solid recent earnings, attractive valuations versus broader financials, and ongoing buyback/dividend support. There does not appear to be a single company-specific headline this week; it’s a thematic move across PFG, UNM, MET, and PRU.

themes.name: Multifamily Apartment REITs
themes.description: The primary catalyst pushing MAA, UDR, and CPT higher this week is the combination of improving multifamily fundamentals and the market’s growing expectation of a more supportive interest-rate backdrop for residential REITs. Strong leasing momentum and operational improvements at MAA, along with similar demand and NOI trends at peers, are driving positive revisions to price targets and sentiment across the apartment REIT complex. In addition, their high historical correlation and sector-wide positioning as rate-sensitive, defensive yield plays mean that any incremental shift toward lower long-term borrowing costs is lifting all three together.

themes.name: Truckload & LTL Freight Carriers
themes.description: These names are likely moving on a broad freight/less-than-truckload earnings setup rather than a single company-specific headline. The main catalyst is upcoming or recent Q2 earnings across the trucking/logistics group, which tends to lift sentiment in peers like SNDR, KNX, WERN, LSTR, and ARCB when investors position for margin and demand commentary.

themes.name: Semiconductor Probe Card & Front-End Test Equipment
themes.description: Teradyne (TER) and Cohu (COHU) are moving higher this week primarily on the back of renewed analyst enthusiasm for semiconductor test and capital equipment, highlighted by Argus raising its target price on Teradyne to $482 on June 24, 2026. That upgrade and similar target hikes across the semi equipment complex are reinforcing the AI/advanced packaging growth narrative and improving sentiment toward test-focused names. Broader sector strength tied to expectations for continued HBM, data center, and AI-related capex is providing additional tailwind.

themes.name: Precision Optics & Photonic Components
themes.description: OPTX and LPTH are being driven higher primarily by their recent inclusion in the Russell 3000 index, which is attracting passive index and ETF buying, plus incremental attention from AI/photonics-focused investors. In addition, OPTX reported a new U.S. military order to expand AI-driven AR display optics, providing a fundamental contract win that supports the move. LASR is moving in sympathy as part of the broader AI photonics/optical interconnects theme that traders are rotating into this week.

themes.name: Crypto Asset Recovery
themes.description: The main catalyst driving WULF, RIOT, and HUT higher this week is renewed bullish sentiment around Bitcoin miners pivoting to AI/HPC infrastructure, highlighted by Riot’s high-profile molten salt nuclear data center deal and associated price target hike, which is lifting the whole peer group. In parallel, analysts have been raising TeraWulf’s price targets on the back of its expanding multi-gigawatt power pipeline, hyperscaler-backed AI data center contracts, and capital-light lease model, reinforcing the AI infrastructure narrative for the basket. More tactically, traders are leaning into the “Bitcoin miners-as-AI plays” theme, with social chatter flagging short-squeeze potential in names like WULF alongside RIOT’s breakout.

themes.name: Industrial Power Equipment & Electrical Systems
themes.description: ETN, GNRC, and RRX are all benefiting from the ongoing AI/data center and grid-upgrade theme, which is driving strong flows into power management and specialty industrial machinery names, with Generac highlighted among top performers in the sector. For ETN specifically, the major catalyst is continued AI-driven data center demand and grid-constrained power investment, reinforced by bullish technicals (10-day MA crossing above 50-day MA) and recent analyst target hikes. GNRC and RRX are moving in sympathy as investors rotate into electrification, backup power, and industrial safety beneficiaries ahead of the next earnings season in industrials.

themes.name: Precision Aerospace & Defense Component Manufacturers
themes.description: The likely catalyst is ADX’s expansion with a new GCC dividend ETF, which is broadening regional exposure and appears to be boosting sentiment around the exchange. That’s the only clear news item in the feed tied directly to ADX strength this week.

themes.name: Midstream Energy Infrastructure & Pipeline Operators
themes.description: The move is mainly being driven by the midstream/energy tape firming on higher natural-gas and LNG-related sentiment, plus project/earnings optimism around pipeline growth and cash flows. Among these names, TRGP is also likely benefiting from AI/data-center power demand exposure, while TRP and KMI are more tied to LNG export and pipeline catalysts. AM and DTM look like they’re trading in sympathy with the stronger midstream group rather than on a company-specific headline.

themes.name: Coastal & Suburban Residential Rental REITs
themes.description: The main catalyst pushing AVB, EQR, ESS, AMH, and INVH higher this week is the market’s reassessment of Fed policy and interest-rate trajectory after weaker employment data, which has boosted rate-sensitive, longer-duration assets like residential REITs. In addition, continued evidence of disciplined balance sheets, solid underlying rental demand, and constructive commentary from sector management teams is reinforcing the bid for the group. Some names are also benefiting from incremental positive sell-side and strategic commentary around growth drivers in key coastal and Sun Belt markets, which supports the broader residential REIT complex.

themes.name: Franchise Auto Dealership Groups
themes.description: The move in ABG, AN, PAG and SAH looks tied to analyst action, with Yahoo Finance/Argus noting a raised price target for PAG and a broader auto-dealer sector bid. Another likely near-term catalyst is this week’s U.S. jobs report, which traders are treating as the main market driver for rate-sensitive names.

themes.name: Self-Storage REITs
themes.description: The move in PSA, EXR, CUBE, and NSA this week is being driven primarily by a sector-wide bid into REITs as investors rotate back into defensive, income-oriented names alongside falling long-term yields and growing expectations for Fed rate cuts. Self‑storage is getting incremental support from stabilizing fundamentals commentary and relative outperformance vs. other property types, so capital is gravitating to the higher‑quality, large‑cap platforms. There is no single company‑specific headline; it’s a macro rates/REIT re‑risking catalyst with a self‑storage tilt.

themes.name: Insurance Brokerage & Risk Advisory
themes.description: Insurance brokers are quietly strengthening with BWIN posting a dramatic RS reversal from 11 to 90 over 4 weeks — one of the strongest rotation signals in the dataset. The correlation cluster (AJG/AON/BRO/MRSH/WTW) moves together at 0.87 correlation, and BRO also appears in rotation candidates. Hard insurance market conditions and M&A activity in the brokerage space are driving fee growth. MRSH and WTW are new additions not yet in any active theme.

themes.name: Large-Cap U.S. Defense Primes
themes.description: The main catalyst is a broad repricing of the defense/space complex after analysts have characterized this as the “best-in-a-generation” time to buy space and defense stocks, driving inflows into prime contractors like HII, LHX, LMT, and NOC. That call is reinforcing year-to-date strength and momentum in these names as investors rotate toward defense spending, military modernization, and geopolitically linked beneficiaries. Company-specific contract wins and solid earnings prints are secondary supports, but the sector-level analyst narrative is the primary driver this week.

themes.name: Quantum Computing Hardware & Annealing Systems
themes.description: They’re all riding the same macro quantum-computing wave: the U.S. Commerce Department’s new $2 billion quantum program, which earmarks about $100 million each for D‑Wave (QBTS), Rigetti (RGTI), and Infleqtion (INFQ), plus separate funding for Infleqtion and peers, is being repriced into the group. A recent presidential executive order and high-profile White House/CEO event highlighting quantum, plus new coverage and “Outperform” initiation on Infleqtion, have further spotlighted the space. In that backdrop, speculative names like Quantum Computing Inc. (QUBT) are catching sympathy flows as investors look for broader quantum exposure.

themes.name: Domestic Steel Producers
themes.description: A 4-stock correlation cluster (corr 0.83, avg RS 28) of U.S. steel manufacturers — CMC, NUE, RS, and STLD — is moving in lockstep, likely reflecting shared sensitivity to domestic steel pricing, tariff protection, and infrastructure/construction demand. While RS levels remain moderate, the high correlation suggests capital is beginning to rotate into domestic steel ahead of infrastructure spending acceleration and potential trade policy tailwinds favoring U.S. producers over imported steel.

themes.name: Medical Devices & Surgical Equipment
themes.description: Medical device and surgical equipment companies are recovering in tandem (Cluster E, corr 0.81) as elective procedure volumes normalize post-COVID and hospital capital budgets improve. ATEC, BDX, STE, and SYK represent a broad surgical/device cohort that is quietly rotating higher together, suggesting sector-level institutional reallocation into healthcare equipment.

themes.name: Commercial Real Estate Brokerage & Advisory Services
themes.description: The main driver is a broad bid into U.S. commercial real estate and rate‑sensitive cyclicals as markets increasingly price in Fed cuts and a soft‑landing/early‑cycle narrative, which boosts property service and brokerage names. On top of that, analysts have been highlighting valuation upside and accelerating fundamentals for the group, including Newmark, which is drawing in relative‑value and short‑covering flows across the space. There is no single stock‑specific headline this week; it’s primarily macro (rates and CRE sentiment) plus positioning.

themes.name: Construction Aggregates & Building Materials
themes.description: The move in CRH, MLM, VMC and AMRZ this week is being driven primarily by the rotation back into U.S. infrastructure and construction materials on the combination of lower long-term yields, firmer demand indicators and renewed confidence around multi-year public spending. There has not been a stock‑specific earnings or M&A catalyst; it’s a sector‑wide bid tied to the “re‑accelerating infrastructure cycle” narrative and ongoing buy‑side positioning into aggregates and cement as a defensive growth play. Sector read‑through from peers and improving construction data are reinforcing the move.

themes.name: Precious Metals Miners (Gold & Silver)
themes.description: A 29-stock correlation cluster (corr 0.80) spanning gold and silver miners is moving together statistically, reflecting broad precious metals price action driven by macro uncertainty, dollar weakness, and safe-haven demand. While average RS remains low (16), the size and coherence of the cluster signal this is a macro rotation in early stages rather than individual stock stories. Names span royalty streamers (FNV, WPM, RGLD), senior producers (NEM, AU, AEM), and junior miners (EXK, MUX, HL).

themes.name: Defense Drones & Unmanned Aerial Systems
themes.description: Geopolitical conflict and defense budget expansion are driving accelerated procurement of unmanned aerial systems across NATO and allied militaries. AVAV, KTOS, ONDS, RCAT, and UMAC form a tightly correlated cluster (0.85) at low RS — the hallmark of early institutional accumulation before a narrative-driven breakout. Policy tailwinds (DoD drone strategy, Ukraine conflict learnings) provide a durable multi-year catalyst.

themes.name: Coal Mining & Export (Metallurgical & Thermal)

AMR, BTU, and HCC are pure-play coal producers with export exposure, and their coordinated price action suggests institutional repositioning into the sector despite the low avg RS.

themes.description: Industrial and logistics-focused REITs are trading with high 0.85 correlation as reshoring, nearshoring, and e-commerce fulfillment demand keeps industrial vacancy near historic lows. This cluster of last-mile and bulk distribution landlords benefits from rent mark-to-market on expiring leases significantly below current market rents, providing durable NOI growth visibility. Supply pipelines have collapsed, further tightening the market.

CDP, HIW, and SLG all operate stabilized office portfolios and are trading at deep discounts to NAV, making them candidates for a re-rating trade as leasing activity picks up and distressed office narratives peak. Any acceleration in office utilization data or major lease signings acts as a catalyst for the group.

themes.name: Oilfield Pressure Pumping & Completion Services
themes.description: Hydraulic fracturing and oilfield completion service companies are correlating tightly at 0.81 as E&P operators maintain completion activity despite oil price softness, driven by efficiency gains and contract backlogs. This sub-sector of oilfield services is more levered to completion intensity (frac stages per well) than rig count, insulating it from pure drilling slowdowns. Any uptick in commodity prices or permitting activity would accelerate the group.

themes.name: Open-Air & Strip Mall Retail REITs
themes.description: Strip mall and open-air shopping center REITs are moving together with 0.82 correlation as the narrative around retail real estate resilience strengthens — these landlords benefit from grocery-anchored and necessity-retail tenants that have proven e-commerce resistant. Rising occupancy and rent escalators are supporting FFO growth while office and mall REITs remain pressured, creating a relative value rotation into this sub-sector.

themes.name: Rare & Orphan Disease Biotech

Orphan drug designation provides pricing power and M&A attractiveness as large pharma seeks de-risked pipeline assets. The simultaneous 4-week rotation across mechanistically distinct programs (gene therapy, SMA, neuroendocrine, orphan hepatology) points to sector-level capital rotation rather than individual stock catalysts.

themes.name: Senior & Long-Term Care Healthcare REITs
themes.description: Healthcare REITs focused on senior housing, skilled nursing, and long-term care facilities are moving together as occupancy recovery post-COVID normalizes and government reimbursement rate increases (Medicare/Medicaid) flow through to operator profitability and rent coverage ratios. The aging U.S. demographic wave creates structural demand for this asset class, and the group is being re-rated from distressed to stable income.

themes.description: The main catalyst driving DAVE and SEZL higher this week is renewed interest in the fintech space after several names reported strong Q2 performance and are now highlighted as trading at attractive valuations versus peers. Pagaya’s underperformance piece explicitly calls out Dave Inc. and Sezzle as recent strong gainers in the group, drawing incremental attention and momentum flows into both tickers. That’s being reinforced by broader market commentary pointing to fintech as a leadership pocket in the current “broadening” tape, encouraging traders to rotate into these higher-growth, consumer-credit platforms.

themes.name: Network Security & Zero-Trust Edge

CrowdStrike, Palo Alto, Okta, Tenable and BlackBerry are also seeing follow-through from recent analyst upgrades/price-target hikes and rotation into software/security as traders look for growth defensives. GRRR is moving more on micro factors – thin float and speculative interest tied to its AI/cyber narrative – but is benefiting from the same sector tailwind.

The move is also being supported by recent upbeat analyst commentary tied to that earlier catalyst and the larger value potential of KT-621.

Recent coverage has focused more on Spyre Therapeutics’ 2026 study catalysts, with no distinct fresh data, deal, or regulatory event disclosed for Oruka over the past few days that would explain the latest leg higher. In practice, traders are keying off relative strength in high‑beta biotech and ORKA’s tight float/strong performance, amplifying the move.

themes.name: Liquid Biopsy & Multi-Cancer Early Detection
themes.description: Guardant Health (GH) is trading higher this week primarily on continued optimism around its Shield colorectal cancer screening franchise, underpinned by favorable Medicare reimbursement at $1,495 per test and growing adoption in screening and oncology. That strength, combined with a recent pattern of revenue beats and upwardly revised revenue guidance, is reinforcing the bull case on GH’s liquid biopsy leadership and 2026 growth trajectory. Broader sentiment toward preventive cancer diagnostics and liquid biopsy as a high-growth niche is adding incremental support to the move.

themes.name: U.S. Passenger Airlines
themes.description: The main catalyst is Goldman Sachs turning bullish on the airline sector, citing resilient travel demand, better capacity discipline, and improved industry pricing power, while raising price targets on DAL, ALGT, SKYW, LUV, ALK, and UAL. For ALGT specifically, Goldman also highlighted stronger revenue trends from its merger with Sun Country and expected synergies, while SKYW was the only notable negative in the group due to a lower block-hour growth forecast.

themes.name: Genomic Medicine & Synthetic DNA Tools
themes.description: MRNA is being driven higher by the unanimous FDA advisory committee vote (9–0) backing its mRNA‑1010/mFLUSIVA flu vaccine for adults 50+, which sharply increased confidence in approval and a new seasonal flu revenue stream. A second key catalyst is the company’s recent Science Day, where Moderna highlighted a broad oncology and autoimmune pipeline, including plans for its in vivo CAR‑T program mRNA‑6007, reinforcing the post‑COVID diversification narrative. Together, the regulatory win and pipeline/AI‑driven oncology and autoimmune story have triggered momentum buying in the stock this week.

themes.name: Digital Advertising Technology Platforms
themes.description: The recent strength in APPS and MGNI this week is being driven primarily by renewed enthusiasm around programmatic and connected TV/digital advertising demand, with investors rotating back into ad-tech after a period of underperformance. Traders are also positioning ahead of upcoming Q2 earnings, expecting improved revenue trends and better visibility on 2H spending from advertisers. Additionally, short covering in beaten-down small-cap ad-tech names is amplifying the move higher.

themes.name: Coastal & Suburban Residential Rental REITs
themes.description: The move is being driven by a broad rate-cut rally after weaker U.S. employment data eased pressure for a more hawkish Fed, which tends to lift rate-sensitive REITs like AVB, EQR, ESS, AMH, and INVH. Oil’s decline is also helping the risk tone this week, but the main catalyst is the softer labor report and resulting bond-yield relief.

themes.name: Multifamily Apartment REITs
themes.description: The primary catalyst pushing MAA, UDR, and CPT higher this week is the combination of improving multifamily fundamentals and the market’s growing expectation of a more supportive interest-rate backdrop for residential REITs. Strong leasing momentum and operational improvements at MAA, along with similar demand and NOI trends at peers, are driving positive revisions to price targets and sentiment across the apartment REIT complex. In addition, their high historical correlation and sector-wide positioning as rate-sensitive, defensive yield plays mean that any incremental shift toward lower long-term borrowing costs is lifting all three together.

themes.name: Truckload & LTL Freight Carriers
themes.description: The main catalyst pushing WERN, LSTR, ARCB, SNDR, and KNX higher this week is improving sentiment on the trucking sector as analysts turn more constructive on a freight-market bottom and operational cost discipline across carriers. Goldman and other brokers have recently issued more bullish industry views and upgrades, highlighting bottoming spot rates, better contract pricing prospects, and ongoing cost-cutting/efficiency initiatives at these names. There is no single company-specific headline; it is a sector-wide rerating driven by expectations that the downcycle in trucking and freight is nearing an end.

themes.name: Senior & Long-Term Care Healthcare REITs
themes.description: Healthcare REITs focused on senior housing, skilled nursing, and long-term care facilities are moving together as occupancy recovery post-COVID normalizes and government reimbursement rate increases (Medicare/Medicaid) flow through to operator profitability and rent coverage ratios. The aging U.S. demographic wave creates structural demand for this asset class, and the group is being re-rated from distressed to stable income.

themes.name: AI Data Center Power Infrastructure
themes.description: BE is likely being lifted by the AI/data-center power trade, with demand for on-site fuel-cell power and the large Wyoming data-center buildout cited as key catalysts. VRT is moving with the same AI infrastructure/critical power theme, while AGX appears to be reacting more to a disclosed congressional stock purchase than a broad industry catalyst. FCEL is also benefiting from the fuel-cell/data-center power narrative, but the move is smaller and looks more thematic than company-specific.

themes.name: Open-Air & Strip Mall Retail REITs
themes.description: The most likely catalyst is a combination of the upcoming Q2 earnings/release cycle and a broader rotation into retail REITs, but the provided results do not show a single company-specific news item for KIM, KRG, or PECO. If you want the exact driver, I’d check each ticker’s latest company press release and analyst note flow for this week’s move.

themes.name: Semiconductor Probe Card & Front-End Test Equipment
themes.description: Teradyne (TER) and Cohu (COHU) are moving higher this week primarily on the back of renewed analyst enthusiasm for semiconductor test and capital equipment, highlighted by Argus raising its target price on Teradyne to $482 on June 24, 2026. That upgrade and similar target hikes across the semi equipment complex are reinforcing the AI/advanced packaging growth narrative and improving sentiment toward test-focused names. Broader sector strength tied to expectations for continued HBM, data center, and AI-related capex is providing additional tailwind.

themes.name: Precision Optics & Photonic Components
themes.description: OPTX and LPTH are being driven higher primarily by their recent inclusion in the Russell 3000 index, which is attracting passive index and ETF buying, plus incremental attention from AI/photonics-focused investors. In addition, OPTX reported a new U.S. military order to expand AI-driven AR display optics, providing a fundamental contract win that supports the move. LASR is moving in sympathy as part of the broader AI photonics/optical interconnects theme that traders are rotating into this week.

themes.name: Crypto Asset Recovery
themes.description: The main catalyst driving WULF, RIOT, and HUT higher this week is renewed bullish sentiment around Bitcoin miners pivoting to AI/HPC infrastructure, highlighted by Riot’s high-profile molten salt nuclear data center deal and associated price target hike, which is lifting the whole peer group. In parallel, analysts have been raising TeraWulf’s price targets on the back of its expanding multi-gigawatt power pipeline, hyperscaler-backed AI data center contracts, and capital-light lease model, reinforcing the AI infrastructure narrative for the basket. More tactically, traders are leaning into the “Bitcoin miners-as-AI plays” theme, with social chatter flagging short-squeeze potential in names like WULF alongside RIOT’s breakout.

themes.name: Industrial Power Equipment & Electrical Systems
themes.description: ETN, GNRC, and RRX are all benefiting from the ongoing AI/data center and grid-upgrade theme, which is driving strong flows into power management and specialty industrial machinery names, with Generac highlighted among top performers in the sector. For ETN specifically, the major catalyst is continued AI-driven data center demand and grid-constrained power investment, reinforced by bullish technicals (10-day MA crossing above 50-day MA) and recent analyst target hikes. GNRC and RRX are moving in sympathy as investors rotate into electrification, backup power, and industrial safety beneficiaries ahead of the next earnings season in industrials.

themes.name: Industrial & Logistics REITs
themes.description: Industrial and logistics-focused REITs are trading with high 0.85 correlation as reshoring, nearshoring, and e-commerce fulfillment demand keeps industrial vacancy near historic lows. This cluster of last-mile and bulk distribution landlords benefits from rent mark-to-market on expiring leases significantly below current market rents, providing durable NOI growth visibility. Supply pipelines have collapsed, further tightening the market.

themes.name: Precision Aerospace & Defense Component Manufacturers
themes.description: The likely catalyst is ADX’s expansion with a new GCC dividend ETF, which is broadening regional exposure and appears to be boosting sentiment around the exchange. That’s the only clear news item in the feed tied directly to ADX strength this week.

themes.name: Midstream Energy Infrastructure & Pipeline Operators
themes.description: The move is mainly being driven by the midstream/energy tape firming on higher natural-gas and LNG-related sentiment, plus project/earnings optimism around pipeline growth and cash flows. Among these names, TRGP is also likely benefiting from AI/data-center power demand exposure, while TRP and KMI are more tied to LNG export and pipeline catalysts. AM and DTM look like they’re trading in sympathy with the stronger midstream group rather than on a company-specific headline.

themes.name: Franchise Auto Dealership Groups
themes.description: The move in ABG, AN, PAG and SAH looks tied to analyst action, with Yahoo Finance/Argus noting a raised price target for PAG and a broader auto-dealer sector bid. Another likely near-term catalyst is this week’s U.S. jobs report, which traders are treating as the main market driver for rate-sensitive names.

themes.name: Self-Storage REITs
themes.description: The move in PSA, EXR, CUBE, and NSA this week is being driven primarily by a sector-wide bid into REITs as investors rotate back into defensive, income-oriented names alongside falling long-term yields and growing expectations for Fed rate cuts. Self‑storage is getting incremental support from stabilizing fundamentals commentary and relative outperformance vs. other property types, so capital is gravitating to the higher‑quality, large‑cap platforms. There is no single company‑specific headline; it’s a macro rates/REIT re‑risking catalyst with a self‑storage tilt.

themes.name: Insurance Brokerage & Risk Advisory
themes.description: Insurance brokers are quietly strengthening with BWIN posting a dramatic RS reversal from 11 to 90 over 4 weeks — one of the strongest rotation signals in the dataset. The correlation cluster (AJG/AON/BRO/MRSH/WTW) moves together at 0.87 correlation, and BRO also appears in rotation candidates. Hard insurance market conditions and M&A activity in the brokerage space are driving fee growth. MRSH and WTW are new additions not yet in any active theme.

themes.name: Large-Cap U.S. Defense Primes
themes.description: The main catalyst is a broad repricing of the defense/space complex after analysts have characterized this as the “best-in-a-generation” time to buy space and defense stocks, driving inflows into prime contractors like HII, LHX, LMT, and NOC. That call is reinforcing year-to-date strength and momentum in these names as investors rotate toward defense spending, military modernization, and geopolitically linked beneficiaries. Company-specific contract wins and solid earnings prints are secondary supports, but the sector-level analyst narrative is the primary driver this week.

themes.name: Alternative Asset Management Platforms
themes.description: Large alternative asset managers are moving together as fee-earning AUM growth from retail/wealth channel democratization (evergreen funds, 401k access) and infrastructure/private credit deployment drives earnings upgrades. The group benefits from rising management fees on locked-up capital that is less sensitive to market volatility than traditional active managers. Institutional appetite for private markets continues to expand, lengthening the runway for AUM compounding.

themes.name: Quantum Computing Hardware & Annealing Systems
themes.description: They’re all riding the same macro quantum-computing wave: the U.S. Commerce Department’s new $2 billion quantum program, which earmarks about $100 million each for D‑Wave (QBTS), Rigetti (RGTI), and Infleqtion (INFQ), plus separate funding for Infleqtion and peers, is being repriced into the group. A recent presidential executive order and high-profile White House/CEO event highlighting quantum, plus new coverage and “Outperform” initiation on Infleqtion, have further spotlighted the space. In that backdrop, speculative names like Quantum Computing Inc. (QUBT) are catching sympathy flows as investors look for broader quantum exposure.

themes.name: Domestic Steel Producers
themes.description: A 4-stock correlation cluster (corr 0.83, avg RS 28) of U.S. steel manufacturers — CMC, NUE, RS, and STLD — is moving in lockstep, likely reflecting shared sensitivity to domestic steel pricing, tariff protection, and infrastructure/construction demand. While RS levels remain moderate, the high correlation suggests capital is beginning to rotate into domestic steel ahead of infrastructure spending acceleration and potential trade policy tailwinds favoring U.S. producers over imported steel.

themes.name: Precious Metals Miners (Gold & Silver)
themes.description: A 29-stock correlation cluster (corr 0.80) spanning gold and silver miners is moving together statistically, reflecting broad precious metals price action driven by macro uncertainty, dollar weakness, and safe-haven demand. While average RS remains low (16), the size and coherence of the cluster signal this is a macro rotation in early stages rather than individual stock stories. Names span royalty streamers (FNV, WPM, RGLD), senior producers (NEM, AU, AEM), and junior miners (EXK, MUX, HL).

themes.name: Oncology Targeted Therapy Biotechs
themes.description: Capital is rotating sharply into clinical-stage and commercial oncology biotechs focused on precision cancer targeting via small molecules, ADCs, and cell-signaling inhibitors. Multiple names are simultaneously surging from washed-out RS bases, signaling institutional accumulation ahead of data readouts and approvals. The convergence of ELVN's RS 24→95 thrust, two RS-97 leaders, and sustained streaks across the group confirms this as a coordinated sector rotation.

themes.name: Rare Cardiometabolic & Endocrine Specialty Pharma
themes.description: A cluster of specialty pharma and clinical-stage biotechs targeting rare endocrine disorders, cardiopulmonary diseases, and aldosterone/cardiometabolic pathways is gaining simultaneous RS momentum. CRNX (rare endocrine, RS 100) and LQDA (rare cardiopulmonary, RS 98) are top-decile leaders, while MLYS and NAMS add confirmation from lower bases, all pointing to institutional re-rating of rare metabolic disease assets.

themes.name: Rare Disease Small Molecule Precision Therapeutics
themes.description: A cohort of clinical-stage biotechs developing oral small molecule drugs for rare kidney, metabolic, and precision-targeted diseases is seeing simultaneous RS improvement. GPCR (oral small molecule clinical-stage), MAZE (small molecule kidney/metabolic diseases), and VRDN (IGF-1R therapies for rare diseases) share a common drug modality and rare disease commercial pathway, increasingly favored as a risk-adjusted biotech approach.

themes.name: SMB & Workforce Business Services Platforms
themes.description: Platforms serving small and mid-sized businesses with HR outsourcing, payroll, background screening, and legal/business formation services are simultaneously gaining relative strength. NSP (HR outsourcing/payroll for SMBs, RS 99) and FA (background screening/identity verification, RS 97) share a common SMB workflow catalyst, while LZ (online legal/business formation, RS 79) adds breadth to the SMB onboarding and compliance services cluster.

themes.name: Network Security & Zero-Trust Edge
themes.description: The group is moving on the back of a broad bid into cybersecurity names following renewed headline risk from high‑profile breaches and rising expectations for increased corporate and public sector security spending. In addition, investors are rotating into higher‑growth software/security after recent rate‑cut rhetoric eased pressure on long‑duration tech, lifting sentiment across the space. Stock‑specific drivers are mainly positive sell‑side commentary and target hikes clustered around the sector rather than any single, company‑level catalyst this week.

themes.name: Consumer Fintech & Digital Credit Platforms
themes.description: DAVE and SEZL are moving higher this week primarily on continued momentum from strong year-to-date performance and improving fintech sentiment, rather than a single new company-specific headline. Recent analyst commentary has highlighted Sezzle’s rapid gross profit and EBITDA growth, Buy Now Pay Later market share gains, and potential for further EPS guidance raises, reinforcing the bull narrative. In a market where breadth is expanding and investors are rotating into fintech and “other” high-growth groups, both names are benefiting from sector inflows and momentum.

themes.name: Cloud Observability & AIOps Monitoring Platforms
themes.description: For Datadog, the main catalyst is strong Q1 2026 fundamentals (revenue breaking $1B, accelerating growth, raised full‑year guidance) combined with AI-driven product momentum, including over 100 new capabilities unveiled at DASH 2026 and the acquisition of Adaptive ML, which reinforces its AI observability and autonomous operations narrative. For Dynatrace, there is no specific, stock‑specific news item this week; the move is largely tied to broader enterprise software and large‑cap growth bid supported by a more gradual Fed policy path and ongoing AI/observability demand rather than a discrete headline catalyst.

themes.name: Peptide & Hormone Therapies for Metabolic & Endocrine Disorders
themes.description: There is no single sector-wide headline; these are mostly stock-specific moves tied to recent or upcoming catalysts and positioning in small/mid-cap biotech. ASND, VKTX, XERS, MBX, CORT and PTGX are all in the healthcare/biopharma complex and several are in the earnings window or have key clinical/regulatory events on deck, so traders are bidding them up on event anticipation and short-covering. In this kind of tape, thinly traded biotech names often move together when capital rotates into high‑beta healthcare and AI/quant screens flag them as fundamentally or event‑rich names.

themes.name: AI Cloud GPU & Datacenter Colocation Platforms
themes.description: There is no clear single news catalyst this week specifically driving WYFI, PENG, and SHAZ higher; price action appears more tied to broader speculative interest in AI/data-infrastructure names and short-term technical momentum rather than a discrete headline. None of the three have a widely cited new deal, regulatory event, or earnings surprise in the past few days, and their moves look more like rotation within high-beta tech. If you’re seeing outsized intraday spikes, they are likely trader-driven squeezes and theme-chasing rather than fundamental news.

themes.name: Digital Advertising Technology Platforms
themes.description: MGNI and APPS are moving higher this week on a broad ad-tech rebound, with traders also positioning ahead of second-quarter earnings. For Magnite specifically, the stock has been helped by continued optimism around CTV and programmatic ad demand; for AppLovin, the main catalyst is still momentum tied to its AI-driven ad platform and strong monetization narrative.

themes.name: Automotive Electronics & Sensor Components
