I don’t see a fresh catalyst in the provided results for OUST or AEVA, so their strength is likely part of a broader small-cap/AI-autonomy momentum trade rather than new headline news.

themes.name: Digital Insurance Distribution & Marketplace Platforms
themes.description: Goosehead Insurance (GSHD) is moving higher on anticipation ahead of its upcoming earnings and continued momentum in the insurance brokerage space, with investors rotating into fee-based, less rate-sensitive financials. Porch Group (PRCH) is rallying on improving sentiment toward small-cap tech and housing-adjacent names as the market prices in a firmer housing backdrop and potential operating leverage in its platform model. No single discrete headline is driving either; it’s mainly positioning around earnings expectations and sector momentum.

themes.name: Targeted Protein Degradation Oncology
themes.description: KYMR is being driven higher by renewed focus on its KT-621 oral STAT6 degrader program for atopic dermatitis and inflammatory diseases, with investors positioning ahead of key Phase 1 data and a “defining” 2H 2026 catalyst window highlighted in recent analysis. GLUE is rallying primarily on the back of its large, up-to-$5.7 billion molecular glue licensing deal with Novartis, which includes $150 million upfront and has reframed the valuation of the platform. Both names are also benefiting from a broader re-rating of molecular glue degraders as a high-value drug development theme.

themes.name: Inflammatory Disease & Immunology Biologics
themes.description: ORKA is higher this week on a positive company-specific catalyst, but the provided results do not identify the exact headline behind the move. The stock was already up about 31.9% over the prior 30 days, suggesting momentum around a recent event or update, though the specific trigger isn’t visible in these results.

themes.name: Genomic Medicine & Synthetic DNA Tools
themes.description: MRNA is being driven higher by the unanimous FDA advisory panel endorsement of its mRNA-1010 flu vaccine, which sharply reduced regulatory risk ahead of the August 5 PDUFA decision and positioned flu as a second major commercial pillar beyond COVID. Additional support is coming from Science Day updates highlighting a broader mRNA pipeline and platform strategy, plus rising analyst and media focus on non-COVID assets and pipeline diversification. TWST’s move is being fueled by renewed interest in synthetic biology and genomics tools as capital rotates back into higher-growth life sciences names, with investors bidding up platform companies leveraged to pharma and biotech R&D spend.

themes.name: Liquid Biopsy & Multi-Cancer Early Detection
themes.description: The primary catalyst for GRAL (GRAIL, Inc.) moving higher this week is read-through enthusiasm from Guardant Health’s strong oncology and screening momentum, particularly around blood-based cancer screening platforms and valuation re-rating in the space. As investors focus on liquid biopsy and early-detection leaders, GRAIL is benefiting from sector-wide rerating tied to growth expectations and increasing confidence in commercial adoption of multi-cancer and colorectal screening tests.

themes.name: SMB & Workforce Business Services Platforms
themes.description: The move in LZ this week is most likely being driven by a broader small-cap/quality-factor bid rather than a company-specific headline, based on the available data. I don’t see a clear news catalyst in the results provided, so the rally may be technical or factor-driven.

themes.name: U.S. Petroleum Refining & Downstream Processing
themes.description: Refiners are catching a bid this week mainly on tightening product markets: strong summer gasoline and jet demand, firmer refining margins, and improving crack spreads, all supportive of earnings power into Q3. There’s also a broader rotation back into energy as investors look for cyclical value exposure amid lingering concerns about inflation and geopolitics. No single company-specific headline is driving it; it’s a sector move tied to macro demand, margins, and positioning.

themes.name: Casual Dining Restaurant Turnaround
themes.description: The primary catalyst driving CBRL higher this week is anticipation around upcoming corporate updates and earnings season dynamics, with investors rotating into discretionary and restaurant names viewed as potential beneficiaries of a “K-shaped” consumer recovery narrative. There is also some positioning ahead of company-specific events like business update calls and broader sector earnings that can re-rate restaurant chains on guidance and traffic trends. No single headline appears to be responsible; it is more a combination of earnings-season sentiment and positioning in beaten-down consumer names.

themes.name: Global Digital Payments & Cross-Border Fintech
themes.description: The move in WSE FOUR PAYP this week is being driven primarily by positive sentiment and re-rating around PayPal’s Q1 2026 results, which showed solid TPV and revenue growth with EPS modestly ahead of guidance and reiterated full‑year outlook. Investors are leaning into the cost-savings and restructuring story, with focus on AI-driven efficiency and margin improvement in 2H 2026 as a medium-term catalyst. Broader rotation into financials and payment names ahead of earnings season is adding an incremental tailwind.

themes.name: Specialty Catastrophe Property Insurance Underwriters
themes.description: SLDE NP is moving higher on optimism around Neptune Insurance’s execution and fundamentals, with investors viewing prior concerns as largely priced in and sentiment improving. The stock is also benefiting from the recent broker action that, despite a rating downgrade to Market Perform, raised the price target to $34, reinforcing a higher valuation baseline. Broader insurance sector strength and risk-on sentiment this week are adding incremental support to the move.

themes.name: Office REIT Recovery & Re-Rating
themes.description: SLG, CDP and HIW are all office-focused REITs, and the main catalyst pushing them higher this week is renewed interest in the beaten-down office REIT space as investors position ahead of upcoming inflation/Fed commentary and potential rate-cut expectations, which directly support cap rates and valuations. There is also some short-covering and mean-reversion in higher-quality office names after a prolonged period of underperformance, with buying focused in names perceived to have more resilient tenant bases and balance sheets. No stock-specific headline has dominated; it’s primarily a sector-wide macro and rate-driven move.

themes.name: Specialty Insurance Underwriting & Brokerage
themes.description: The move in the insurance brokers/MGAs this week is being driven mainly by a sector-wide bid on expectations for strong Q2 earnings and continued pricing strength in commercial P&C, rather than a single stock-specific headline for WTW, KNSL, RYAN, or PLMR. Investors are leaning into names with visible organic growth, margin leverage from higher rates, and resilient demand for specialty and excess lines. There is also some rotation back into defensive financials after recent volatility in rate-cut expectations, which is benefiting the group.

themes.name: Golf Equipment & Apparel
themes.description: The main catalyst this week for Callaway Golf (CALY) is a wave of bullish analyst activity, including target price hikes toward the 19 area and positive relative performance commentary versus other consumer discretionary names. Shares have broken to new 52-week highs on momentum as investors chase year-to-date outperformance of roughly 50–55%, rather than reacting to any specific company news or fundamental headline. In short, it’s an analyst- and momentum-driven move, not a discrete news event.

themes.name: Insurance Brokerage & Distribution Services
themes.description: AON, AJG, and BRO are higher mainly on a sector rerating: investors are rotating into insurance brokers after mixed-to-strong brokerage commentary and relative-strength buying in the group. The best read from the available items is that the move is not one single company-specific headline, but broader optimism around margins, pricing power, and defensive characteristics in the insurance-broker space.

themes.name: Custom AI Silicon & Chip Architecture Licensing
themes.description: The group is moving on the back of renewed enthusiasm around AI infrastructure and edge compute, with investors rotating into mixed-signal, connectivity, and IP names levered to accelerating data center and networking spend. Specifically, positive sell-side commentary on AI-related custom silicon, stronger-than-expected demand indications for high-speed connectivity/optical interconnect, and expectations for incremental ARM-based and RISC-V design wins are lifting CEVA, ALAB, ARM, MXL, MRVL, and CRDO. There is no single stock-specific headline; the catalyst is a sector-wide AI semiconductor and communications upcycle narrative.

themes.name: U.S. Passenger Airlines
themes.description: The main catalyst is Goldman Sachs turning broadly bullish on the U.S. airline sector, raising net profit forecasts for Q3/Q4 2026 and hiking price targets for United, Allegiant, Delta, SkyWest and others on the back of resilient demand and improved industry pricing and capacity discipline. Additional support comes from lower fuel prices after Middle East risk premium faded, which improves margin outlook versus prior expectations. The move is sector-wide rather than company-specific, so these names are rising on upgraded earnings/price-target revisions and a stronger macro backdrop for airlines.

themes.name: Franchise Auto Dealership Groups
themes.description: ABG, PAG and SAH are all moving higher on a combination of sector-wide auto retail strength and expectations for resilient U.S. consumer demand, rather than a single, stock-specific headline this week. The group is also benefiting from the broader rotation into cyclical/value names as investors position for a soft-landing scenario and potentially lower rates into year-end. No major company-specific earnings, M&A, or guidance revisions have hit the tape for these three names in the past few days, so the move appears mostly macro and sector driven.

themes.name: Insurance Brokerage & Risk Advisory
themes.description: BWIN (The Baldwin Insurance Group) and MRSH (Marsh & McLennan) are both moving higher this week largely on sector-wide strength in insurance brokers rather than a specific, stock‑specific headline catalyst. The group is benefiting from a supportive backdrop of resilient commercial insurance pricing and steady demand, which is flowing through to brokers’ revenue and margin expectations. Any near-term move is being driven more by positioning into the space ahead of upcoming earnings than by discrete company news.

themes.name: Property & Casualty Insurance Underwriters
themes.description: The move is primarily part of a broad bid into P&C insurers as investors rotate into defensive, rate‑sensitive financials on the back of stable-to-lower long‑term yields and benign catastrophe activity so far in the current storm season. There has also been ongoing support from the soft landing narrative for the US economy, which favors carriers with strong commercial and specialty exposure like TRV, CINF, WRB and ORI. No single stock‑specific headline is driving the group; it’s mostly sector‑level macro positioning and relief that loss trends remain manageable.

themes.name: B2B Digital Financial Infrastructure & Payment Rails Modernization
themes.description: PAY is likely moving on sector-wide enthusiasm ahead of Q2 fintech earnings and ongoing optimism that digital payments volumes remain resilient despite higher-for-longer rates. QTWO is grinding higher on expectations for solid Q2 SaaS results and continued digital banking adoption, with investors positioning ahead of earnings rather than reacting to a single stock-specific headline. ALKT’s strength is being driven by Jana Partners’ activist campaign pushing the company to explore a sale and governance changes, which has sharpened focus on takeover optionality and re-rated the stock.

themes.name: Truckload & LTL Freight Carriers
themes.description: The main catalyst this week is a bullish shift in Street sentiment on U.S. trucking, with at least one major broker turning positive on the group and highlighting an approaching cyclical bottom and improving volume/pricing setup for truckload and LTL carriers. That constructive industry call, combined with positioning after a prolonged freight downturn, is driving broad sector buying across names like WERN, LSTR, TFII, ARCB, SNDR, and KNX. No company-specific breaking news is the primary driver; it’s a sector re-rating trade tied to the trucking cycle.

themes.name: Semiconductor Wafer Foundry & Advanced IC Manufacturing
themes.description: The group is trading higher on a broad semiconductor equipment rebound, with investors rotating into names exposed to foundry and memory capex after recent optimism around a healthier 2026 spending cycle and AI-driven wafer-fab demand. MKSI, ACMR, UMC, KLAC, and AMAT are especially sensitive to this theme; PDF Solutions is also benefiting indirectly from stronger chip-production activity and equipment spending. The move appears to be sector-driven rather than tied to one single company-specific headline.

themes.name: Life Insurance & Annuity Providers
themes.description: The main catalyst is a sector-wide rerating of life insurers on expectations that higher-for-longer interest rates and tight credit spreads will continue to support investment income and reserve economics, driving better return-on-equity profiles for PFG, PRU, UNM, and MET. In addition, investors are positioning ahead of upcoming Q2 earnings with a bias that asset-sensitive financials will show resilient spread income and strong capital return (buybacks/dividends), attracting rotation out of more fully priced growth and AI names. There is no single company-specific headline; it’s a macro/sector trade around rates, earnings, and capital return.

themes.name: European Bank Stocks
themes.description: The move is largely driven by renewed expectations for lower ECB and BoE rates later this year, which boost the outlook for European and UK rate‑sensitive banks like Santander, ING, BBVA, NatWest, Lloyds and Barclays. In addition, better‑than‑feared recent economic data in the eurozone and UK and a stabilization in credit spreads are supporting the sector. Some flow is also rotation into financials ahead of the next earnings season as investors position for still‑solid net interest income and potential capital return updates.

themes.name: Private Mortgage Insurance
themes.description: The move is most likely being driven by a sector re-rating in mortgage insurers, with broker price-target updates lifting sentiment across MTG and NMIH and spilling over to ESNT and RDN. Yahoo Finance’s specialty insurance snapshot shows higher targets for MTG and NMIH, which is consistent with the group trading higher on valuation-positive news rather than a company-specific catalyst.

themes.name: AI Memory & Storage
themes.description: The move is being driven mainly by a storage-sector rebound on AI data-center demand and a broader rotation into cyclical semis, with investors focusing on stronger NAND/HBM demand and improved profitability outlooks for memory names. A specific near-term catalyst cited this week is the upbeat read-through from sector commentary and analyst revisions on NAND pricing/profitability, rather than a single company-specific headline.

themes.name: Senior & Long-Term Care Healthcare REITs
themes.description: The move across senior housing/healthcare REITs this week is being driven mainly by a sector-wide bid on the “defensive yield + aging demographics” theme as rates drift lower and investors rotate back into higher-dividend, non-cyclical real estate. There hasn’t been a stock-specific headline for SBRA, VTR, OHI, NHI, or LTC; instead, the catalyst is broader REIT factor buying and short-covering in healthcare names ahead of the next earnings cycle. In other words, it’s a macro/rates and positioning story, not a company-level news event.

themes.name: U.S. Regional & Mid-Cap Commercial Banks
themes.description: The move is being driven less by bank-specific news and more by a broad regional-bank rally, with investors rotating into financials on the view that the group can benefit from a steadier rate outlook and stronger earnings leverage. Among the names you listed, the most specific near-term catalyst is FITB’s upgrade to Buy and related positive analyst commentary, which is helping sentiment across the peer group.

themes.name: Coastal & Suburban Residential Rental REITs
themes.description: The move is being driven primarily by a macro REIT/housing trade: falling long-term yields and growing expectations of Fed easing are supporting interest-rate-sensitive residential REITs, while housing fundamentals remain solid. Additionally, a widely circulated piece this week arguing the U.S. housing shortage may be peaking but not reversing quickly has refocused investors on institutional single-family and multifamily landlords as structural beneficiaries of constrained affordability and rental demand. There does not appear to be a stock-specific catalyst unique to INVH, EQR, AMH, AVB, or ESS beyond this broader factor rotation into residential REITs.

themes.name: Industrial & Logistics REITs
themes.description: EGP, FR and TRNO are all trading higher this week primarily on continued strength and fund inflows into the industrial REIT space, as investors rotate back into high-quality logistics and infill industrial names leveraged to still-tight warehouse markets and potential rate-cut tailwinds. TRNO in particular is benefiting from bullish analyst positioning and a constructive earnings/FFO growth outlook after recent commentary and estimates that highlight above-average rent spreads and balance sheet capacity for accretive acquisitions. There has been no single stock-specific headline this week; the move is largely sector- and macro-driven re‑rating.

themes.name: Government-Funded Senior & Post-Acute Care Re-Rating
themes.description: ALHC (Alignment Healthcare) and AVAH (Aveanna Healthcare) do not appear to have a specific company‑level news catalyst this week; the move looks tied to broader strength in value and small/mid‑cap healthcare services names as investors rotate into defensive, managed-care and home health exposure. Both benefit from the current bid into Medicare/Medicaid and home‑care reimbursement plays as rates stabilize and election‑related policy risk is being repriced more favorably. The rally is more sector‑flow driven than headline‑driven.

themes.name: Bitcoin & Crypto Mining Infrastructure
themes.description: CIFR, CORZ, HUT, IREN, and WULF are all pure-play Bitcoin mining operators showing high beta-adjusted correlation, reflecting their shared exposure to Bitcoin price, mining difficulty, and hash rate economics. While current RS is depressed, the cluster correlation at 0.82 signals coordinated institutional positioning that could accelerate on any Bitcoin catalyst.

themes.name: Multifamily Apartment REITs
themes.description: The move is being driven mainly by a broad bid into multifamily/apartment REITs as investors price in a turning point in the apartment rental cycle, with expectations of moderating new supply and improving rent growth in key Sunbelt and coastal markets. Recent commentary from names like Camden Property Trust highlighting 4–5% expected rent growth in 2026–2027 as Sunbelt supply declines has reinforced the bullish narrative for the whole group. There is no single stock-specific headline this week; it is a sector-wide re‑rating on the improving fundamental outlook.

themes.name: Satellite Communications & Space Data Services
themes.description: VSAT, SPIR, and MDA are being driven higher this week by a broad space sector risk-on move tied to major catalysts: Rocket Lab’s acquisition of Iridium, the surge in space-focused ETFs like Tema Space Innovators (NASA), and heightened investor focus on the upcoming SpaceX listing as a valuation and liquidity benchmark for the sector. These names are key components or peers in the “new space” infrastructure and services theme, so they are catching flows and momentum as capital rotates into space stocks.

themes.name: Open-Air & Strip Mall Retail REITs
themes.description: The main catalyst is renewed interest in retail REITs as a group on the back of stabilizing interest-rate expectations and improving brick-and-mortar traffic data, driving sector-wide inflows into names like KRG, PECO, SKT, KIM, and UE. There is also positioning ahead of the coming Q2 earnings season, with investors rotating into higher-quality, open-air and outlet-focused retail REITs perceived as beneficiaries of resilient consumer spending. No stock-specific headline is driving the move; it is primarily a sector and factor rotation trade.

themes.name: Quantum Computing & Quantum-Safe Networking
themes.description: QNT and ARQQ are moving higher this week on the back of a broad quantum-computing sector re‑rating following Quantinuum’s recent Nasdaq listing and growing media focus on “next wave” quantum names, which has pulled in speculative and thematic ETF money into the group. In particular, QNT is benefiting from its high-profile IPO and leadership narrative in logical qubits, while ARQQ is catching sympathy flows as investors look for liquid quantum encryption exposure within the same theme. Macro catalysts like the upcoming Fed meeting and ongoing AI/infrastructure hype are adding a supportive risk-on backdrop for high-beta tech, amplifying those sector-specific moves.

themes.name: Senior Living & Housing Operators
themes.description: The main catalyst for BKD this week is follow-through interest after Brookdale’s early June financing announcement extending 2027 mortgage maturities and expanding its credit facility, which eased near‑term balance sheet and refinancing risk. In the background, investors remain focused on continued occupancy improvement updates and the evolving post‑Russell index removal shareholder base as secondary drivers of sentiment rather than new discrete news.

themes.name: Bitcoin Mining & Crypto Infrastructure Operators
themes.description: The main catalyst pushing CIFR, CORZ, HUT, IREN, and WULF higher this week is renewed focus on bitcoin miners as scarce, large-scale power and data-center landlords for the AI compute boom, with investors re-rating those that have significant contracted or negotiable power capacity and AI/HPC pivot plans. VanEck’s widely circulated note on a $50B+ funding gap and market preference for miners with real leases and anchor AI deals (highlighting CIFR, HUT, WULF, CORZ, and IREN as better positioned) has sharpened that narrative and driven flows into the group. This is layered on top of ongoing bitcoin price resilience and deal flow headlines (new leases, capacity expansions, and tenant negotiations) that reinforce the AI-infrastructure thesis for these names.

themes.name: AI Data Center Power Infrastructure
themes.description: BE is likely being lifted by the AI/data-center power trade, with demand for on-site fuel-cell power and the large Wyoming data-center buildout cited as key catalysts. VRT is moving with the same AI infrastructure/critical power theme, while AGX appears to be reacting more to a disclosed congressional stock purchase than a broad industry catalyst. FCEL is also benefiting from the fuel-cell/data-center power narrative, but the move is smaller and looks more thematic than company-specific.

themes.name: Semiconductor Probe Card & Front-End Test Equipment
themes.description: Teradyne (TER) and Cohu (COHU) are moving higher this week primarily on the back of renewed analyst enthusiasm for semiconductor test and capital equipment, highlighted by Argus raising its target price on Teradyne to $482 on June 24, 2026. That upgrade and similar target hikes across the semi equipment complex are reinforcing the AI/advanced packaging growth narrative and improving sentiment toward test-focused names. Broader sector strength tied to expectations for continued HBM, data center, and AI-related capex is providing additional tailwind.

themes.name: Commercial Real Estate Brokerage & Advisory Services
themes.description: The likely catalyst is a broker/analyst upgrade wave in real estate services, with Yahoo Finance showing target-price raises for CWK and JLL this week. That kind of sector-wide analyst optimism can also lift CBRE and NMRK as peers even if the specific note names only a subset directly.

themes.name: Pure-Play Quantum Computing Hardware
themes.description: The move in IONQ, QUBT, RGTI and SKYT this week is being driven by renewed speculative interest in the quantum computing theme, helped by social/media chatter and influencer coverage around “four quantum stocks” and the broader Nasdaq/AI rebound rather than a specific fundamental company headline. There is also some rotation back into high‑beta, future‑tech names after recent underperformance, with traders leaning into the quantum narrative alongside AI and semiconductor strength. No single stock-specific catalyst (earnings, major contract, or regulatory event) appears to be the primary driver; it’s largely thematic momentum and sentiment.

themes.name: In Vivo Gene & Engineered Cell Therapy Clinical Re-Rating
themes.description: A cluster of clinical-stage companies using genetic delivery modalities (in vivo AAV gene therapy, CAR-T, engineered cell platforms) are accelerating together as multi-program data readouts and regulatory momentum de-risk the therapeutic modality itself — distinct from the tools/sequencing 'Genomic Medicine' theme and from small-molecule rare-disease plays.

themes.name: Wealth Management & Retail Brokerage Platforms
themes.description: The main catalyst this week is rising expectations for higher-for-longer interest rates and steeper yield curves, which improve net interest income and cash sweep economics for wealth/asset managers and retail brokers like AMP, LPLA, RJF, SCHW, SEIC, and SF. There is also incremental optimism around retail trading activity and flows into advisory/managed accounts, supporting revenue and margin outlooks across the group. Sector rotation into financials as a relative value play is amplifying the move.

themes.name: Precision Optics & Photonic Components
themes.description: OPTX and LPTH are being driven higher primarily by their recent inclusion in the Russell 3000 index, which is attracting passive index and ETF buying, plus incremental attention from AI/photonics-focused investors. In addition, OPTX reported a new U.S. military order to expand AI-driven AR display optics, providing a fundamental contract win that supports the move. LASR is moving in sympathy as part of the broader AI photonics/optical interconnects theme that traders are rotating into this week.

themes.name: Industrial Power Equipment & Electrical Systems
themes.description: ETN, GNRC, and RRX are all benefiting from the ongoing AI/data center and grid-upgrade theme, which is driving strong flows into power management and specialty industrial machinery names, with Generac highlighted among top performers in the sector. For ETN specifically, the major catalyst is continued AI-driven data center demand and grid-constrained power investment, reinforced by bullish technicals (10-day MA crossing above 50-day MA) and recent analyst target hikes. GNRC and RRX are moving in sympathy as investors rotate into electrification, backup power, and industrial safety beneficiaries ahead of the next earnings season in industrials.

themes.name: Precision Aerospace & Defense Component Manufacturers
themes.description: The likely catalyst is ADX’s expansion with a new GCC dividend ETF, which is broadening regional exposure and appears to be boosting sentiment around the exchange. That’s the only clear news item in the feed tied directly to ADX strength this week.

themes.name: Midstream Energy Infrastructure & Pipeline Operators
themes.description: The move is mainly being driven by the midstream/energy tape firming on higher natural-gas and LNG-related sentiment, plus project/earnings optimism around pipeline growth and cash flows. Among these names, TRGP is also likely benefiting from AI/data-center power demand exposure, while TRP and KMI are more tied to LNG export and pipeline catalysts. AM and DTM look like they’re trading in sympathy with the stronger midstream group rather than on a company-specific headline.

themes.name: Self-Storage REITs
themes.description: The move in PSA, EXR, CUBE, and NSA this week is being driven primarily by a sector-wide bid into REITs as investors rotate back into defensive, income-oriented names alongside falling long-term yields and growing expectations for Fed rate cuts. Self‑storage is getting incremental support from stabilizing fundamentals commentary and relative outperformance vs. other property types, so capital is gravitating to the higher‑quality, large‑cap platforms. There is no single company‑specific headline; it’s a macro rates/REIT re‑risking catalyst with a self‑storage tilt.

themes.name: Large-Cap U.S. Defense Primes
themes.description: The main catalyst is a broad repricing of the defense/space complex after analysts have characterized this as the “best-in-a-generation” time to buy space and defense stocks, driving inflows into prime contractors like HII, LHX, LMT, and NOC. That call is reinforcing year-to-date strength and momentum in these names as investors rotate toward defense spending, military modernization, and geopolitically linked beneficiaries. Company-specific contract wins and solid earnings prints are secondary supports, but the sector-level analyst narrative is the primary driver this week.

themes.name: Alternative Asset Management Platforms
themes.description: Large alternative asset managers are moving together as fee-earning AUM growth from retail/wealth channel democratization (evergreen funds, 401k access) and infrastructure/private credit deployment drives earnings upgrades. The group benefits from rising management fees on locked-up capital that is less sensitive to market volatility than traditional active managers. Institutional appetite for private markets continues to expand, lengthening the runway for AUM compounding.

themes.name: Quantum Computing Hardware & Annealing Systems
themes.description: They’re all riding the same macro quantum-computing wave: the U.S. Commerce Department’s new $2 billion quantum program, which earmarks about $100 million each for D‑Wave (QBTS), Rigetti (RGTI), and Infleqtion (INFQ), plus separate funding for Infleqtion and peers, is being repriced into the group. A recent presidential executive order and high-profile White House/CEO event highlighting quantum, plus new coverage and “Outperform” initiation on Infleqtion, have further spotlighted the space. In that backdrop, speculative names like Quantum Computing Inc. (QUBT) are catching sympathy flows as investors look for broader quantum exposure.

themes.name: Domestic Steel Producers
themes.description: A 4-stock correlation cluster (corr 0.83, avg RS 28) of U.S. steel manufacturers — CMC, NUE, RS, and STLD — is moving in lockstep, likely reflecting shared sensitivity to domestic steel pricing, tariff protection, and infrastructure/construction demand. While RS levels remain moderate, the high correlation suggests capital is beginning to rotate into domestic steel ahead of infrastructure spending acceleration and potential trade policy tailwinds favoring U.S. producers over imported steel.

themes.name: Precious Metals Miners (Gold & Silver)
themes.description: A 29-stock correlation cluster (corr 0.80) spanning gold and silver miners is moving together statistically, reflecting broad precious metals price action driven by macro uncertainty, dollar weakness, and safe-haven demand. While average RS remains low (16), the size and coherence of the cluster signal this is a macro rotation in early stages rather than individual stock stories. Names span royalty streamers (FNV, WPM, RGLD), senior producers (NEM, AU, AEM), and junior miners (EXK, MUX, HL).

themes.name: Crypto Asset Recovery
themes.description: Auto-retired 2026-07-08: dropped during merge/absorption (no successor found) (prior stage Fading, 2 tickers).

themes.name: Non-US & Frontier Market Digital Financial Services Re-Rating
themes.description: A cross-sector cohort of non-US digital financial ecosystem operators — spanning Southeast Asian e-commerce/payments, Japanese fintech, cross-border remittance rails, and Central Asian/Eastern European brokerage — is being simultaneously re-rated as investors seek digital economy exposure outside US megacap platforms, with each name showing coordinated RS acceleration from deeply oversold bases.

themes.name: Oilfield Pressure Pumping & Completion Services
themes.description: Hydraulic fracturing and oilfield completion service companies are correlating tightly at 0.81 as E&P operators maintain completion activity despite oil price softness, driven by efficiency gains and contract backlogs. This sub-sector of oilfield services is more levered to completion intensity (frac stages per well) than rig count, insulating it from pure drilling slowdowns. Any uptick in commodity prices or permitting activity would accelerate the group.

themes.name: Rare & Orphan Disease Biotech
themes.description: A cluster of clinical-stage rare and orphan disease biotechs is showing synchronized RS acceleration from deeply oversold levels (all were below RS 30, now 91-99), signaling institutional re-accumulation. Orphan drug designation provides pricing power and M&A attractiveness as large pharma seeks de-risked pipeline assets. The simultaneous 4-week rotation across mechanistically distinct programs (gene therapy, SMA, neuroendocrine, orphan hepatology) points to sector-level capital rotation rather than individual stock catalysts.

themes.name: Network Security & Zero-Trust Edge
themes.description: The group is moving on the back of a broad bid into cybersecurity names following renewed headline risk from high‑profile breaches and rising expectations for increased corporate and public sector security spending. In addition, investors are rotating into higher‑growth software/security after recent rate‑cut rhetoric eased pressure on long‑duration tech, lifting sentiment across the space. Stock‑specific drivers are mainly positive sell‑side commentary and target hikes clustered around the sector rather than any single, company‑level catalyst this week.

themes.name: Digital Advertising Technology Platforms
themes.description: MGNI and APPS are moving higher this week on a broad ad-tech rebound, with traders also positioning ahead of second-quarter earnings. For Magnite specifically, the stock has been helped by continued optimism around CTV and programmatic ad demand; for AppLovin, the main catalyst is still momentum tied to its AI-driven ad platform and strong monetization narrative.

themes.name: Peptide & Hormone Therapies for Metabolic & Endocrine Disorders
themes.description: There is no single sector-wide headline; these are mostly stock-specific moves tied to recent or upcoming catalysts and positioning in small/mid-cap biotech. ASND, VKTX, XERS, MBX, CORT and PTGX are all in the healthcare/biopharma complex and several are in the earnings window or have key clinical/regulatory events on deck, so traders are bidding them up on event anticipation and short-covering. In this kind of tape, thinly traded biotech names often move together when capital rotates into high‑beta healthcare and AI/quant screens flag them as fundamentally or event‑rich names.

themes.name: Inflammatory Disease & Immunology Biologics
themes.description: ORKA is higher this week on a positive company-specific catalyst, but the provided results do not identify the exact headline behind the move. The stock was already up about 31.9% over the prior 30 days, suggesting momentum around a recent event or update, though the specific trigger isn’t visible in these results.

themes.name: Consumer Fintech & Digital Credit Platforms
themes.description: DAVE and SEZL are moving higher this week primarily on continued momentum from strong year-to-date performance and improving fintech sentiment, rather than a single new company-specific headline. Recent analyst commentary has highlighted Sezzle’s rapid gross profit and EBITDA growth, Buy Now Pay Later market share gains, and potential for further EPS guidance raises, reinforcing the bull narrative. In a market where breadth is expanding and investors are rotating into fintech and “other” high-growth groups, both names are benefiting from sector inflows and momentum.

themes.name: Liquid Biopsy & Multi-Cancer Early Detection
themes.description: The primary catalyst for GRAL (GRAIL, Inc.) moving higher this week is read-through enthusiasm from Guardant Health’s strong oncology and screening momentum, particularly around blood-based cancer screening platforms and valuation re-rating in the space. As investors focus on liquid biopsy and early-detection leaders, GRAIL is benefiting from sector-wide rerating tied to growth expectations and increasing confidence in commercial adoption of multi-cancer and colorectal screening tests.

themes.name: Targeted Protein Degradation Oncology
themes.description: KYMR is being driven higher by renewed focus on its KT-621 oral STAT6 degrader program for atopic dermatitis and inflammatory diseases, with investors positioning ahead of key Phase 1 data and a “defining” 2H 2026 catalyst window highlighted in recent analysis. GLUE is rallying primarily on the back of its large, up-to-$5.7 billion molecular glue licensing deal with Novartis, which includes $150 million upfront and has reframed the valuation of the platform. Both names are also benefiting from a broader re-rating of molecular glue degraders as a high-value drug development theme.

themes.name: Genomic Medicine & Synthetic DNA Tools
themes.description: MRNA is being driven higher by the unanimous FDA advisory panel endorsement of its mRNA-1010 flu vaccine, which sharply reduced regulatory risk ahead of the August 5 PDUFA decision and positioned flu as a second major commercial pillar beyond COVID. Additional support is coming from Science Day updates highlighting a broader mRNA pipeline and platform strategy, plus rising analyst and media focus on non-COVID assets and pipeline diversification. TWST’s move is being fueled by renewed interest in synthetic biology and genomics tools as capital rotates back into higher-growth life sciences names, with investors bidding up platform companies leveraged to pharma and biotech R&D spend.

themes.name: Digital Insurance Distribution & Marketplace Platforms
themes.description: Goosehead Insurance (GSHD) is moving higher on anticipation ahead of its upcoming earnings and continued momentum in the insurance brokerage space, with investors rotating into fee-based, less rate-sensitive financials. Porch Group (PRCH) is rallying on improving sentiment toward small-cap tech and housing-adjacent names as the market prices in a firmer housing backdrop and potential operating leverage in its platform model. No single discrete headline is driving either; it’s mainly positioning around earnings expectations and sector momentum.

themes.name: U.S. Petroleum Refining & Downstream Processing
themes.description: Refiners are catching a bid this week mainly on tightening product markets: strong summer gasoline and jet demand, firmer refining margins, and improving crack spreads, all supportive of earnings power into Q3. There’s also a broader rotation back into energy as investors look for cyclical value exposure amid lingering concerns about inflation and geopolitics. No single company-specific headline is driving it; it’s a sector move tied to macro demand, margins, and positioning.

themes.name: AI Cloud GPU & Datacenter Colocation Platforms
themes.description: There is no clear single news catalyst this week specifically driving WYFI, PENG, and SHAZ higher; price action appears more tied to broader speculative interest in AI/data-infrastructure names and short-term technical momentum rather than a discrete headline. None of the three have a widely cited new deal, regulatory event, or earnings surprise in the past few days, and their moves look more like rotation within high-beta tech. If you’re seeing outsized intraday spikes, they are likely trader-driven squeezes and theme-chasing rather than fundamental news.

themes.name: SMB & Workforce Business Services Platforms
themes.description: The move in LZ this week is most likely being driven by a broader small-cap/quality-factor bid rather than a company-specific headline, based on the available data. I don’t see a clear news catalyst in the results provided, so the rally may be technical or factor-driven.

themes.name: Non-US & Frontier Market Digital Financial Services Re-Rating
themes.description: SE (Sea Ltd) is trading higher on follow-through from its recent earnings beat and improving profitability trends in e-commerce and digital entertainment, which have attracted renewed buying interest from growth managers. WSE (Westwing Group) and PAYP (PayPoint) are moving up largely on rotation back into European consumer/retail and payments names ahead of the next earnings cycle, with short covering amplifying the move. FRHC (Freedom Holding) is benefiting from optimism around its Kazakh and Central Asian brokerage and banking operations, where stronger regional trading volumes and expectations of regulatory clarity have driven incremental institutional inflows.

themes.name: Cloud Observability & AIOps Monitoring Platforms
themes.description: For Datadog, the main catalyst is strong Q1 2026 fundamentals (revenue breaking $1B, accelerating growth, raised full‑year guidance) combined with AI-driven product momentum, including over 100 new capabilities unveiled at DASH 2026 and the acquisition of Adaptive ML, which reinforces its AI observability and autonomous operations narrative. For Dynatrace, there is no specific, stock‑specific news item this week; the move is largely tied to broader enterprise software and large‑cap growth bid supported by a more gradual Fed policy path and ongoing AI/observability demand rather than a discrete headline catalyst.

themes.name: Casual Dining Restaurant Turnaround
themes.description: The primary catalyst driving CBRL higher this week is anticipation around upcoming corporate updates and earnings season dynamics, with investors rotating into discretionary and restaurant names viewed as potential beneficiaries of a “K-shaped” consumer recovery narrative. There is also some positioning ahead of company-specific events like business update calls and broader sector earnings that can re-rate restaurant chains on guidance and traffic trends. No single headline appears to be responsible; it is more a combination of earnings-season sentiment and positioning in beaten-down consumer names.

themes.name: Franchise Auto Dealership Groups
themes.description: ABG, SAH, and PAG are riding the same core catalyst: a sharp bid into U.S. auto retail and dealer stocks as investors rotate into economically sensitive, rate-cut beneficiaries with still-resilient consumer demand. The group is also catching a high-beta tailwind from this week’s broader U.S. equity/AI-led risk-on move, which is lifting cyclicals and value names tied to real-economy spending.

themes.name: U.S. Passenger Airlines
themes.description: The main catalyst is Goldman Sachs turning broadly bullish on the U.S. airline sector, raising net profit forecasts for Q3/Q4 2026 and hiking price targets for United, Allegiant, Delta, SkyWest and others on the back of resilient demand and improved industry pricing and capacity discipline. Additional support comes from lower fuel prices after Middle East risk premium faded, which improves margin outlook versus prior expectations. The move is sector-wide rather than company-specific, so these names are rising on upgraded earnings/price-target revisions and a stronger macro backdrop for airlines.

themes.name: Automotive Electronics & Sensor Components
themes.description: MEI’s move appears tied to momentum in Methode Electronics after a sharp March-to-May run and a recent consolidation breakout narrative, not a clear company-specific news event. I don’t see a fresh catalyst in the provided results for OUST or AEVA, so their strength is likely part of a broader small-cap/AI-autonomy momentum trade rather than new headline news.

themes.name: Truckload & LTL Freight Carriers
themes.description: The move is being driven by a broader rebound in trucking and freight stocks, helped by signs that the intermodal/freight market is tightening and improving. The clearest catalyst in the provided news is STG Logistics exiting Chapter 11 after cutting debt by about 90%, which is fueling optimism across the logistics group.

themes.name: Office REIT Recovery & Re-Rating
themes.description: SLG, CDP and HIW are all office-focused REITs, and the main catalyst pushing them higher this week is renewed interest in the beaten-down office REIT space as investors position ahead of upcoming inflation/Fed commentary and potential rate-cut expectations, which directly support cap rates and valuations. There is also some short-covering and mean-reversion in higher-quality office names after a prolonged period of underperformance, with buying focused in names perceived to have more resilient tenant bases and balance sheets. No stock-specific headline has dominated; it’s primarily a sector-wide macro and rate-driven move.

themes.name: Specialty Catastrophe Property Insurance Underwriters
themes.description: SLDE NP is moving higher on optimism around Neptune Insurance’s execution and fundamentals, with investors viewing prior concerns as largely priced in and sentiment improving. The stock is also benefiting from the recent broker action that, despite a rating downgrade to Market Perform, raised the price target to $34, reinforcing a higher valuation baseline. Broader insurance sector strength and risk-on sentiment this week are adding incremental support to the move.

themes.name: Insurance Brokerage & Distribution Services
themes.description: AON, AJG, and BRO are higher mainly on a sector rerating: investors are rotating into insurance brokers after mixed-to-strong brokerage commentary and relative-strength buying in the group. The best read from the available items is that the move is not one single company-specific headline, but broader optimism around margins, pricing power, and defensive characteristics in the insurance-broker space.

themes.name: Custom AI Silicon & Chip Architecture Licensing
themes.description: The group is moving on the back of renewed enthusiasm around AI infrastructure and edge compute, with investors rotating into mixed-signal, connectivity, and IP names levered to accelerating data center and networking spend. Specifically, positive sell-side commentary on AI-related custom silicon, stronger-than-expected demand indications for high-speed connectivity/optical interconnect, and expectations for incremental ARM-based and RISC-V design wins are lifting CEVA, ALAB, ARM, MXL, MRVL, and CRDO. There is no single stock-specific headline; the catalyst is a sector-wide AI semiconductor and communications upcycle narrative.

themes.name: Specialty Insurance Underwriting & Brokerage
themes.description: The move in the insurance brokers/MGAs this week is being driven mainly by a sector-wide bid on expectations for strong Q2 earnings and continued pricing strength in commercial P&C, rather than a single stock-specific headline for WTW, KNSL, RYAN, or PLMR. Investors are leaning into names with visible organic growth, margin leverage from higher rates, and resilient demand for specialty and excess lines. There is also some rotation back into defensive financials after recent volatility in rate-cut expectations, which is benefiting the group.

themes.name: Senior Living & Housing Operators
themes.description: BKD is trading higher on the back of its June financing announcement, where Brookdale extended 2027 non‑recourse mortgage maturities, secured $188M in new Freddie Mac loans, repaid $200M of mortgage debt, and expanded its credit facility to $200M maturing in 2029. This materially improves liquidity and pushes out debt maturities, which is supportive for the equity and out-of-the-money calls like JAN. Broader strength in healthcare and senior living names alongside improving occupancy trends this year is likely adding incremental support.

themes.name: Property & Casualty Insurance Underwriters
themes.description: The move is primarily part of a broad bid into P&C insurers as investors rotate into defensive, rate‑sensitive financials on the back of stable-to-lower long‑term yields and benign catastrophe activity so far in the current storm season. There has also been ongoing support from the soft landing narrative for the US economy, which favors carriers with strong commercial and specialty exposure like TRV, CINF, WRB and ORI. No single stock‑specific headline is driving the group; it’s mostly sector‑level macro positioning and relief that loss trends remain manageable.

themes.name: Life Insurance & Annuity Providers
themes.description: The main catalyst is a sector-wide rerating of life insurers on expectations that higher-for-longer interest rates and tight credit spreads will continue to support investment income and reserve economics, driving better return-on-equity profiles for PFG, PRU, UNM, and MET. In addition, investors are positioning ahead of upcoming Q2 earnings with a bias that asset-sensitive financials will show resilient spread income and strong capital return (buybacks/dividends), attracting rotation out of more fully priced growth and AI names. There is no single company-specific headline; it’s a macro/sector trade around rates, earnings, and capital return.

themes.name: B2B Digital Financial Infrastructure & Payment Rails Modernization
themes.description: PAY is likely moving on sector-wide enthusiasm ahead of Q2 fintech earnings and ongoing optimism that digital payments volumes remain resilient despite higher-for-longer rates. QTWO is grinding higher on expectations for solid Q2 SaaS results and continued digital banking adoption, with investors positioning ahead of earnings rather than reacting to a single stock-specific headline. ALKT’s strength is being driven by Jana Partners’ activist campaign pushing the company to explore a sale and governance changes, which has sharpened focus on takeover optionality and re-rated the stock.

themes.name: European Bank Stocks
themes.description: The move is largely driven by renewed expectations for lower ECB and BoE rates later this year, which boost the outlook for European and UK rate‑sensitive banks like Santander, ING, BBVA, NatWest, Lloyds and Barclays. In addition, better‑than‑feared recent economic data in the eurozone and UK and a stabilization in credit spreads are supporting the sector. Some flow is also rotation into financials ahead of the next earnings season as investors position for still‑solid net interest income and potential capital return updates.

themes.name: Wealth Management & Retail Brokerage Platforms
themes.description: The main catalyst pushing AMP, RJF, SCHW, and SEIC higher this week is rotation into U.S. wealth management and asset/retirement platforms as investors position for a softer Fed path and ongoing equity market strength, which supports fee-based revenue and AUM growth. The group is also benefiting from improving net new asset trends, stabilizing cash sorting, and the view that rate cuts will be gradual enough to preserve spread income while reigniting flows into advisory and managed accounts. A lack of negative company-specific news plus upcoming earnings as a potential positive catalyst is reinforcing the bid in the space.

themes.name: Semiconductor Wafer Foundry & Advanced IC Manufacturing
themes.description: The group is trading higher on a broad semiconductor equipment rebound, with investors rotating into names exposed to foundry and memory capex after recent optimism around a healthier 2026 spending cycle and AI-driven wafer-fab demand. MKSI, ACMR, UMC, KLAC, and AMAT are especially sensitive to this theme; PDF Solutions is also benefiting indirectly from stronger chip-production activity and equipment spending. The move appears to be sector-driven rather than tied to one single company-specific headline.

themes.name: U.S. Regional & Mid-Cap Commercial Banks
themes.description: The move is being driven less by bank-specific news and more by a broad regional-bank rally, with investors rotating into financials on the view that the group can benefit from a steadier rate outlook and stronger earnings leverage. Among the names you listed, the most specific near-term catalyst is FITB’s upgrade to Buy and related positive analyst commentary, which is helping sentiment across the peer group.

themes.name: AI Memory & Storage
themes.description: The move is being driven by a broad flash-memory rally after SanDisk/SNDK turned into the market’s leadership name this week, with traders betting on stronger pricing, AI storage demand, and a tighter NAND supply backdrop lifting the entire group. That strength is spilling over into Micron, Western Digital, Seagate, Silicon Motion, and Qumulo as investors rotate into storage-semiconductor beneficiaries rather than reacting to a single company-specific headline.

themes.name: Private Mortgage Insurance
themes.description: The move is most likely being driven by a sector re-rating in mortgage insurers, with broker price-target updates lifting sentiment across MTG and NMIH and spilling over to ESNT and RDN. Yahoo Finance’s specialty insurance snapshot shows higher targets for MTG and NMIH, which is consistent with the group trading higher on valuation-positive news rather than a company-specific catalyst.

themes.name: Senior & Long-Term Care Healthcare REITs
themes.description: The move across senior housing/healthcare REITs this week is being driven mainly by a sector-wide bid on the “defensive yield + aging demographics” theme as rates drift lower and investors rotate back into higher-dividend, non-cyclical real estate. There hasn’t been a stock-specific headline for SBRA, VTR, OHI, NHI, or LTC; instead, the catalyst is broader REIT factor buying and short-covering in healthcare names ahead of the next earnings cycle. In other words, it’s a macro/rates and positioning story, not a company-level news event.

themes.name: Coastal & Suburban Residential Rental REITs
themes.description: The move is being driven primarily by a macro REIT/housing trade: falling long-term yields and growing expectations of Fed easing are supporting interest-rate-sensitive residential REITs, while housing fundamentals remain solid. Additionally, a widely circulated piece this week arguing the U.S. housing shortage may be peaking but not reversing quickly has refocused investors on institutional single-family and multifamily landlords as structural beneficiaries of constrained affordability and rental demand. There does not appear to be a stock-specific catalyst unique to INVH, EQR, AMH, AVB, or ESS beyond this broader factor rotation into residential REITs.

themes.name: Multifamily Apartment REITs
themes.description: The move is being driven mainly by a broad bid into multifamily/apartment REITs as investors price in a turning point in the apartment rental cycle, with expectations of moderating new supply and improving rent growth in key Sunbelt and coastal markets. Recent commentary from names like Camden Property Trust highlighting 4–5% expected rent growth in 2026–2027 as Sunbelt supply declines has reinforced the bullish narrative for the whole group. There is no single stock-specific headline this week; it is a sector-wide re‑rating on the improving fundamental outlook.

themes.name: Industrial & Logistics REITs
themes.description: EGP, FR and TRNO are all trading higher this week primarily on continued strength and fund inflows into the industrial REIT space, as investors rotate back into high-quality logistics and infill industrial names leveraged to still-tight warehouse markets and potential rate-cut tailwinds. TRNO in particular is benefiting from bullish analyst positioning and a constructive earnings/FFO growth outlook after recent commentary and estimates that highlight above-average rent spreads and balance sheet capacity for accretive acquisitions. There has been no single stock-specific headline this week; the move is largely sector- and macro-driven re‑rating.

themes.name: Golf Equipment & Apparel
themes.description: The main catalyst this week for Callaway Golf (CALY) is a wave of bullish analyst activity, including target price hikes toward the 19 area and positive relative performance commentary versus other consumer discretionary names. Shares have broken to new 52-week highs on momentum as investors chase year-to-date outperformance of roughly 50–55%, rather than reacting to any specific company news or fundamental headline. In short, it’s an analyst- and momentum-driven move, not a discrete news event.

themes.name: Open-Air & Strip Mall Retail REITs
themes.description: The main catalyst is renewed interest in retail REITs as a group on the back of stabilizing interest-rate expectations and improving brick-and-mortar traffic data, driving sector-wide inflows into names like KRG, PECO, SKT, KIM, and UE. There is also positioning ahead of the coming Q2 earnings season, with investors rotating into higher-quality, open-air and outlet-focused retail REITs perceived as beneficiaries of resilient consumer spending. No stock-specific headline is driving the move; it is primarily a sector and factor rotation trade.

themes.name: Government-Funded Senior & Post-Acute Care Re-Rating
themes.description: ALHC (Alignment Healthcare) and AVAH (Aveanna Healthcare) do not appear to have a specific company‑level news catalyst this week; the move looks tied to broader strength in value and small/mid‑cap healthcare services names as investors rotate into defensive, managed-care and home health exposure. Both benefit from the current bid into Medicare/Medicaid and home‑care reimbursement plays as rates stabilize and election‑related policy risk is being repriced more favorably. The rally is more sector‑flow driven than headline‑driven.

themes.name: Satellite Communications & Space Data Services
themes.description: VSAT, SPIR, and MDA are being driven higher this week by a broad space sector risk-on move tied to major catalysts: Rocket Lab’s acquisition of Iridium, the surge in space-focused ETFs like Tema Space Innovators (NASA), and heightened investor focus on the upcoming SpaceX listing as a valuation and liquidity benchmark for the sector. These names are key components or peers in the “new space” infrastructure and services theme, so they are catching flows and momentum as capital rotates into space stocks.

themes.name: Quantum Computing & Quantum-Safe Networking
themes.description: QNT and ARQQ are moving higher this week on the back of a broad quantum-computing sector re‑rating following Quantinuum’s recent Nasdaq listing and growing media focus on “next wave” quantum names, which has pulled in speculative and thematic ETF money into the group. In particular, QNT is benefiting from its high-profile IPO and leadership narrative in logical qubits, while ARQQ is catching sympathy flows as investors look for liquid quantum encryption exposure within the same theme. Macro catalysts like the upcoming Fed meeting and ongoing AI/infrastructure hype are adding a supportive risk-on backdrop for high-beta tech, amplifying those sector-specific moves.

themes.name: Bitcoin Mining & Crypto Infrastructure Operators
themes.description: The main catalyst pushing CIFR, CORZ, HUT, IREN, and WULF higher this week is renewed focus on bitcoin miners as scarce, large-scale power and data-center landlords for the AI compute boom, with investors re-rating those that have significant contracted or negotiable power capacity and AI/HPC pivot plans. VanEck’s widely circulated note on a $50B+ funding gap and market preference for miners with real leases and anchor AI deals (highlighting CIFR, HUT, WULF, CORZ, and IREN as better positioned) has sharpened that narrative and driven flows into the group. This is layered on top of ongoing bitcoin price resilience and deal flow headlines (new leases, capacity expansions, and tenant negotiations) that reinforce the AI-infrastructure thesis for these names.

themes.name: AI Data Center Power Infrastructure
themes.description: BE is likely being lifted by the AI/data-center power trade, with demand for on-site fuel-cell power and the large Wyoming data-center buildout cited as key catalysts. VRT is moving with the same AI infrastructure/critical power theme, while AGX appears to be reacting more to a disclosed congressional stock purchase than a broad industry catalyst. FCEL is also benefiting from the fuel-cell/data-center power narrative, but the move is smaller and looks more thematic than company-specific.

themes.name: Oilfield Pressure Pumping & Completion Services
themes.description: Hydraulic fracturing and oilfield completion service companies are correlating tightly at 0.81 as E&P operators maintain completion activity despite oil price softness, driven by efficiency gains and contract backlogs. This sub-sector of oilfield services is more levered to completion intensity (frac stages per well) than rig count, insulating it from pure drilling slowdowns. Any uptick in commodity prices or permitting activity would accelerate the group.

themes.name: Semiconductor Probe Card & Front-End Test Equipment
themes.description: Teradyne (TER) and Cohu (COHU) are moving higher this week primarily on the back of renewed analyst enthusiasm for semiconductor test and capital equipment, highlighted by Argus raising its target price on Teradyne to $482 on June 24, 2026. That upgrade and similar target hikes across the semi equipment complex are reinforcing the AI/advanced packaging growth narrative and improving sentiment toward test-focused names. Broader sector strength tied to expectations for continued HBM, data center, and AI-related capex is providing additional tailwind.

themes.name: Commercial Real Estate Brokerage & Advisory Services
themes.description: The likely catalyst is a broker/analyst upgrade wave in real estate services, with Yahoo Finance showing target-price raises for CWK and JLL this week. That kind of sector-wide analyst optimism can also lift CBRE and NMRK as peers even if the specific note names only a subset directly.

themes.name: Pure-Play Quantum Computing Hardware
themes.description: The move in IONQ, QUBT, RGTI and SKYT this week is being driven by renewed speculative interest in the quantum computing theme, helped by social/media chatter and influencer coverage around “four quantum stocks” and the broader Nasdaq/AI rebound rather than a specific fundamental company headline. There is also some rotation back into high‑beta, future‑tech names after recent underperformance, with traders leaning into the quantum narrative alongside AI and semiconductor strength. No single stock-specific catalyst (earnings, major contract, or regulatory event) appears to be the primary driver; it’s largely thematic momentum and sentiment.

themes.name: Precision Optics & Photonic Components
themes.description: OPTX and LPTH are being driven higher primarily by their recent inclusion in the Russell 3000 index, which is attracting passive index and ETF buying, plus incremental attention from AI/photonics-focused investors. In addition, OPTX reported a new U.S. military order to expand AI-driven AR display optics, providing a fundamental contract win that supports the move. LASR is moving in sympathy as part of the broader AI photonics/optical interconnects theme that traders are rotating into this week.

themes.name: Industrial Power Equipment & Electrical Systems
themes.description: ETN, GNRC, and RRX are all benefiting from the ongoing AI/data center and grid-upgrade theme, which is driving strong flows into power management and specialty industrial machinery names, with Generac highlighted among top performers in the sector. For ETN specifically, the major catalyst is continued AI-driven data center demand and grid-constrained power investment, reinforced by bullish technicals (10-day MA crossing above 50-day MA) and recent analyst target hikes. GNRC and RRX are moving in sympathy as investors rotate into electrification, backup power, and industrial safety beneficiaries ahead of the next earnings season in industrials.

themes.name: Self-Storage REITs
themes.description: The move in PSA, EXR, CUBE, and NSA this week is being driven primarily by a sector-wide bid into REITs as investors rotate back into defensive, income-oriented names alongside falling long-term yields and growing expectations for Fed rate cuts. Self‑storage is getting incremental support from stabilizing fundamentals commentary and relative outperformance vs. other property types, so capital is gravitating to the higher‑quality, large‑cap platforms. There is no single company‑specific headline; it’s a macro rates/REIT re‑risking catalyst with a self‑storage tilt.

themes.name: Large-Cap U.S. Defense Primes
themes.description: The main catalyst is a broad repricing of the defense/space complex after analysts have characterized this as the “best-in-a-generation” time to buy space and defense stocks, driving inflows into prime contractors like HII, LHX, LMT, and NOC. That call is reinforcing year-to-date strength and momentum in these names as investors rotate toward defense spending, military modernization, and geopolitically linked beneficiaries. Company-specific contract wins and solid earnings prints are secondary supports, but the sector-level analyst narrative is the primary driver this week.

themes.name: Alternative Asset Management Platforms
themes.description: Large alternative asset managers are moving together as fee-earning AUM growth from retail/wealth channel democratization (evergreen funds, 401k access) and infrastructure/private credit deployment drives earnings upgrades. The group benefits from rising management fees on locked-up capital that is less sensitive to market volatility than traditional active managers. Institutional appetite for private markets continues to expand, lengthening the runway for AUM compounding.

themes.name: Domestic Steel Producers
themes.description: Auto-retired 2026-07-09: dropped during merge/absorption (no successor found) (prior stage Fading, 2 tickers).
