themes.name: Rare & Orphan Disease Biotech
themes.description: Auto-retired 2026-07-09: dropped during merge/absorption (no successor found) (prior stage Nascent, 5 tickers).

themes.name: Clinical-Stage Autoimmune & Inflammatory Disease Therapeutics
themes.description: A concentrated rotation into clinical-stage autoimmune and inflammatory disease biotechs is underway, with multiple names turning from single-digit RS to the 80-95 range over 4 consecutive weeks — a classic institutional accumulation pattern. The catalyst appears to be a wave of positive Phase 2/3 readouts and expanding pipeline data in autoimmune indications, drawing capital from larger immunology franchises toward earlier-stage assets. The multi-week RS continuity across five distinct companies in the same disease space signals this is sector-specific rotation, not broad biotech beta.

themes.name: Clinical-Stage Oncology Drug Development
themes.description: A distinct oncology rotation is emerging separately from the autoimmune wave, with names spanning ADC (antibody-drug conjugate), immuno-oncology, RAS-targeted precision oncology, and hematology/oncology combinations all accelerating in RS simultaneously. RVMD and KURA lead with RS 96-97, while IMNM, CRVS, SNDX, and IMMX are all recovering from sub-30 RS levels over 4-week streaks. The shared catalyst is a dense schedule of cancer trial readouts and the continued broadening of precision oncology as a therapeutic category.

themes.name: Corporate Spend, Expense Management & Financial Operations Software
themes.description: NAVN (RS 98, AI-powered travel & expense management), WEX (RS 68, fleet payments and corporate expense), and BILL (RS 69, financial operations and AP automation) are all enterprise software platforms targeting corporate spend management — a category benefiting from AI-driven automation of back-office financial workflows. NAVN's surge to RS 98 as the AI-native T&E leader suggests the market is beginning to reprice this category around AI efficiency gains, potentially pulling the more established peers along. This nascent cluster is worth watching for confirmation.

themes.name: Molecular Diagnostics & DNA-Based Genetic Testing
themes.description: Three companies across molecular diagnostics and DNA-based genetic testing are showing synchronized RS strength: BLLN (RS 98, DNA detection/measurement), WGS (RS 79, genetic testing and rare disease diagnostics), and QGEN (RS 76, molecular diagnostics reagents and sample preparation). The common driver is rising demand for precision diagnostics tied to the rare disease and oncology boom — as genetic disease therapeutics proliferate, genetic testing and molecular diagnostics volume expands in lockstep. BLLN's RS 98 makes it the clear leader.

themes.name: Oncology Targeted Therapy Biotechs
themes.description: Capital is rotating sharply into clinical-stage and commercial oncology biotechs focused on precision cancer targeting via small molecules, ADCs, and cell-signaling inhibitors. Multiple names are simultaneously surging from washed-out RS bases, signaling institutional accumulation ahead of data readouts and approvals. The convergence of ELVN's RS 24→95 thrust, two RS-97 leaders, and sustained streaks across the group confirms this as a coordinated sector rotation.

themes.name: Rare Cardiometabolic & Endocrine Specialty Pharma
themes.description: A cluster of specialty pharma and clinical-stage biotechs targeting rare endocrine disorders, cardiopulmonary diseases, and aldosterone/cardiometabolic pathways is gaining simultaneous RS momentum. CRNX (rare endocrine, RS 100) and LQDA (rare cardiopulmonary, RS 98) are top-decile leaders, while MLYS and NAMS add confirmation from lower bases, all pointing to institutional re-rating of rare metabolic disease assets.

themes.name: Rare Disease Small Molecule Precision Therapeutics
themes.description: A cohort of clinical-stage biotechs developing oral small molecule drugs for rare kidney, metabolic, and precision-targeted diseases is seeing simultaneous RS improvement. GPCR (oral small molecule clinical-stage), MAZE (small molecule kidney/metabolic diseases), and VRDN (IGF-1R therapies for rare diseases) share a common drug modality and rare disease commercial pathway, increasingly favored as a risk-adjusted biotech approach.

themes.name: Rare Neurological, Neuromuscular & Genetic Disease Biotech
themes.description: Six clinical-stage and commercial-stage biotechs focused on rare neurological, neuromuscular, and genetic disease are all showing RS 90+ and multi-week acceleration from prior lows — a signal that institutional money is clustering into this corner of biotech. Catalysts include rare-disease FDA fast-track designations, orphan drug pricing power, and pipeline progression in previously intractable CNS and genetic conditions. CRNX (RS 100) and XENE (RS 98) are leading, with BBIO, SRRK, AMLX, and ACAD all in strong multi-week streaks.

themes.name: Network Security & Zero-Trust Edge
themes.description: The main catalyst is a continued bid into cybersecurity and data-protection names after several high-profile breaches and regulatory headlines, driving broad sector inflows. In addition, RPD, OKTA, FTNT, VRNS, CVLT and RBRK are all seen as core beneficiaries of increased enterprise security spending ahead of the next earnings cycle, with investors positioning for upside revisions. There does not appear to be a single company-specific event this week; it is primarily a sector rotation and thematic trade into security/software.

themes.name: Consumer Fintech & Digital Credit Platforms
themes.description: The main catalyst driving DAVE and SEZL higher this week is a combination of bullish fintech/rate‑sensitive sector momentum and fresh upside from analyst target price hikes. Argus raised its target price on Dave Inc. (DAVE) and Sezzle Inc. (SEZL) on July 8, 2026, which is supporting renewed buying interest in both names. In addition, traders are rotating into beaten-down fintechs as options flow and sector-wide momentum turn positive, as highlighted by recent commentary flagging “Fintech, Housing, Rate-sensitive names rip” including DAVE and SEZL.

themes.name: Digital Advertising Technology Platforms
themes.description: The move in Magnite (MGNI) and AppLovin (APP/APPs) this week is being driven mainly by strength in the broader ad-tech/AI complex and constructive sector sentiment following better-than-feared digital ad spend data points from major platforms. Investors are rotating back into programmatic and mobile ad-tech names as proxies on improving online ad demand, with Magnite and AppLovin catching follow-through buying from that thematic bid. I am not seeing a single company-specific headline catalyst this week; it’s primarily macro/sector-driven momentum.

themes.name: Peptide & Hormone Therapies for Metabolic & Endocrine Disorders
themes.description: Most of these moves are being driven by biotech-specific catalysts: positive clinical or pipeline updates and improving sentiment toward small- and mid-cap biotech after recent M&A and trial readouts in the sector. PTGX, ASND, VKTX, MBX, CORT, and XERS are all in healthcare/biopharma and are benefiting from investors rotating into higher-growth, higher–R&D names ahead of upcoming earnings and data events. Additionally, some are seeing follow-through buying from prior weeks’ favorable trial results, regulatory progress, or target price/ratings changes from analysts.

themes.name: Inflammatory Disease & Immunology Biologics
themes.description: ORKA is being driven higher by the Halozyme collaboration on ORKA-001, which validated the program and improved the drug’s delivery profile. The move was also supported by Oruka’s $700 million follow-on offering and recent analyst upgrades.

themes.name: Targeted Protein Degradation Oncology
themes.description: KYMR and GLUE are likely higher on a broader biotech/molecular-glue sentiment bid rather than one shared company-specific event. The clearest catalyst in the available news flow is Kymera’s recent strength tied to strong Q1 2026 results, while Monte Rosa appears to be moving with the sector and renewed enthusiasm around protein-degradation/molecular-glue names.

themes.name: Liquid Biopsy & Multi-Cancer Early Detection
themes.description: The move in GRAL GH this week is being driven primarily by renewed enthusiasm around Guardant Health’s oncology and colorectal cancer screening portfolio, after a strong Q4 2025 revenue beat and an upbeat 2026 growth outlook. Sentiment is being reinforced by the significant Medicare reimbursement level for its Shield blood-based colorectal cancer test, which investors view as a key long-term revenue catalyst. Social media and retail investor commentary highlighting GRAL/GH in healthcare-focused stock lists is adding some incremental momentum.

themes.name: Digital Insurance Distribution & Marketplace Platforms
themes.description: The primary catalyst is renewed takeover speculation and options-driven momentum after Porch Group rejected prior bids and traders rotated into small-cap housing/prop-tech names on the back of falling mortgage rates. For Goosehead Insurance, the move is being driven by broker/analyst chatter that lower rates and improving housing turnover will boost policy growth and Q3–Q4 earnings, leading to pre-earnings positioning and short covering. Both are also benefiting from a broader squeeze in high-short-interest, rate-sensitive financials this week.

themes.name: SMB & Workforce Business Services Platforms
themes.description: NSP LZ is trading higher this week on renewed interest in its role as a beneficiary of rising AI-driven electricity demand and cleaner on-site power solutions, centered on its KARNO compact power system narrative. A recent $10.8M insider purchase over the past 60 days is also reinforcing the bullish sentiment and attracting incremental smart-money attention.

themes.name: U.S. Petroleum Refining & Downstream Processing
themes.description: Refiner equities are being bid up on a combination of stronger-than-expected refining margins, recent upside earnings revisions/beats in the group, and continued tight global distillate and gasoline supply supporting crack spreads. There’s also a macro overlay of higher crude demand forecasts and resilient US product consumption, reinforcing expectations that 2H26 refining cash flows and buyback capacity will remain robust.

themes.name: Casual Dining Restaurant Turnaround
themes.description: The move appears to be tied to a broader restaurant-sector relief/upgrade theme, with analysts highlighting valuation and turnaround progress rather than a single company-specific headline. For CBRL specifically, Yahoo Finance notes “fair value rises” as analysts weigh turnaround progress, while the broader week has been dominated by earnings-season setup and consumer-spending read-throughs.

themes.name: AI Cloud GPU & Datacenter Colocation Platforms
themes.description: The main catalyst this week is a sector-wide squeeze in AI and data-infrastructure names following better-than-feared earnings and renewed buying in momentum baskets, which is pulling WYFI, SHAZ, and PENG higher together as part of that theme. In SHAZ’s case, traders are explicitly keying off “big catalysts, gap moves, and deep pullbacks” highlighted by momentum services, which has drawn in pattern and short-covering flows. WYFI and PENG are benefiting from sympathy and basket flows rather than stock-specific headlines, as investors reposition into higher-beta AI and infrastructure plays after the recent shakeout.

themes.name: Franchise Auto Dealership Groups
themes.description: PAG, SAH, and ABG are moving higher this week on a combination of sector momentum in auto retail and company-specific news, with Asbury Automotive (ABG) getting an added boost after being named to TIME’s “America’s Best Companies 2026” list, which improves its visibility and sentiment. PAG and SAH are largely following the broader positive read-through for franchise dealers ahead of Q2 earnings, as investors position for resilient unit volumes and stable margins. No single macro shock is driving the move; it’s primarily sentiment and positioning into upcoming results, with ABG’s recognition acting as the clearest discrete catalyst among the three.

themes.name: Non-US & Frontier Market Digital Financial Services Re-Rating
themes.description: PAYP on the Warsaw Stock Exchange has been moving higher this week primarily on a technical/valuation-driven rebound rather than a fresh, stock-specific news catalyst. The move is being framed by traders as a catch-up rally in beaten-down payment names like PayPal after prolonged underperformance and compressed multiples, aided by improving broader risk sentiment toward fintech and IT stocks. In other words, it’s momentum and sector rotation, not a new fundamental announcement from the company.

themes.name: Genomic Medicine & Synthetic DNA Tools
themes.description: MRNA’s move higher over the past month has been driven primarily by a strong non-COVID pipeline narrative and a key FDA advisory committee’s 9-0 vote in favor of its RSV vaccine for older adults, which boosted confidence in future revenue and broadened its story beyond COVID. TWST has been climbing on renewed interest in synthetic biology and genomics names as investors rotate into platform biotech stories with large potential TAM, aided by improving sentiment toward higher-risk growth and SMID-cap biotech. Both are benefiting from a broader rebound in select biotech driven by pipeline validation and risk-on equity flows.

themes.name: Molecular Diagnostics & DNA-Based Genetic Testing
themes.description: BLLN has been moving on continued post-IPO momentum and retail interest in small-cap biotech/AI names, but there is no single, stock-specific news release acting as a clear catalyst this week. WGS is trading higher on positive sentiment around genetic testing and precision medicine peers plus ongoing integration/turnaround expectations following its rebranding/history as GeneDx. QGEN’s strength is tied to the broader bid in diagnostics and life-science tools on improving volume trends and defensive growth positioning rather than a discrete company-specific headline.

themes.name: Specialty Catastrophe Property Insurance Underwriters
themes.description: There is no specific, stock-moving news catalyst on NP or SLDE this week; the move appears driven more by momentum/technicals and traders anticipating future catalysts than by a fresh fundamental announcement. Recent sector attention around defense/laser/advanced materials names (e.g., nLIGHT’s large JLWS defense contract win and bullish analyst reaction) is likely adding spillover enthusiasm to related tickers. In short, the strength looks sentiment- and sympathy-driven rather than tied to a discrete new company-specific headline.

themes.name: Corporate Spend, Expense Management & Financial Operations Software
themes.description: NAVN, WEX, and BILL are being bid up this week largely on expectations around upcoming earnings and improving sentiment toward payment/fintech names as rates peak and transaction volumes stay resilient. There is no single stock-specific headline driving the moves; instead, it’s a combination of rotation into profitable growth, short covering, and investors positioning ahead of Q2 prints.

themes.name: Emerging Market Digital Commerce & Fintech Platforms
themes.description: MELI is being driven higher mainly by Bank of America reiterating a Buy and highlighting accelerating Brazil growth plus the rapid expansion of MercadoLibre’s credit card business. The market is also reacting to the company’s strong operating momentum in Brazil, including 38% FX-neutral GMV growth and 56% items sold growth.

themes.name: Antibody-Based Oncology & Autoimmune Biologics
themes.description: GMAB is trading higher on continued enthusiasm around its novel antibody-drug conjugate pipeline and recent positive sell-side commentary highlighting upside to its partnered programs. ZYME is moving up on renewed interest in its bispecific antibody platform following recent data presentations and ongoing M&A/speculation in the antibody engineering space. ABVX strength is driven by biotech risk-on sentiment and positioning ahead of expected clinical and regulatory updates later this year.

themes.name: Office REIT Recovery & Re-Rating
themes.description: The move in HIW, CDP and SLG this week is being driven primarily by a continued bid into office REITs as a group on the back of improving sentiment around rate cuts and stabilization in key office markets, rather than a company-specific headline. Flows have rotated into higher-quality, well-leased office names, and traders are also positioning ahead of upcoming earnings as a potential catalyst for more constructive commentary on leasing, occupancy and balance sheets. There has not been a single discrete news item for these three tickers that clearly explains the move; it’s a sector and macro-driven rally.

themes.name: U.S. Passenger Airlines
themes.description: The main catalyst is a strong fundamental backdrop for U.S. airlines, led by Delta’s better‑than‑expected Q2 earnings and record revenue, which is lifting the broader group on read‑through. Lower fuel prices and still‑solid post-summer demand expectations are reinforcing the bid in full‑service and leisure carriers (DAL, UAL, ALK, LUV, ALGT, SKYW). There is also some catch‑up/rerating as investors rotate into cyclicals after the recent tech-led volatility.

themes.name: Insurance Brokerage & Distribution Services
themes.description: The primary catalyst is the ongoing bid speculation and market positioning around the Willis Towers Watson (WTW) process, which has refocused attention on the value of large insurance brokers and their potential consolidation/strategic alternatives. In that context, investors are rotating into the highest‑quality, scale brokerage names – AON, Arthur J. Gallagher (AJG), and Brown & Brown (BRO) – as relative winners from any industry rationalization, while also pricing in solid Q2 earnings momentum and resilient commercial P&C pricing. There is no single company‑specific headline this week; it’s a sector rerating driven by M&A/strategic optionality and expectations for strong upcoming prints.

themes.name: Specialty Insurance Underwriting & Brokerage
themes.description: The move appears to be driven by a broader rally in insurance/brokerage names rather than a single company-specific headline; the only provided source is an earnings calendar, which does not identify a catalyst for KNSL, RYAN, WTW, or PLMR. If you want, I can help pinpoint whether the driver is earnings, guidance, M&A, or a sector rotation.

themes.name: Cloud Observability & AIOps Monitoring Platforms
themes.description: DDOG is grinding higher on follow-through from its strong Q1 print and raised FY26 guidance, plus AI-focused product momentum including the Adaptive ML acquisition and features unveiled at the DASH 2026 conference. DT’s move is being driven by Starboard Value’s activist deal: new board seats, a $1 billion buyback authorization, and a commitment to a “Rule of 50” margin profile by FY2029, which has sharpened both turnaround and potential M&A narratives. Broader support from a constructive Fed/liquidity backdrop for growth software is an additional tailwind for both names.

themes.name: Truckload & LTL Freight Carriers
themes.description: The group is moving on a broad transportation/trucking rebound narrative: tightening truckload capacity from regulatory enforcement and carrier exits has pushed spot and contract rates higher, improving earnings leverage for asset-based and brokerage names. At the same time, intermodal volumes and pricing are inflecting up, diesel costs are rising (supporting contract repricing), and June freight and manufacturing data have been supportive for truckload and LTL demand, which collectively boosts sentiment across TFII, KNX, LSTR, SNDR, ARCB, and WERN.

themes.name: Property & Casualty Insurance Underwriters
themes.description: The move is being driven primarily by a sector-wide rotation into property & casualty insurers on the back of improving pricing, benign catastrophe loss experience, and rising expectations for better underwriting margins. There’s also anticipation of solid Q2 earnings and potential reserve releases/discipline across commercial lines, which is pulling capital into high-quality P&C names like TRV, WRB, CINF, ORI, HIG, and THG. Finally, stabilizing interest rate expectations are supporting their investment income outlook without materially increasing pressure on loss costs, reinforcing the bid in the group.

themes.name: Life Insurance & Annuity Providers
themes.description: The main catalyst this week is a broad move higher in U.S. life insurers on expectations that lower long‑term rates and tighter credit spreads will boost the value of their investment portfolios and reduce reserve pressure, coupled with steady macro data that support life and retirement product demand. Sector flows have been rotating into defensive financials after the recent volatility in cyclicals and tech, benefiting UNM, MET, PRU and PFG as a group rather than name‑specific news. There are no major, stock‑specific headlines for these four; the bid is primarily sector and factor driven.

themes.name: Automotive Electronics & Sensor Components
themes.description: The main catalyst is renewed interest in autonomous driving and ADAS suppliers after positive data and commentary from major EV and auto OEMs this week, which is pulling up lidar names like Ouster (OUST) and Aeva (AEVA) and related sensing/analog plays like Allegro MicroSystems (ALGM) and Methode Electronics (MEI). There is also some rotation into beaten‑down small/mid-cap semis and auto-supplier tech ahead of upcoming earnings, with short covering amplifying the move. I am not seeing a stock-specific, company-only headline for any of the four; the move appears sector- and theme-driven.

themes.name: European Bank Stocks
themes.description: The move is being driven mainly by renewed strength in European/U.K. banks on expectations of better net interest income and capital returns, not a single company-specific headline. For LYG specifically, Lloyds-related coverage this week points to ongoing structural hedge tailwinds and UK bank policy talk; BBVA, SAN, BCS, and NWG are being lifted by the same sector rotation into large-cap European financials.

themes.name: U.S. Regional & Mid-Cap Commercial Banks
themes.description: The move in regional banks like TFC, EWBC, FITB, WSFS, PNC, and CFG this week is being driven primarily by a drop in Treasury yields and a risk-on shift after softer economic data reduced immediate Fed rate hike fears, supporting financials broadly. There is also growing speculation that a more benign credit environment and stable consumer metrics will prevent the kind of credit stress that had been priced into regional banks earlier in the year. Additionally, some investors are positioning ahead of the upcoming earnings season for regional banks, expecting management commentary to confirm improving funding costs and deposit stability.

themes.name: B2B Digital Financial Infrastructure & Payment Rails Modernization
themes.description: The main catalyst driving ALKT higher this week is the activist involvement of JANA Partners, which recently disclosed a 6.3% stake and is publicly pressing Alkami’s board to explore a potential sale and governance changes. JANA’s campaign has sharpened investor focus on a takeout scenario and value-unlock roadmap, fueling short-term upside momentum off a previously weak longer-term base. Additional support comes from continued positive MANTL/Alkami client-deposit case studies that reinforce the growth narrative, but the activist sale push is the dominant driver.

themes.name: Wealth Management & Retail Brokerage Platforms
themes.description: The main catalyst this week is a sharp, broad-based bid into the wealth/asset management complex as investors rotate into interest-rate-sensitive financials on growing expectations of Fed easing and a steeper yield curve, which improves cash spreads and advisory flows. In addition, several brokers and asset managers have seen positive sell-side commentary and target raises tying them to resilient retail flows and record market levels, boosting sentiment across the group. There are no stock-specific blockbuster headlines; this is largely a sector-driven move.

themes.name: Semiconductor Wafer Foundry & Advanced IC Manufacturing
themes.description: The group is moving higher on renewed optimism around AI-driven wafer fab equipment demand and expectations that upcoming Q2 earnings from semi-cap equipment names will show stronger orders and improving visibility into 2026 capex. In addition, several recent buy-side notes have highlighted upside risk to consensus for front‑end tools and specialty consumables as foundries and leading-edge logic customers accelerate node transitions. There is no single stock-specific headline; the catalyst is broadly sector‑wide AI infrastructure spending and capex upgrade themes.

themes.name: Custom AI Silicon & Chip Architecture Licensing
themes.description: These names are being bid up on continued enthusiasm around AI and high‑speed networking, with investors rotating into semiconductor and IP suppliers leveraged to data center, cloud, and custom accelerators. The move is being reinforced by strong order commentary from hyperscale customers, recent AI‑related product announcements, and analysts raising estimates and price targets across the group. There’s also a broader sector tailwind as traders position ahead of upcoming earnings as a way to play upside to AI demand.

themes.name: AI Memory & Storage
themes.description: The move higher in SNDK, MU, WDC, STX and SIMO this week is being driven primarily by the ongoing AI-driven memory and storage “supercycle,” with investors rotating back into data center infrastructure plays after recent volatility and recognizing sustained demand for DRAM, HBM, NAND and enterprise storage tied to AI buildouts. Sector commentary highlights surging AI data center capex, record Micron HBM demand, and SanDisk’s pure‑play NAND/enterprise SSD growth, which is pulling the broader memory/storage complex higher as a group. In the background, analyst upgrades and rising conviction that the AI spending cycle extends into 2027 are reinforcing the bid in these names.

themes.name: Private Mortgage Insurance
themes.description: The primary catalyst is renewed optimism around U.S. housing and mortgage credit performance, with stable-to-improving delinquency trends and continued strength in purchase mortgage volumes supporting the fundamental backdrop for the private mortgage insurers. In addition, falling credit spreads and a bid into financials tied to housing/consumer credit are boosting sentiment toward the group, while these names still trade at modest multiples to book and earnings, attracting value and cyclical buyers. There has been no stock-specific breaking news this week; the move is largely macro- and sector-driven.

themes.name: Coastal & Suburban Residential Rental REITs
themes.description: The move in AVB, AMH, INVH, EQR, and ESS this week is being driven primarily by a broad bid into U.S. residential REITs as investors rotate back into defensives with stable cash flows amid geopolitical and macro uncertainty, alongside a V‑shaped recovery in equities and strong Q1 earnings that have improved risk sentiment for income‑oriented sectors. Expectations for solid upcoming earnings prints in the group (e.g., INVH’s Q2 release later this month) and continued revenue/margin growth in U.S. real estate are reinforcing the bid. Sector flows rather than name‑specific headlines are the dominant catalyst.

themes.name: Industrial & Logistics REITs
themes.description: FR, TRNO, and EGP are moving higher this week primarily on the back of bullish sell-side action, with fresh price target raises on all three names on July 8 that highlighted attractive valuation and solid industrial fundamentals. In the background, the broader equity REIT backdrop has turned more constructive, with REITWeek commentary pointing to above-trend earnings growth and strengthening fundamentals across industrial/logistics, which supports multiple expansion. Company-specific news has been incremental rather than transformative, so the main near-term catalyst is the repricing following these target hikes within a stronger sector narrative.

themes.name: Multifamily Apartment REITs
themes.description: The move in CPT, UDR, and IRT appears to be more sector-driven than company-specific, with apartment REITs catching a bid after Scotiabank raised its Camden Property Trust price target to $109, which helped reinforce a generally constructive tone for the group. I don’t see a clear new operating catalyst for UDR or IRT in the provided items, so the likely driver is broader apartment REIT momentum and analyst sentiment rather than a single fundamental headline.

themes.name: Open-Air & Strip Mall Retail REITs
themes.description: The move in KIM, KRG, UE, SKT, and PECO this week is primarily driven by a sector-wide bid into high-quality shopping center REITs as investors rotate back into defensive, yield-oriented real estate ahead of potential Fed easing. Flows are being reinforced by better-than-feared recent leasing/foot-traffic data across open-air centers and buy-side positioning that had been underweight the group, creating a short-covering/underweight-covering dynamic. There is no single company-specific headline; it’s a thematic trade into strip-center REITs on improving fundamentals and rate expectations.

themes.name: Senior Living & Housing Operators
themes.description: BKD is trading higher on the back of its June financing announcement, where Brookdale extended 2027 non‑recourse mortgage maturities, secured $188M in new Freddie Mac loans, repaid $200M of mortgage debt, and expanded its credit facility to $200M maturing in 2029. This materially improves liquidity and pushes out debt maturities, which is supportive for the equity and out-of-the-money calls like JAN. Broader strength in healthcare and senior living names alongside improving occupancy trends this year is likely adding incremental support.

themes.name: Senior & Long-Term Care Healthcare REITs
themes.description: The move across senior housing/healthcare REITs this week is being driven mainly by a sector-wide bid on the “defensive yield + aging demographics” theme as rates drift lower and investors rotate back into higher-dividend, non-cyclical real estate. There hasn’t been a stock-specific headline for SBRA, VTR, OHI, NHI, or LTC; instead, the catalyst is broader REIT factor buying and short-covering in healthcare names ahead of the next earnings cycle. In other words, it’s a macro/rates and positioning story, not a company-level news event.

themes.name: Golf Equipment & Apparel
themes.description: The main catalyst this week for Callaway Golf (CALY) is a wave of bullish analyst activity, including target price hikes toward the 19 area and positive relative performance commentary versus other consumer discretionary names. Shares have broken to new 52-week highs on momentum as investors chase year-to-date outperformance of roughly 50–55%, rather than reacting to any specific company news or fundamental headline. In short, it’s an analyst- and momentum-driven move, not a discrete news event.

themes.name: Government-Funded Senior & Post-Acute Care Re-Rating
themes.description: ALHC (Alignment Healthcare) and AVAH (Aveanna Healthcare) do not appear to have a specific company‑level news catalyst this week; the move looks tied to broader strength in value and small/mid‑cap healthcare services names as investors rotate into defensive, managed-care and home health exposure. Both benefit from the current bid into Medicare/Medicaid and home‑care reimbursement plays as rates stabilize and election‑related policy risk is being repriced more favorably. The rally is more sector‑flow driven than headline‑driven.

themes.name: Satellite Communications & Space Data Services
themes.description: VSAT, SPIR, and MDA are being driven higher this week by a broad space sector risk-on move tied to major catalysts: Rocket Lab’s acquisition of Iridium, the surge in space-focused ETFs like Tema Space Innovators (NASA), and heightened investor focus on the upcoming SpaceX listing as a valuation and liquidity benchmark for the sector. These names are key components or peers in the “new space” infrastructure and services theme, so they are catching flows and momentum as capital rotates into space stocks.

themes.name: Quantum Computing & Quantum-Safe Networking
themes.description: QNT and ARQQ are moving higher this week on the back of a broad quantum-computing sector re‑rating following Quantinuum’s recent Nasdaq listing and growing media focus on “next wave” quantum names, which has pulled in speculative and thematic ETF money into the group. In particular, QNT is benefiting from its high-profile IPO and leadership narrative in logical qubits, while ARQQ is catching sympathy flows as investors look for liquid quantum encryption exposure within the same theme. Macro catalysts like the upcoming Fed meeting and ongoing AI/infrastructure hype are adding a supportive risk-on backdrop for high-beta tech, amplifying those sector-specific moves.

themes.name: Bitcoin Mining & Crypto Infrastructure Operators
themes.description: The main catalyst pushing CIFR, CORZ, HUT, IREN, and WULF higher this week is renewed focus on bitcoin miners as scarce, large-scale power and data-center landlords for the AI compute boom, with investors re-rating those that have significant contracted or negotiable power capacity and AI/HPC pivot plans. VanEck’s widely circulated note on a $50B+ funding gap and market preference for miners with real leases and anchor AI deals (highlighting CIFR, HUT, WULF, CORZ, and IREN as better positioned) has sharpened that narrative and driven flows into the group. This is layered on top of ongoing bitcoin price resilience and deal flow headlines (new leases, capacity expansions, and tenant negotiations) that reinforce the AI-infrastructure thesis for these names.

themes.name: Life Science Tools & Analytical Instruments
themes.description: The move in DHR, MTD, TMO, and WAT this week is largely a continuation of the broader bid into research tools/consumables and life science instrumentation following strong Q1 prints and guidance across the group. The space has been in a sustained uptrend (average stock up ~20%+ since earnings), with investors rotating back into high-quality defensives as geopolitical risk fades, rates stabilize, and cyclicality in capex and biopharma spend looks less threatening. There is no single company-specific headline this week; it’s more a sector re‑rating and macro-driven risk-on rotation into high-margin, oligopolistic lab tools names.

themes.name: AI Data Center Power Infrastructure
themes.description: BE is likely being lifted by the AI/data-center power trade, with demand for on-site fuel-cell power and the large Wyoming data-center buildout cited as key catalysts. VRT is moving with the same AI infrastructure/critical power theme, while AGX appears to be reacting more to a disclosed congressional stock purchase than a broad industry catalyst. FCEL is also benefiting from the fuel-cell/data-center power narrative, but the move is smaller and looks more thematic than company-specific.

themes.name: Oilfield Pressure Pumping & Completion Services
themes.description: Hydraulic fracturing and oilfield completion service companies are correlating tightly at 0.81 as E&P operators maintain completion activity despite oil price softness, driven by efficiency gains and contract backlogs. This sub-sector of oilfield services is more levered to completion intensity (frac stages per well) than rig count, insulating it from pure drilling slowdowns. Any uptick in commodity prices or permitting activity would accelerate the group.

themes.name: Commercial Real Estate Brokerage & Advisory Services
themes.description: The likely catalyst is a broker/analyst upgrade wave in real estate services, with Yahoo Finance showing target-price raises for CWK and JLL this week. That kind of sector-wide analyst optimism can also lift CBRE and NMRK as peers even if the specific note names only a subset directly.

themes.name: Pure-Play Quantum Computing Hardware
themes.description: The move in IONQ, QUBT, RGTI and SKYT this week is being driven by renewed speculative interest in the quantum computing theme, helped by social/media chatter and influencer coverage around “four quantum stocks” and the broader Nasdaq/AI rebound rather than a specific fundamental company headline. There is also some rotation back into high‑beta, future‑tech names after recent underperformance, with traders leaning into the quantum narrative alongside AI and semiconductor strength. No single stock-specific catalyst (earnings, major contract, or regulatory event) appears to be the primary driver; it’s largely thematic momentum and sentiment.

themes.name: Self-Storage REITs
themes.description: The move in PSA, EXR, CUBE, and NSA this week is being driven primarily by a sector-wide bid into REITs as investors rotate back into defensive, income-oriented names alongside falling long-term yields and growing expectations for Fed rate cuts. Self‑storage is getting incremental support from stabilizing fundamentals commentary and relative outperformance vs. other property types, so capital is gravitating to the higher‑quality, large‑cap platforms. There is no single company‑specific headline; it’s a macro rates/REIT re‑risking catalyst with a self‑storage tilt.

themes.name: Large-Cap U.S. Defense Primes
themes.description: The main catalyst is a broad repricing of the defense/space complex after analysts have characterized this as the “best-in-a-generation” time to buy space and defense stocks, driving inflows into prime contractors like HII, LHX, LMT, and NOC. That call is reinforcing year-to-date strength and momentum in these names as investors rotate toward defense spending, military modernization, and geopolitically linked beneficiaries. Company-specific contract wins and solid earnings prints are secondary supports, but the sector-level analyst narrative is the primary driver this week.

themes.name: Alternative Asset Management Platforms
themes.description: Large alternative asset managers are moving together as fee-earning AUM growth from retail/wealth channel democratization (evergreen funds, 401k access) and infrastructure/private credit deployment drives earnings upgrades. The group benefits from rising management fees on locked-up capital that is less sensitive to market volatility than traditional active managers. Institutional appetite for private markets continues to expand, lengthening the runway for AUM compounding.

themes.name: Clinical-Stage Autoimmune & Inflammatory Disease Therapeutics
themes.description: Auto-retired 2026-07-10: dropped during merge/absorption (no successor found) (prior stage Nascent, 5 tickers).

themes.name: Clinical-Stage Oncology Drug Development
themes.description: Auto-retired 2026-07-10: absorbed/superseded by 'Oncology Targeted Therapy Biotechs' (prior stage Nascent, 6 tickers).

themes.description: Auto-retired 2026-07-10: dropped during merge/absorption (no successor found) (prior stage Fading, 2 tickers).

themes.description: Auto-retired 2026-07-10: dropped during merge/absorption (no successor found) (prior stage Nascent, 5 tickers).

themes.description: Auto-retired 2026-07-10: dropped during merge/absorption (no successor found) (prior stage Fading, 2 tickers).

themes.name: Rare Cardiometabolic & Endocrine Specialty Pharma
themes.description: Auto-retired 2026-07-10: dropped during merge/absorption (no successor found) (prior stage Nascent, 4 tickers).

themes.name: Rare Disease Small Molecule Precision Therapeutics
themes.description: Auto-retired 2026-07-10: dropped during merge/absorption (no successor found) (prior stage Nascent, 3 tickers).

themes.description: Auto-retired 2026-07-10: dropped during merge/absorption (no successor found) (prior stage Nascent, 6 tickers).

themes.name: Semiconductor Probe Card & Front-End Test Equipment
themes.description: Auto-retired 2026-07-10: dropped during merge/absorption (no successor found) (prior stage Fading, 2 tickers).

themes.name: In Vivo Gene & Engineered Cell Therapy Clinical Re-Rating
themes.description: A cluster of clinical-stage companies using genetic delivery modalities (in vivo AAV gene therapy, CAR-T, engineered cell platforms) are accelerating together as multi-program data readouts and regulatory momentum de-risk the therapeutic modality itself — distinct from the tools/sequencing 'Genomic Medicine' theme and from small-molecule rare-disease plays.

themes.name: Network Security & Zero-Trust Edge
themes.description: The main catalyst pushing BB, TENB, CRWD, QLYS, RBRK, and PANW higher this week is a broad bid into cybersecurity driven by accelerating AI-related threat activity and growing conviction that security spending is resilient and non-discretionary in the current IT budget cycle. For PANW and CRWD specifically, recent blockbuster quarters, strong AI-native product momentum, and a series of bullish analyst target hikes have reinforced the sector’s rerating and pulled peers higher via read-through. Rubrik and other names are also benefiting from the same “AI-era cybersecurity” narrative and platform-consolidation theme, which is attracting incremental institutional flows into the group.

themes.name: Online Insurance Comparison & Digital Marketplace Platforms
themes.description: The move higher in MAX Ever this week is being driven primarily by renewed optimism around MediaAlpha (ticker MAX) as investors focus on its sharp fundamental rebound and rising Street expectations. Recent commentary has highlighted the company’s 2024 revenue growth of over 120%, a swing back to profitability, and a strong recovery in P&C insurance advertising demand, which has led to the stock setting new 12‑month highs and prompted at least one target price increase to $17. In short, the catalyst is a combination of strong reported results, improved industry conditions, and fresh positive sell‑side attention.

themes.name: Traditional Staffing & Talent Placement Services
themes.description: MAN RHI is being driven higher mainly by anticipation of upcoming earnings and AI-related positioning. ManpowerGroup has its Q2 2026 earnings webcast scheduled for July 16, which is acting as a near-term catalyst, alongside a cluster of positive news on its role in AI-enabled workforce solutions and new product launches. Investors are positioning for an inflection in hiring and talent demand, with AI themes amplifying sentiment in the name this week.

themes.name: SMB & Workforce Business Services Platforms
themes.description: The move in NSP appears tied mainly to notable insider buying activity, with one insider accumulating roughly $10.8M of stock over the past 60 days, which is being read as a “smart money” confidence signal. LZ’s strength this week looks more thematic, trading as a small-cap growth name benefiting from a broader bid into SaaS/legal-tech and speculative AI-adjacent plays rather than a specific company headline. There is no clear, stock-specific bullish news catalyst for either beyond positioning and sentiment.

themes.name: Casual Dining Restaurant Turnaround
themes.description: CBRL is trading higher this week primarily on follow-through from its recent earnings beat and raised FY26 revenue and EBITDA outlook, which reinforced the credibility of its turnaround story and margin improvement plans. In the last few days, multiple analysts have raised fair value estimates and price targets (including upgrades and target hikes from firms like Argus, Wells Fargo, UBS and others), adding a fresh positive valuation catalyst and highlighting high short interest as an additional technical tailwind. The announced $0.25 quarterly dividend and ongoing corporate restructuring aimed at cutting G&A and advertising expenses are secondary supports to the move.

themes.name: Peptide & Hormone Therapies for Metabolic & Endocrine Disorders
themes.description: VKTX is being driven by renewed obesity pipeline momentum, specifically initiation of the Phase 1 VK3019 obesity trial and a cluster of bullish analyst reiterations around VK2735 maintenance data as a key upcoming catalyst. For MBX, ASND, PTGX, XERS, and CORT, the move is largely sector- and factor-driven: small/mid-cap biotech and specialty pharma are catching flows on risk-on sentiment and recent positive data/regulatory milestones in their respective pipelines (e.g., late‑stage/near‑commercial assets and FDA review timelines), rather than a single stock‑specific headline this week.

themes.name: U.S. Petroleum Refining & Downstream Processing
themes.description: The main catalyst is a refining-margin rally: higher fuel demand, elevated crack spreads, and tight global product supplies are boosting downstream refiners like DINO, MPC, PSX, PBF, VLO, and DK. Geopolitical disruptions in the Middle East and related shipping/commodity disruptions have also helped widen crack spreads and lift the group. For MPC specifically, fresh analyst upside has added support, with Jefferies raising its price target ahead of earnings.

themes.name: Digital Advertising Technology Platforms
themes.description: The main catalyst pushing Magnite (MGNI) higher this week is growing investor optimism around its recent and expanding strategic partnerships, particularly the Walmart Connect retail media integration, plus ongoing CTV and commerce-media wins. RBC Capital reiterated an Outperform rating and highlighted the Walmart Connect expansion as a meaningful positive for Magnite’s positioning and sentiment, while BTIG’s recent Buy initiation and strong CTV growth and AI/mediation product updates are reinforcing the bullish narrative. In short, the tape is reacting to the combination of retail media/CTV partnership momentum, supportive analyst coverage, and a broader recognition of Magnite as a differentiated CTV and commerce-media SSP.

themes.name: Digital Insurance Distribution & Marketplace Platforms
themes.description: For GSHD, the main near-term catalyst is the July 10 announcement that Goosehead was named to Selling Power’s “60 Best Companies to Sell For 2026” list, which has highlighted its strong sales culture and growth trajectory to momentum and quality-focused investors. For PRCH, the stock is being driven higher by a cluster of recent positive company-specific developments: strong Q1 2026 results, launch of its insurance offering in Michigan, and incremental buyback-related news from its purchase of PRCH shares from the Reciprocal, all reinforcing the housing/insurance growth narrative as rate-cut expectations support the sector.

themes.name: Consumer Fintech & Digital Credit Platforms
themes.description: DAVE is being driven higher by a combination of strong Q1 fundamentals (47% revenue growth, 57% adjusted EBITDA growth) and a raised full-year outlook, which has reinforced the bull case and led to upward earnings estimate revisions. Additional momentum is coming from the new Coastal Community Bank funding arrangement, which improves capital efficiency and unlocks significant liquidity, plus inclusion in the S&P SmallCap 600 and expanded share repurchase authorization that are attracting incremental institutional and index-demand flows. SEZL’s move is more sentiment- and momentum-driven, with the stock still riding a sharp multi-month rerating in BNPL, supported by continued fundamental strength, even as recent analyst downgrades on valuation highlight growing scrutiny rather than a clear new positive catalyst this week.

themes.name: Genomic Medicine & Synthetic DNA Tools
themes.description: MRNA has been driven higher primarily by follow-through from its recent Science Day, where management laid out a broader mRNA platform strategy beyond vaccines, highlighting new oncology and autoimmune programs that expanded long-term pipeline and revenue expectations. TWST’s strength is largely an extension of its post-Analyst Day re-rating, with Evercore ISI lifting its price target to $102 on July 6 and emphasizing AI-driven order momentum and improving growth/margin outlook in life science tools. Both moves are more about evolving medium-term narratives and analyst positioning than a single, discrete headline this week.

themes.name: Insurance Brokerage & Distribution Services
themes.description: The main catalyst this week is positive fundamental and corporate news across the insurance brokerage group: AJG is getting bid on continued organic and acquisition-led growth plus fresh price target hikes and Canada expansion headlines, with BRO moving in tandem given its high historical correlation and similar M&A-driven growth profile, while AON is supported by strong recent earnings, a large new buyback authorization and broadly positive analyst sentiment. Together, these reinforce the sector’s earnings visibility, capital return and consolidation story, attracting incremental institutional flows into the group.

themes.name: Emerging Market Digital Commerce & Fintech Platforms
themes.description: For MELI, the main near-term catalyst is its announcement of a new distribution center hub in Nuevo León, Mexico, which is boosting the logistics growth narrative and drawing fresh institutional interest. More broadly, the move up this week is being reinforced by investors revisiting the company’s strong Q1 growth metrics and AI/fintech expansion story ahead of its early‑August earnings, after a prior post‑earnings selloff left the stock looking underearning and undervalued. For SE, the recent strength is tied to renewed buying in emerging‑market consumer and e-commerce names, with Sea trading higher alongside Brazilian and LatAm platforms as capital rotates back into beaten‑down growth and digital commerce plays.

themes.name: Targeted Protein Degradation Oncology
themes.description: KYMR is being driven mainly by Kymera’s early completion of enrollment in the KT-621 BROADEN2 Phase 2b atopic dermatitis trial, which pulled topline data forward to year-end 2026 from mid-2027. Analysts also raised price targets on the faster timeline, reinforcing the move.

themes.name: Corporate Spend, Expense Management & Financial Operations Software
themes.description: NAVN is being driven higher this week primarily by its launch of the Model Context Protocol (MCP) for travel and expense management, which materially strengthens its AI positioning in corporate travel and spend. That catalyst is reinforced by strong Q1/FY27 fundamentals (40% revenue growth, raised full-year guidance) and ongoing enterprise wins, which are boosting confidence in its long-term growth and margin profile. WEX and BILL are moving more on sector sympathy and broader fintech/payment strength rather than a specific, stock‑unique news catalyst comparable to NAVN’s MCP launch.

themes.name: Specialty Catastrophe Property Insurance Underwriters
themes.description: The move in SLDE and NP this week appears to be driven by stock-specific fundamentals and sentiment rather than a single, fresh headline catalyst. For Slide Insurance (SLDE), the key ongoing drivers are the April entry into New Jersey, planned E&S product launches in California in 1H26, and strong Q1 policy growth tied to Florida Citizens depopulation, all of which have reinforced a positive “moderate buy” analyst stance and rising target prices. For Neptune Insurance (NP), the main underpinning is its higher 2026 revenue guidance and solid growth outlook, which has kept buyers engaged even without a new discrete news release this week.

themes.name: Cloud Observability & AIOps Monitoring Platforms
themes.description: DDOG is being driven higher by continued AI-driven momentum following its blowout Q1 print above $1B in revenue, raised 2026 guidance, and the strong product/AI narrative out of the June DASH 2026 conference and Adaptive ML acquisition. Investor focus is on Datadog’s expanding AI observability stack, large AI lab contracts, and ongoing analyst target hikes, rather than any single new headline this week. DT is benefiting from the sector read‑through and renewed interest in observability/AIOps names, plus the Starboard Value activism at Dynatrace that added board members and a $1B buyback, boosting sentiment across the space.

themes.name: Specialty Insurance Underwriting & Brokerage
themes.description: RYAN is being supported by recent price target increases from Wells Fargo and Mizuho, which have improved sentiment despite a Wolfe Research downgrade and valuation concerns. WTW’s strength is mostly part of the broader bid for high‑quality insurance and brokerage names this week, with no single stock‑specific catalyst dominating. PLMR is moving higher on the back of a strong Q1 beat, raised 2026 adjusted net income guidance, expanded reinsurance coverage, and favorable commentary that it remains undervalued, while KNSL is benefiting from solid fundamentals, a strong E&S market backdrop, and technical momentum after trading back above its 50‑day moving average despite earlier downgrades.

themes.name: Oilfield Pressure Pumping & Completion Services
themes.description: Hydraulic fracturing and oilfield completion service companies are correlating tightly at 0.81 as E&P operators maintain completion activity despite oil price softness, driven by efficiency gains and contract backlogs. This sub-sector of oilfield services is more levered to completion intensity (frac stages per well) than rig count, insulating it from pure drilling slowdowns. Any uptick in commodity prices or permitting activity would accelerate the group.

themes.name: Franchise Auto Dealership Groups
themes.description: ABG, PAG, and SAH are being bid up primarily on sector-wide optimism around auto retailers’ growth prospects, including accelerating online/digital sales, ongoing acquisition-driven consolidation, and resilient demand (including EV) supporting earnings and cash flow. There is also incremental attention on Asbury after being named to TIME’s America’s Best Companies list and receiving a price target hike from UBS, which is helping sentiment across the group. Broader macro drivers (oil, CPI, Fed) are not stock-specific but are contributing to a rotation into more cyclical/value names like auto dealers this week.

themes.name: Molecular Diagnostics & DNA-Based Genetic Testing
themes.description: QGEN is being driven higher by renewed takeover speculation, with reports that private equity firms including EQT, Advent and KKR are exploring a potential acquisition and recent 13G filings highlighting notable institutional stakes. WGS (GeneDx) is moving up on technical momentum — a bull flag breakout following a multi‑month double bottom, strong recent percentage gains, and broader bullish sentiment toward precision medicine and genomic diagnostics rather than a specific new fundamental catalyst. I do not see a discrete news catalyst for BLLN this week; its strength appears more flow/technically driven, likely tied to speculative interest and sector momentum rather than a headline event.

themes.name: Office REIT Recovery & Re-Rating
themes.description: The move in HIW, CDP, and SLG this week is being driven mainly by improving sentiment around office REIT fundamentals and leasing, plus positioning ahead of SL Green’s July 22 Q2 earnings release and upgraded FY 2026 EPS guidance. SLG in particular has strong recent leasing metrics, a guidance upgrade well above prior consensus, and an asset sale at 7 Dey Street that together reinforce the “recovery in prime office” narrative for the group. Analyst price target hikes (e.g., Ladenburg nudging SLG’s target higher) are adding incremental support to the trade.

themes.name: Truckload & LTL Freight Carriers
themes.description: The group is moving higher on renewed optimism around the trucking cycle and improving freight fundamentals, with spot TL rates and intermodal volumes inflecting higher as excess capacity exits and diesel prices rise. In parallel, analysts have been revising earnings estimates and price targets upward for truckload names like KNX and LSTR, reinforcing a sentiment shift toward better 2H26/2027 profitability. More broadly, investors are rotating into economically sensitive transport stocks as macro data and nearshoring trends support a firmer outlook for U.S.–Mexico and domestic freight demand.

themes.name: Property & Casualty Insurance Underwriters
themes.description: LMND is being driven higher by its high-profile Tesla autonomous-driving insurance partnership, including plans to significantly cut insurance rates for miles driven on FSD, plus follow-through from a recent short-squeeze/strong quarter narrative. For AFG, AXS, WRB, TRV, and SKWD, the move is largely sectoral: property‑casualty/ specialty insurers are in favor on expectations of still-firm commercial pricing and strong underwriting margins, with SKWD additionally benefiting from continued momentum after a blowout Q1 2026 print and bullish analyst targets tied to the Apollo acquisition.

themes.name: Automotive Electronics & Sensor Components
themes.description: OUST and AEVA are being driven higher by the broader “physical AI” lidar theme, with Ouster’s Rev8 platform gaining Build America, Buy America compliance and landing multiple robotics, infrastructure, and defense partnerships that have pulled the whole lidar complex up. AEVA specifically has tailwinds from its strategic Bendix partnership for Class 8 truck active safety and prior high-profile design wins with NVIDIA DRIVE Hyperion and major OEM programs, reinforcing its role in production ADAS and autonomy. MEI is likely moving in sympathy as part of the same advanced sensing/automation basket rather than on a distinct, stock-specific catalyst.

themes.name: Antibody-Based Oncology & Autoimmune Biologics
themes.description: GMAB is moving higher on positive Phase 3 data for epcoritamab plus lenalidomide in relapsed/refractory DLBCL, along with European Commission approval for epcoritamab-based regimens and incremental pipeline/BD headlines including the Merus acquisition. ZYME is being bid up on anticipation of the August 25 PDUFA for zanidatamab in first-line HER2+ GEA and the associated Jazz milestone/royalty upside, following strong HERIZON-GEA-01 Phase 3 data. ABVX is rallying on the recent positive Phase 3 ulcerative colitis data for obefazimod and the successful $920m offering, which together have reset expectations around funding, de‑risking of the lead asset, and potential strategic interest.

themes.name: AI Cloud GPU & Datacenter Colocation Platforms
themes.description: PENG is being driven higher by the announcement that Penguin Solutions was named an Nvidia AI Factory Specialized Partner, putting it on Nvidia’s short list of full‑stack AI infrastructure builders. WYFI is rallying on WhiteFiber’s July 9 release of initial R&D results for its cross‑data‑center distributed GPU supercluster, hitting 111.2 Tbps bandwidth at 0.9 ms latency and reinforcing its AI infrastructure story.

themes.name: B2B Digital Financial Infrastructure & Payment Rails Modernization
themes.description: QTWO is being helped by its recent inclusion in the Russell 2000 Defensive and Russell 2000 Growth‑Defensive indexes, which is driving index-related demand and improving visibility with institutional investors. PAY is moving on rising expectations around its next earnings report as the key near-term catalyst. ALKT is trading higher on activist pressure from Jana Partners pushing the company to explore a potential sale and governance changes, which has refocused investors on strategic value and optionality.

themes.name: Life Science Tools & Analytical Instruments
themes.description: The primary catalyst this week is renewed positive sentiment around life science tools heading into late-July Q2 earnings, with investors positioning ahead of expected bioprocessing and biopharma strength for Danaher, Thermo Fisher and peers. For TMO specifically, Deutsche Bank’s short-term “Catalyst call: Buy” and ongoing enthusiasm around the Clario acquisition and mRNA/biopharma exposure are supporting the move. WAT is benefitting from follow‑through after Barclays’ recent upgrade and raised target tied to data center‑related growth in its water technologies portfolio.

themes.name: Life Insurance & Annuity Providers
themes.description: The main catalyst is Unum’s second long-term care (LTC) risk-transfer deal, which prompted UBS to raise UNM’s price target and highlighted successful legacy runoff and capital deployment in life/health insurers. That transaction is improving sentiment toward the broader life/health complex and “second-order” beneficiaries with excess capital and legacy cleanup optionality, including MET, PRU, and PFG. In short, the UNM LTC de-risking and associated buyback/capital return narrative is lifting the whole group.

themes.name: Orthopedic & Surgical Device Recovery
themes.description: CNMD is trading higher on news that Conmed is formally exploring strategic alternatives, including a potential sale to private equity, after Bloomberg reported takeover interest and the stock jumped about 10% in after-hours trading. SYK’s strength this week is driven by improving sentiment around recovery from its March cyberattack, with investors positioning for deferred revenue to be recaptured in 2H 2026 and multiple sell-side upgrades/price target raises (notably BMO and Argus) reinforcing the medtech rebound narrative.

themes.name: U.S. Passenger Airlines
themes.description: The main catalyst is a sharp drop in crude oil prices, which lowers jet fuel costs and boosts airline margins, lifting the whole group higher this week. Airlines are also getting a tailwind from strong summer travel demand and a positive pre-earnings setup, with United and Delta in focus ahead of upcoming results.

themes.name: Wealth Management & Retail Brokerage Platforms
themes.description: The main catalyst looks like a broad wealth-management/rate-sensitive rally, with investors favoring firms tied to stable rates, stronger client activity, and improving fee income. For SCHW and LPLA, the lift is being driven by strong trading/activity trends and upbeat outlooks on client assets and advisory flows; for RJF and SEIC, it’s more about analyst optimism and sector strength than a single company-specific headline. AMP and RJF also appear to be getting some support from recent price-target/analyst action rather than a fresh event-specific news item.

themes.name: U.S. Regional & Mid-Cap Commercial Banks
themes.description: The main catalyst is a broad bid into U.S. regional banks off improving net interest income leverage as long‑term Treasury yields move higher, which supports bank earnings power and makes the sector relatively more attractive than short‑duration fixed income. For individual names, recent strong earnings and guidance (e.g., PNC’s net interest income growth and raised 2026 outlook; Glacier Bancorp’s record Q1 profit and wider NIM) plus consistent dividend strength are reinforcing the move. Positioning ahead of upcoming Q2 results for regionals is also contributing to the bid as investors recalibrate expectations for credit quality and margin resilience.

themes.name: Private Mortgage Insurance
themes.description: The move is most likely being driven by a broader rally in mortgage insurers on improving sentiment around earnings and valuation, with ESNT specifically getting a constructive short-term boost from a buy-rated earnings outlook and RDN hitting a 52-week high after a string of earnings beats. I don’t see evidence of a single company-specific headline tying all four names together, so this looks more like sector rotation than one shared catalyst.

themes.name: Coastal & Suburban Residential Rental REITs
themes.description: The main catalyst is a sector-wide bid into residential REITs on the back of a “rates lower for longer” narrative and renewed demand for defensive, income-oriented names as macro and geopolitical risks stay elevated. Within that, AVB and EQR are getting an extra lift from their recently announced all-stock merger of equals, which creates a $50B+ multifamily REIT platform and is being framed as strategically and financially accretive. AMH and INVH are also benefiting from the same housing-supply constraints and strong rental-demand theme that continues to support fundamentals and credit markets for single‑family rental REITs.

themes.name: Inflammatory Disease & Immunology Biologics
themes.description: ORKA is being driven higher by the Halozyme collaboration on ORKA-001, which validated the program and improved the drug’s delivery profile. The move was also supported by Oruka’s $700 million follow-on offering and recent analyst upgrades.

themes.name: Semiconductor Wafer Foundry & Advanced IC Manufacturing
themes.description: The group is trading higher on a combination of bullish Wall Street research calling for a wafer fab equipment “supercycle” driven by AI-capex, and a cluster of recent price-target hikes across the semi-cap equipment complex (KLAC, AMAT, LRCX, etc.). That’s reinforced by improving memory and HBM capex signals from Samsung/SK Hynix and expectations for a 2027 AI build-out cycle, which directly benefits core WFE suppliers like AMAT, LRCX, KLAC, MKSI, ACMR and UCTT. There is also an element of short-covering/dip-buying after last week’s sector selloff, with investors rotating back into AI-levered equipment names ahead of upcoming earnings.

themes.name: Custom AI Silicon & Chip Architecture Licensing
themes.description: This week’s strength in ALAB, CRDO, ARM, MRVL, and CEVA is being driven primarily by continued AI-infrastructure momentum, recent beat-and-raise quarters, and aggressive analyst upgrades and price-target hikes across the group. ALAB, CRDO, and MRVL are benefiting from rapid revenue growth tied to hyperscaler AI connectivity demand and bullish long-term forecasts for custom AI chips and optical interconnects, while ARM and MRVL are also catching a broad semiconductor rally as investors rotate back into chip designers and data-center networking names. CEVA’s move is more idiosyncratic, tied to its newly announced landmark edge-AI licensing deal with a major U.S. software and AI platform company, which has highlighted its IP leverage in next‑gen intelligent devices.

themes.name: Commercial Real Estate Brokerage & Advisory Services
themes.description: The likely catalyst is a broker/analyst upgrade wave in real estate services, with Yahoo Finance showing target-price raises for CWK and JLL this week. That kind of sector-wide analyst optimism can also lift CBRE and NMRK as peers even if the specific note names only a subset directly.

themes.name: European Bank Stocks
themes.description: The main catalyst is the ongoing bid speculation and regulatory push around ING’s potential acquisition of Commerzbank, which is lifting European bank sentiment and sparking a catch-up trade in large cross-border lenders like ING, BCS, BBVA, LYG, NWG and SAN. A secondary driver is renewed optimism around capital-return capacity and regulatory easing (e.g., Bank of England capital rule discussions for UK banks) plus still-supportive rate expectations in Europe, improving net interest income and supporting re-rating across the sector. These names are moving in tandem as investors rotate back into European banks on M&A optionality, capital flexibility and attractive valuations.

themes.name: Senior & Long-Term Care Healthcare REITs
themes.description: The main catalyst appears to be sector-specific healthcare REIT momentum, not a single company-specific headline: Ventas was added to the Russell 1000 Defensive and Value-Defensive indexes on June 27, which can drive passive buying, while Omega Healthcare and LTC are being supported by the broader view that senior housing/nursing demand improves with aging demographics and a steadier healthcare backdrop. For OHI specifically, recent coverage points to stabilized healthcare conditions, resolution of tenant issues, and more investment activity as the likely drivers.

themes.name: Industrial & Logistics REITs
themes.description: TRNO is being bid up on continued leasing momentum in its coastal industrial portfolio, with recent Florida, California, and New Jersey deals bringing several properties to full occupancy and extending lease terms into the 2030s, which improves cash flow visibility and supports the bull case. EGP is seeing interest ahead of its upcoming dividend payable on July 15 and its Q2 earnings call, with investors positioning for steady industrial fundamentals, ongoing dividend consistency, and management’s update following recent Nareit REITweek activity.

themes.name: Non-US & Frontier Market Digital Financial Services Re-Rating
themes.description: PAYP on the Warsaw Stock Exchange has been moving higher this week primarily on a technical/valuation-driven rebound rather than a fresh, stock-specific news catalyst. The move is being framed by traders as a catch-up rally in beaten-down payment names like PayPal after prolonged underperformance and compressed multiples, aided by improving broader risk sentiment toward fintech and IT stocks. In other words, it’s momentum and sector rotation, not a new fundamental announcement from the company.

themes.name: Open-Air & Strip Mall Retail REITs
themes.description: This week’s strength in KRG, PECO, UE, and SKT is being driven primarily by a bid for high‑quality shopping center REITs as investors rotate back into income and defensives on lower rate expectations and stabilizing consumer data. For KRG specifically, flows linked to its recent removal from the Russell 2000 Dynamic Index are contributing to incremental volatility and upside as active managers reassess positioning. Sector‑wide, improving retail foot traffic metrics and resilient rent collections are reinforcing the narrative that open‑air and necessity‑based retail centers can outperform in a slower‑growth, lower‑rate environment.

themes.name: Multifamily Apartment REITs
themes.description: The move in CPT, UDR, and IRT appears to be more sector-driven than company-specific, with apartment REITs catching a bid after Scotiabank raised its Camden Property Trust price target to $109, which helped reinforce a generally constructive tone for the group. I don’t see a clear new operating catalyst for UDR or IRT in the provided items, so the likely driver is broader apartment REIT momentum and analyst sentiment rather than a single fundamental headline.

themes.name: Senior Living & Housing Operators
themes.description: BKD is trading higher on the back of its June financing announcement, where Brookdale extended 2027 non‑recourse mortgage maturities, secured $188M in new Freddie Mac loans, repaid $200M of mortgage debt, and expanded its credit facility to $200M maturing in 2029. This materially improves liquidity and pushes out debt maturities, which is supportive for the equity and out-of-the-money calls like JAN. Broader strength in healthcare and senior living names alongside improving occupancy trends this year is likely adding incremental support.

themes.name: Golf Equipment & Apparel
themes.description: The main catalyst this week for Callaway Golf (CALY) is a wave of bullish analyst activity, including target price hikes toward the 19 area and positive relative performance commentary versus other consumer discretionary names. Shares have broken to new 52-week highs on momentum as investors chase year-to-date outperformance of roughly 50–55%, rather than reacting to any specific company news or fundamental headline. In short, it’s an analyst- and momentum-driven move, not a discrete news event.

themes.name: Government-Funded Senior & Post-Acute Care Re-Rating
themes.description: ALHC (Alignment Healthcare) and AVAH (Aveanna Healthcare) do not appear to have a specific company‑level news catalyst this week; the move looks tied to broader strength in value and small/mid‑cap healthcare services names as investors rotate into defensive, managed-care and home health exposure. Both benefit from the current bid into Medicare/Medicaid and home‑care reimbursement plays as rates stabilize and election‑related policy risk is being repriced more favorably. The rally is more sector‑flow driven than headline‑driven.

themes.name: Satellite Communications & Space Data Services
themes.description: VSAT, SPIR, and MDA are being driven higher this week by a broad space sector risk-on move tied to major catalysts: Rocket Lab’s acquisition of Iridium, the surge in space-focused ETFs like Tema Space Innovators (NASA), and heightened investor focus on the upcoming SpaceX listing as a valuation and liquidity benchmark for the sector. These names are key components or peers in the “new space” infrastructure and services theme, so they are catching flows and momentum as capital rotates into space stocks.

themes.name: Quantum Computing & Quantum-Safe Networking
themes.description: QNT and ARQQ are moving higher this week on the back of a broad quantum-computing sector re‑rating following Quantinuum’s recent Nasdaq listing and growing media focus on “next wave” quantum names, which has pulled in speculative and thematic ETF money into the group. In particular, QNT is benefiting from its high-profile IPO and leadership narrative in logical qubits, while ARQQ is catching sympathy flows as investors look for liquid quantum encryption exposure within the same theme. Macro catalysts like the upcoming Fed meeting and ongoing AI/infrastructure hype are adding a supportive risk-on backdrop for high-beta tech, amplifying those sector-specific moves.

themes.name: Bitcoin Mining & Crypto Infrastructure Operators
themes.description: The main catalyst pushing CIFR, CORZ, HUT, IREN, and WULF higher this week is renewed focus on bitcoin miners as scarce, large-scale power and data-center landlords for the AI compute boom, with investors re-rating those that have significant contracted or negotiable power capacity and AI/HPC pivot plans. VanEck’s widely circulated note on a $50B+ funding gap and market preference for miners with real leases and anchor AI deals (highlighting CIFR, HUT, WULF, CORZ, and IREN as better positioned) has sharpened that narrative and driven flows into the group. This is layered on top of ongoing bitcoin price resilience and deal flow headlines (new leases, capacity expansions, and tenant negotiations) that reinforce the AI-infrastructure thesis for these names.

themes.name: AI Data Center Power Infrastructure
themes.description: BE is likely being lifted by the AI/data-center power trade, with demand for on-site fuel-cell power and the large Wyoming data-center buildout cited as key catalysts. VRT is moving with the same AI infrastructure/critical power theme, while AGX appears to be reacting more to a disclosed congressional stock purchase than a broad industry catalyst. FCEL is also benefiting from the fuel-cell/data-center power narrative, but the move is smaller and looks more thematic than company-specific.

themes.name: Oilfield Pressure Pumping & Completion Services
themes.description: Hydraulic fracturing and oilfield completion service companies are correlating tightly at 0.81 as E&P operators maintain completion activity despite oil price softness, driven by efficiency gains and contract backlogs. This sub-sector of oilfield services is more levered to completion intensity (frac stages per well) than rig count, insulating it from pure drilling slowdowns. Any uptick in commodity prices or permitting activity would accelerate the group.

themes.name: Commercial Real Estate Brokerage & Advisory Services
themes.description: The likely catalyst is a broker/analyst upgrade wave in real estate services, with Yahoo Finance showing target-price raises for CWK and JLL this week. That kind of sector-wide analyst optimism can also lift CBRE and NMRK as peers even if the specific note names only a subset directly.

themes.name: Pure-Play Quantum Computing Hardware
themes.description: The move in IONQ, QUBT, RGTI and SKYT this week is being driven by renewed speculative interest in the quantum computing theme, helped by social/media chatter and influencer coverage around “four quantum stocks” and the broader Nasdaq/AI rebound rather than a specific fundamental company headline. There is also some rotation back into high‑beta, future‑tech names after recent underperformance, with traders leaning into the quantum narrative alongside AI and semiconductor strength. No single stock-specific catalyst (earnings, major contract, or regulatory event) appears to be the primary driver; it’s largely thematic momentum and sentiment.

themes.name: Self-Storage REITs
themes.description: The move in PSA, EXR, CUBE, and NSA this week is being driven primarily by a sector-wide bid into REITs as investors rotate back into defensive, income-oriented names alongside falling long-term yields and growing expectations for Fed rate cuts. Self‑storage is getting incremental support from stabilizing fundamentals commentary and relative outperformance vs. other property types, so capital is gravitating to the higher‑quality, large‑cap platforms. There is no single company‑specific headline; it’s a macro rates/REIT re‑risking catalyst with a self‑storage tilt.

themes.name: Alternative Asset Management Platforms
themes.description: Large alternative asset managers are moving together as fee-earning AUM growth from retail/wealth channel democratization (evergreen funds, 401k access) and infrastructure/private credit deployment drives earnings upgrades. The group benefits from rising management fees on locked-up capital that is less sensitive to market volatility than traditional active managers. Institutional appetite for private markets continues to expand, lengthening the runway for AUM compounding.

themes.name: AI Memory & Storage
themes.description: Auto-retired 2026-07-13: dropped during merge/absorption (no successor found) (prior stage Fading, 6 tickers).

themes.name: In Vivo Gene & Engineered Cell Therapy Clinical Re-Rating
themes.description: Auto-retired 2026-07-13: dropped during merge/absorption (no successor found) (prior stage Nascent, 5 tickers).

themes.name: Large-Cap U.S. Defense Primes
themes.description: Auto-retired 2026-07-13: dropped during merge/absorption (no successor found) (prior stage Fading, 2 tickers).

themes.name: B2B Marketing Automation & Customer Engagement SaaS
themes.description: BRZE, KVYO, and HUBS are all cloud-based platforms helping mid-market and enterprise companies orchestrate personalized customer engagement across email, SMS, and digital channels. AI-powered personalization is accelerating replacement of legacy marketing tools, and recent earnings from BRZE (RS 97) and KVYO have shown re-accelerating revenue growth that's snapping RS back sharply. The shared catalyst is AI-enhanced campaign automation driving net revenue retention above 100% and new logo additions.

themes.name: Health & Wellness Consumer Nutrition Brands
