themes.description: The main catalyst pushing CALY higher this week is the sharp upward revision in earnings estimates and the resulting Zacks upgrade to a Strong Buy rating. Consensus full-year earnings estimates have risen materially over the past 60 days, improving sentiment toward profitability and driving relative outperformance versus other consumer discretionary names. A cleaner balance sheet after full repayment of the term loan B is a secondary supporting factor, but the earnings estimate upgrades and Zacks re-rating are the core near-term driver.

themes.name: Government & Commercial Management Consulting Services
themes.description: ICFI is being driven higher by a bullish sell-side writeup highlighting its launch of an AI-powered licensing, permitting and inspections accelerator built on Salesforce and DocuSign, which positions it well for government digital modernization. The same note also emphasizes increased capital return (share repurchase authorization lifted to $165M plus ongoing dividends) and a $14M Caltrans recompete win that reinforces long-term revenue visibility and contract momentum. HURN’s move is being supported by incremental institutional interest, including a newly disclosed $1.26M position by Y Intercept Hong Kong, alongside ongoing positive sentiment around its healthcare-focused M&A (RelateCare) and strong 2025 results.

themes.name: Coastal & Suburban Residential Rental REITs
themes.description: The main catalyst this week is a rotation into defensive, income-oriented residential REITs as investors seek shelter from macro and geopolitical volatility, combined with solid fundamental support from tight housing supply and resilient rental demand. For AVB and EQR specifically, the previously announced all-stock merger of equals remains a structural tailwind, with investors pricing in expected margin expansion, balance sheet strength, and a higher combined dividend yield once the deal closes in the second half of 2026. For INVH, AMH, ESS, and AVB/EQR peers, the broader theme of housing affordability pressures and limited homeownership access continues to support single-family and multifamily rental pricing power, underpinning multiple expansion across the group.

themes.name: Genomic Medicine & Synthetic DNA Tools
themes.description: MRNA is being driven higher primarily by anticipation of the upcoming FDA decision on its seasonal flu vaccine mRNA-1010 (PDUFA date August 5) and, to a lesser extent, positioning ahead of its July 31 Q2 earnings call, both widely flagged as near-term catalysts. TWST’s strength is tied to improving sentiment on its growth and margin trajectory plus a recent price target hike from Canaccord Genuity to $120, which has reinforced the bull case despite mixed earnings and insider selling.

themes.name: Packaged & Shelf-Stable Food Manufacturers
themes.description: CAG (Conagra), CPB (Campbell's), GIS (General Mills), and KHC (Kraft Heinz) are all large packaged food conglomerates with high correlation (0.85) facing shared headwinds from consumer trade-down to private label, volume pressure, and commodity cost normalization. While currently at very low RS, this cluster could inflect simultaneously if consumer staples sentiment turns or M&A/restructuring activity emerges.

themes.name: Orthopedic & Surgical Device Recovery
themes.description: CNMD is trading higher on news that Conmed is formally exploring strategic alternatives, including a potential sale to private equity, after Bloomberg reported takeover interest and the stock jumped about 10% in after-hours trading. SYK’s strength this week is driven by improving sentiment around recovery from its March cyberattack, with investors positioning for deferred revenue to be recaptured in 2H 2026 and multiple sell-side upgrades/price target raises (notably BMO and Argus) reinforcing the medtech rebound narrative.

themes.name: Domestic Steel Producers
themes.description: CMC, NUE, RS, STLD, and TX form a tight correlation cluster (0.82 corr) of domestic U.S. steel producers all moving in tandem. Potential tariff protection on imported steel, infrastructure bill spending on rebar/structural steel, and domestic manufacturing re-shoring are the shared policy catalysts. While current avg RS of 35 is low, the cluster coherence suggests early institutional positioning — price action is consolidating before any narrative breakout, and domestic steel historically leads industrials when trade policy tightens.

themes.name: Non-US & Frontier Market Digital Financial Services Re-Rating
themes.description: PAYP on the Warsaw Stock Exchange has been moving higher this week primarily on a technical/valuation-driven rebound rather than a fresh, stock-specific news catalyst. The move is being framed by traders as a catch-up rally in beaten-down payment names like PayPal after prolonged underperformance and compressed multiples, aided by improving broader risk sentiment toward fintech and IT stocks. In other words, it’s momentum and sector rotation, not a new fundamental announcement from the company.

themes.name: For-Profit Education & Workforce Training Providers
themes.description: The common driver across these names is renewed bullishness on for‑profit and career-focused education tied to earnings revisions and analyst upgrades in the consumer/education space. CVSA, PRDO, STRA, and UTI are benefiting from strong enrollment trends, EPS estimate revisions, and fresh price-target hikes that have put them on “earnings revisions” and “high visibility” screens for investors, while LINC and LAUR are moving in sympathy as part of the same education-services trade. Sector rotation into non-essential consumer/education names with clearer profit visibility this earnings season is amplifying the move.

themes.name: Diversified Medical Device & Life Science Instrument Manufacturers
themes.description: Procedure volume recovery post-COVID, hospital capital expenditure normalization, and the return of elective procedures are lifting a broad cohort of medical device and life science instrument companies. These names share exposure to hospital/lab capital budgets and procedure-driven consumables revenue that is rebounding as health systems stabilize finances.

themes.name: Multifamily Apartment REITs
themes.description: The move in CPT, UDR, and IRT appears to be more sector-driven than company-specific, with apartment REITs catching a bid after Scotiabank raised its Camden Property Trust price target to $109, which helped reinforce a generally constructive tone for the group. I don’t see a clear new operating catalyst for UDR or IRT in the provided items, so the likely driver is broader apartment REIT momentum and analyst sentiment rather than a single fundamental headline.

themes.name: Minimally Invasive & Robotic Surgical Device Platforms
themes.description: ISRG (robotic-assisted surgery), BSX (cardiac & endoscopic devices), and PEN (endoscopy equipment) are all leaders in minimally invasive surgical technologies, sharing tailwinds from hospital capital spending recovery, expanding robotic procedure volumes, and growing evidence supporting minimally invasive approaches over open surgery. These companies serve similar hospital capital purchasing committees and benefit from the same procedure volume normalization cycle.

themes.name: U.S. Defense Prime Contractors
themes.description: Escalating geopolitical tensions across multiple theaters (Europe, Middle East, Indo-Pacific) are driving sustained U.S. and NATO defense budget expansion, benefiting the five major U.S. prime contractors that dominate large-platform procurement. Congressional budget momentum and allied re-armament spending underpin multi-year backlog growth for these names.

themes.name: Crude & Product Tanker Operators
themes.description: Global oil trade route disruptions (Red Sea, sanctions on Russian crude) are sustaining elevated tanker day-rates for both crude carriers and product tankers. This cluster of pure-play tanker operators is benefiting from prolonged ton-mile demand as cargo reroutes through longer shipping lanes, with limited new vessel supply keeping rates elevated.

themes.name: Quantum Computing Hardware & Software
themes.description: IONQ, QBTS, QUBT, RGTI, and INFQ represent the publicly traded quantum computing ecosystem, moving with tight correlation (0.86) on quantum computing milestone news and government/enterprise investment announcements. While RS remains low, the cluster acts as a unit on quantum computing breakthroughs and policy catalysts, making it a watchlist rotation theme for when sentiment turns.

themes.name: Speculative Space & Defense New Space Ventures
themes.description: Government contracts from NASA, Space Force, and DoD are validating pre-revenue and early-revenue space companies across satellite broadband, lunar logistics, launch, and drone/autonomous defense systems. Risk-appetite for high-beta story-driven government-backed space and defense disruptors is turning on simultaneously as contract wins prove commercial viability.

themes.name: Precious Metals Miners & Royalty Streamers
themes.description: Gold prices breaking multi-year highs on central bank buying, de-dollarization flows, and safe-haven demand are lifting the entire precious metals mining and streaming complex simultaneously. The royalty/streaming model names (FNV, WPM, RGLD, OR, TFPM) provide leveraged exposure with lower operational risk, while the pure miners capture direct price upside. This is a broad rotation into real assets that is statistically coherent across 33 names.

themes.name: Bitcoin Mining & Crypto Infrastructure Operators
themes.description: CIFR, CORZ, HUT, IREN, and WULF are all pure-play Bitcoin miners with correlated price action (corr 0.84) tied directly to Bitcoin price levels and mining economics. While currently at low RS levels, this cluster moves as a unit on crypto sentiment shifts, and any sustained Bitcoin rally or mining economics improvement would create simultaneous breakouts across all names.

themes.name: Global Crude & Product Tanker Fleet Operators
themes.description: DHT, ECO, NAT, STNG, TNK, FRO, INSW, and TRMD form a highly correlated cluster of tanker operators spanning crude and refined product shipping. Tanker day rates and fleet utilization are driven by global trade route disruptions, OPEC output decisions, and geopolitical rerouting — all stocks move in lockstep. The existing theme covers only INSW and TRMD; the broader cohort deserves its own cluster.

themes.name: Cyber Exposure Management & Vulnerability Assessment
themes.description: TENB’s move is being driven mainly by an earnings beat and upbeat guidance, plus the market leaning into its AI/platform-expansion narrative after the recent Q1 print. RPD is moving more on sector sympathy and the broader cybersecurity bid from the Russia-linked threat warning, while QLYS is catching the same cybersecurity rotation and AI/security platform enthusiasm.

themes.name: Traditional Staffing & Talent Placement Services
themes.description: Robert Half (ticker RHI) is moving higher this week primarily on the back of strong positive earnings momentum in the staffing/HR space and a recent wave of bullish research coverage highlighting upside in its valuation and cyclical recovery potential. Several recent articles and analyst notes have framed RHI as a beneficiary of employers re-hiring and reassessing earlier AI-driven layoffs, improving sentiment toward traditional staffing firms. In addition, an uptick in news flow and short-squeeze chatter around business services names is adding a technical tailwind to the stock.

themes.name: Online Insurance Comparison & Digital Marketplace Platforms
themes.description: MAX here appears to be moving on a broad macro tailwind rather than company-specific news: the latest catalyst cited is the stronger-than-expected ISM Services PMI, which signaled continued expansion in the services sector and lifted MediaAlpha alongside other service-oriented names. That said, the move is modest and looks more like sentiment/sector support than a fresh MAX-specific headline.

themes.name: U.S. Petroleum Refining & Downstream Processing
themes.description: The group is being driven higher by a sharp move up in U.S. refining margins, with 3-2-1 crack spreads hitting record or near‑record levels on the back of tight product inventories, strong gasoline/diesel demand and Middle East supply disruptions. At the same time, earnings estimates and sentiment for the refiners have been revised higher into upcoming Q2 prints, reinforcing the bid in VLO, PBF, MPC, DK, PSX and DINO as investors price in outsized profitability.

themes.name: Cloud Communications & Contact Center Platforms
themes.description: FIVN is trading higher on follow-through from its strong Q1 print and guidance, including accelerating AI-driven revenue, a swing to GAAP profitability, and the announcement of a new $200M share repurchase program that has reinforced the bullish analyst narrative around its Voice AI Agents and broader CX AI positioning. BAND’s move is more technical and sentiment-driven, with the stock in an established uptrend supported by bullish market/AI SaaS sentiment rather than a single discrete company-specific news release this week.

themes.name: Peptide & Hormone Therapies for Metabolic & Endocrine Disorders
themes.description: XERS is benefiting from a broader bid into small-cap biotech and diabetes/obesity-adjacent names, with no single stock-specific catalyst dominating this week. PTGX is moving higher on continued momentum and buy-side focus around rusfertide’s FDA priority review (August PDUFA) and the commercial/royalty ramp from Icotyde following Johnson & Johnson’s psoriasis approval. MBX is reacting to corporate governance/leadership changes, with the recent CEO and CFO appointments being reframed by the market as a positive strategic reset, while CORT is being bid on defensive growth and ongoing expectations for Korlym cash flows plus pipeline optionality rather than a discrete headline.

themes.name: SMB & Workforce Business Services Platforms
themes.description: The main catalysts for LegalZoom (LZ) this week are a fresh downgrade to Hold from Wall Street Zen and growing concern pieces flagging LZ among stocks to potentially “dump,” which are prompting positioning shifts and short-covering after the name hit a 52-week low and was heavily de-rated earlier in the year. On the fundamental side, some investors are leaning into the improving small-business formation and activity data that support LZ’s core demand narrative, creating a tug-of-war between cautious analyst sentiment and a stronger macro backdrop for entrepreneurship. For Insperity (NSP), the key driver is renewed focus on PEO/human-capital plays as the market reassesses labor-cost and HR-compliance trends in a decelerating but still tight employment environment, with the stock reacting to changing views around its “reduce” consensus and its leverage to any stabilization in SMB hiring.

themes.name: AI-Powered Software Development & DevOps Platforms
themes.description: Progress Software (PRGS) is moving higher on follow-through from its recent earnings beat and raised full-year guidance, which highlighted resilient demand for its application development and data connectivity tools, plus ongoing share repurchases supporting EPS. GitLab (GTLB) is being driven by a cluster of positive AI- and growth-related catalysts: an investor update highlighting faster consumption growth and strong traction for its GitLab Duo agentic AI platform, upward EPS and revenue guidance revisions, and multiple recent analyst price target hikes alongside its recognition as a Leader in Gartner’s 2026 DevSecOps Magic Quadrant.

themes.name: Precious Metals Miners & Royalty Streamers
themes.description: Gold prices breaking multi-year highs on central bank buying, de-dollarization flows, and safe-haven demand are lifting the entire precious metals mining and streaming complex simultaneously. The royalty/streaming model names (FNV, WPM, RGLD, OR, TFPM) provide leveraged exposure with lower operational risk, while the pure miners capture direct price upside. This is a broad rotation into real assets that is statistically coherent across 33 names.

themes.name: Cloud Observability & AIOps Monitoring Platforms
themes.description: DT, PD, and DDOG are all benefiting from a renewed bid into observability/monitoring and incident-response names tied to AI infrastructure spend and cloud security, with Datadog in particular seeing momentum from strong Q1 results, rising revenue/EPS estimates, and aggressive AI product launches like Bits AI and GPU/LLM observability. The group is also catching support from recent analyst price target hikes and “Leader” recognition in observability, which reinforce the AI-driven growth narrative and institutional interest in the space. In the absence of stock-specific headlines this week for DT and PD, the move is largely a sympathy trade to DDOG’s AI/observability strength and positioning ahead of the next earnings prints.

themes.name: Franchise Auto Dealership Groups
themes.description: For PAG, the main catalyst is anticipation of its upcoming Q2 2026 earnings release and conference call, with investors positioning ahead of the numbers after the stock began trading higher following recent coverage highlighting its strength. For SAH, the move is being driven by rotation into auto dealers ahead of earnings and relief that sector fundamentals and margins remain resilient despite macro headwinds. For ABG, the stock is benefitting from the broader rally in asset/wealth managers as markets hit new highs and fee-based revenue visibility improves, prompting investors to add cyclicals with operating leverage to capital markets.

themes.name: Network Security & Zero-Trust Edge
themes.description: The main catalyst is a sector-wide cybersecurity rally after IBM said clients were shifting spending toward infrastructure and that “rapidly-evolving” cyber concerns remained a priority, which investors took as a sign that security budgets are still holding up despite AI-related spending shifts. PANW also has its own idiosyncratic support from recent strong earnings and multiple analyst price-target hikes, while the broader AI-security narrative is helping names like CRWD, VRNS, RBRK, BB, and CVLT.

themes.name: U.S. Apparel & Fashion Recovery
themes.description: CROX is being driven higher mainly by analyst upgrades and raised price targets after its strong Q1 beat and improved guidance, with investors also positioning ahead of the July 30 earnings report. RVLV is moving on renewed momentum from its Q1 revenue beat and buyer growth, which has re-energized the stock’s growth narrative. UA is up largely on broader athletic-apparel sentiment and follow-through from improving brand and earnings expectations, rather than a single new company-specific catalyst.

themes.name: Consumer Fintech & Digital Credit Platforms
themes.description: Sezzle (SEZL) and Dave (DAVE) are being driven higher primarily by continued momentum from strong recent earnings beats and guidance, plus their positioning as high-growth fintech plays into the current market rotation back into growth. A recent note highlighting SEZL and DAVE as top financial stocks ahead of Q2 earnings and continued focus on their upside surprise track records has attracted incremental momentum and retail interest. Broader supportive macro backdrop for financials and BNPL/consumer-finance names (easing inflation and yields, improving sentiment toward growth) is adding beta tailwind to the individual stock-specific strength.

themes.name: Corporate Spend, Expense Management & Financial Operations Software
themes.description: NAVN is being driven higher by a blowout quarter with record growth, strong corporate demand, and a raised FY27 revenue outlook, which has the stock surging and analysts hiking price targets. WEX has moved up on the market’s view that it is undervalued following its strategic Bluon agreement, which enhances its field service management offering and supports growth in payments and mobility. BILL’s strength is primarily sentiment-driven around its long-term growth story, with a supportive “moderate buy” analyst consensus, rather than a single discrete news catalyst this week.

themes.name: B2B Digital Financial Infrastructure & Payment Rails Modernization
themes.description: QTWO is moving higher on continued enthusiasm around real-time and regulatory technology themes, with recent RegTech-focused AI coverage highlighting it among beneficiaries of tightening AI compliance rules. PAY is bid on the broader strength in payments and RegTech names as investors position for accelerating digital and instant payment adoption. ACIW is rallying on a cluster of positive catalysts: a Q1 EPS beat and raised guidance, strategic wins with the European Payments Initiative and Rabobank’s Wero instant payments, PSP expansion in Africa, and fresh upside from sell-side price target hikes and a potential sale of its billing division; I do not see a discrete new headline this week beyond ongoing momentum from those developments.

themes.name: Professional Advisory & Business Services
themes.description: CBZ is trading higher on follow-through from its recent earnings beat and guidance commentary, which highlighted resilient demand across its advisory and outsourcing businesses and improving margin trajectory. CRAI is being bid up after BriaCell Therapeutics engaged CRA International to support commercial strategy and market access for its Bria-IMT oncology program, positioning CRAI for incremental high-value consulting revenue tied to a late-stage asset.

themes.name: Enterprise HR & Workforce Management SaaS
themes.description: WDAY is being driven higher by optimism around upcoming Q2 earnings on July 23, continued AI-focused product and partnership announcements, and positive analyst sentiment and price targets tied to its role as a core HR/finance cloud platform. PCTY is benefiting from the same SaaS HR/Payroll trade, where investors are rotating back into profitable HCM names ahead of earnings season and leaning on recurring subscription growth and margin expansion. ALIT is moving with the broader HR/benefits and outsourcing complex as investors price in stable cash flows and potential upside from cost-cutting and digital transformation demand rather than any single, stock-specific headline.

themes.name: Insurance Brokerage & Distribution Services
themes.description: AJG, AON, and BRO are moving higher mainly on a sector-wide rally in insurance brokers, helped by a fresh wave of bullish analyst commentary on AJG and improving sentiment around brokerage growth. For AJG specifically, upgrades and higher price targets from firms like Piper Sandler, RBC, Wells Fargo, Mizuho, Barclays, and UBS, plus acquisition activity, have reinforced the move. BRO is likely catching the same sympathy bid because these names often trade in lockstep with AJG.

themes.name: Casual Dining Restaurant Turnaround
themes.description: EAT and CBRL are being driven higher by the ongoing restaurant turnaround trade, with money rotating into casual dining names that have recently shown improving traffic, margins, and guest satisfaction metrics. For Cracker Barrel specifically, the key tailwind is its recent upside earnings surprise, raised outlook, and visible operational turnaround (including better customer satisfaction and margin expansion), which have attracted both retail attention and incremental institutional interest. In short, the move is more about continued follow-through on positive turnaround news and positioning than about a fresh, single headline this week.

themes.name: Inflammatory Disease & Immunology Biologics
themes.description: ORKA is higher mainly on renewed enthusiasm for its psoriasis pipeline, especially the strong EVERLAST-A Week 16 data on ORKA-001 showing 63.5% PASI 100, with traders also looking ahead to longer-term data in the second half of 2026. Analyst target hikes and the stock’s recent Russell index inclusion are adding momentum.

themes.name: Truckload & LTL Freight Carriers
themes.description: The main catalyst lifting TFII, SNDR, LSTR, WERN, ARCB, and KNX this week is the rotation back into U.S. trucking and logistics on expectations of improving freight demand and AI-driven industrial and earnings strength across the broader market. Lower inflation prints and the growing consensus that the Fed is closer to easing have supported cyclical transports, while strong AI-related earnings from large industrial/tech names are reinforcing the view that freight volumes and pricing will stabilize into 2H. There is no stock-specific headline driving one of these names individually; it’s a sector move tied to macro, cyclicals, and positioning.

themes.name: Digital Advertising Technology Platforms
themes.description: Magnite and QuinStreet are moving higher on sympathetic momentum to the broader ad-tech and performance marketing complex, helped by renewed interest in AI-driven ad solutions and connected TV/retail media data partnerships such as Magnite’s work with Walmart and dentsu/DIRECTV. There is also a structural catalyst from the DOJ antitrust case against Google’s ad business, which is driving flows into independent ad platforms. In the absence of company-specific headlines this week, the move is largely a sector re-rating and correlation trade rather than a discrete news release for MGNI or QNST.

themes.name: Cloud Supply Chain & Warehouse Management Software
themes.description: The move in MANH is being driven mainly by renewed optimism around its cloud-native platform and AI-powered supply chain offerings after a record Q4 2025, raised 2026 guidance, and an expanded $500 million buyback that signaled strong management confidence. For SPSC, the key near-term catalyst is anticipation of its Q2 2026 results release on July 30, with investors positioning ahead of the print given the focus on recurring revenue metrics, cross-sell and net retention. Broader sector sentiment around supply-chain and retail logistics software, where both names are leveraged to cloud and AI themes, is also helping.

themes.name: Cross-Border & International Payments Processing
themes.description: GPN is trading higher on follow-through from its strong Q1 print (10% EPS growth, 5.5% net revenue growth), the $500 million accelerated buyback, and a recent Morgan Stanley upgrade highlighting improved positioning and upcoming Q2 earnings. EEFT is benefiting from a bullish re-rating as multiple analysts flag upside to its consensus target (roughly mid‑teens to low‑20% above spot) alongside a “BUY” conviction call and positive technical uptrend, turning it into a relative winner amid the recent broader-market correction.

themes.name: Uniform Rental & Managed Workwear Services
themes.description: For CTAS, the move higher is being driven by its FY26 earnings beat (revenue and EPS above consensus), record margins, and FY27 revenue/EPS guidance that came in above Street expectations, prompting multiple price target hikes and reinforcing the UniFirst acquisition as a medium‑term upside catalyst. For VSTS, the stock is benefiting from sympathy/sector momentum around business services/uniform and facilities names after CTAS’s strong print and guidance, with investors rotating into similar quality compounders in the space rather than any single, stock‑specific headline.

themes.name: B2B Marketing Automation & Customer Engagement SaaS
themes.description: KVYO is being driven higher by continued enthusiasm around its recent AI product rollouts, accelerating revenue growth, record operating margins, raised full‑year guidance, and an expanded share buyback, which together reinforce the earnings and profitability story. BRZE is moving in sympathy as a closely correlated peer in marketing automation/CRM software, benefiting from read‑through from KVYO’s strength rather than a discrete new headline of its own. HUBS is riding the broader rebound in high‑quality SaaS/CRM names after strong Q1 results and a reputation for consistent estimate beats, with investors positioning ahead of upcoming investor events despite a prior downgrade.

themes.name: Digital Payments Processing & Merchant Acquiring Networks
themes.description: FOUR is trading higher on continued follow‑through from last week’s upside earnings surprise and raised guidance, which has investors leaning into the name as a small‑cap payments winner ahead of broader fintech M&A chatter. PYPL is being driven almost entirely by the reported $53 billion, $60.50/share takeover offer from Stripe and Advent International, which has re-rated the stock toward the bid and fueled expectations for either a higher offer or competing interest. Both moves are being reinforced by traders positioning ahead of upcoming earnings and board responses to the PYPL bid.

themes.name: Specialty Insurance Underwriting & Brokerage
themes.description: PLMR is being driven higher by a fresh JPMorgan catalyst: analyst Pablo Singzon reiterated an Overweight and raised the price target from 150 to 167, signaling stronger conviction and attracting incremental buyer interest. RYAN is getting a lift from institutional positioning, with Fenimore Asset Management’s Q2 13F showing an 80%+ increase in its stake, reinforcing the “contrarian value” narrative after prior underperformance. KNSL strength is tied to ongoing earnings momentum in the E&S segment and bullish technicals, with the stock trading above key moving averages into its upcoming July 23 earnings, keeping sentiment and positioning skewed positively.

themes.name: Wealth Management & Retail Brokerage Platforms
themes.description: The move higher in the online brokers and asset managers this week is being driven primarily by a sector-wide bid into Financials as investors rotate into YTD laggards alongside easing inflation and supportive macro data, which is improving sentiment around trading activity and asset-gathering. Lower yields and expectations for a more dovish Fed path are helping rate-sensitive, transaction- and fee-driven platforms like HOOD, AMP, RJF, SF, SCHW, and SEIC as markets rally and risk appetite picks up. For SEIC specifically, positive earnings revisions and strong momentum factors are adding an idiosyncratic tailwind on top of the broader sector move.

themes.name: Emerging Market Digital Commerce & Fintech Platforms
themes.description: MELI is being bid up mainly on investors revisiting the long-term growth story ahead of its upcoming Q2 earnings, with renewed focus on accelerating revenue, strong user engagement, and management’s confidence in its heavy ecosystem investments across e-commerce and fintech. Several recent articles and analyst pieces frame the stock as a potential once-in-a-decade compounder despite margin pressure, which is drawing in buyers after the roughly 13% rebound from June lows. The near-term earnings date and the market’s willingness to look through margin compression toward AI, logistics, credit and advertising-driven growth are the key catalysts this week.

themes.name: Life Insurance & Annuity Providers
themes.description: The move higher in UNM, MET, PFG, and PRU this week is being driven mainly by a supportive macro backdrop for life insurers (rising long-end yields, narrower credit fears, and rotation into financials) plus strong fundamentals and recent earnings beats across the group. Company-specific news is reinforcing the bid: MET and PRU are trading near 52-week highs after upside earnings surprises and ongoing buybacks/strategic deals, while PFG just printed an all‑time high on the back of robust total return, dividend growth, and the Beam Benefits acquisition. Sector pieces highlighting life and group-benefits insurers as beneficiaries of declining P&C pricing and improving credit sentiment are adding incremental momentum to the space, including UNM.

themes.name: Cloud Data Storage & Analytics Infrastructure
themes.description: Snowflake (SNOW) is trading higher this week mainly on continued momentum from its late-May Q1 FY2027 earnings beat and the announcement of a $6 billion, five-year AWS infrastructure partnership, which together reinforced the AI-driven growth narrative and led to a series of analyst price target hikes and upgrades. Investor sentiment remains supported by 34% product revenue growth, record remaining performance obligations above $9 billion, and raised FY2027 product revenue guidance, keeping buyers active into the current week. Backblaze (BLZE) does not have a specific new fundamental news catalyst this week; its move appears more tied to general AI/cloud storage sentiment and retail/media-driven volatility rather than a discrete company-specific announcement.

themes.name: Office REIT Recovery & Re-Rating
themes.description: The move in SLG, HIW and CDP this week is being driven mainly by a broad bid into office and commercial REITs as investors position ahead of Q2 earnings and on growing expectations of lower rates supporting cap values and funding costs. HIW has an explicit Q2 print scheduled for July 28, and the group is benefiting from improving sentiment around US office fundamentals and income stability, rather than any stock-specific headline. No major company-specific news for SLG or CDP appears to be the catalyst; it’s largely sector-wide rotation into beaten-down office REITs on the rates and macro narrative.

themes.name: Targeted Protein Degradation Oncology
themes.description: GLUE and KYMR are both trading higher this week primarily on continued momentum from recent positive clinical and pipeline developments, rather than a fresh single-day headline. For Kymera (KYMR), the key driver remains the late-June catalyst of accelerated completion of enrollment in the Phase 2b BROADEN2 trial of lead STAT6 degrader KT-621 in atopic dermatitis, which pulled forward the topline data timeline and de-risked the program, with follow‑through support from recent bullish research and July management/pipeline updates. For Monte Rosa (GLUE), the move is largely a sympathy/sector bid into targeted protein degradation names and positioning ahead of upcoming data/milestones, with no major new company‑specific announcement this week.

themes.name: Specialty Catastrophe Property Insurance Underwriters
themes.description: The main catalyst driving Slide Insurance Holdings (SLDE) higher this week is its recent strong quarterly earnings beat combined with a newly authorized $100 million share repurchase program, which is lifting sentiment and attracting incremental institutional interest. Emerald Mutual Fund Advisers Trust’s disclosed share purchases are reinforcing the bullish narrative, while the stock’s technical setup near 52-week highs is drawing momentum and breakout traders.

themes.name: Digital Insurance Distribution & Marketplace Platforms
themes.description: The move in Porch Group (PRCH) and Goosehead Insurance (GSHD) this week appears to be driven more by technical momentum and renewed interest in the insurance/insurtech space than by a single, specific news catalyst. PRCH has seen a slight uptick in news volume versus its normal pace, but without a standout company-specific headline or earnings event this week, while GSHD’s recent strength is tied to a broader rerating as investors revisit its growth and valuation story. In other words, the catalyst is largely sector momentum and positioning rather than a discrete news item.

themes.name: Industrial & Logistics REITs
themes.description: The main driver is a sector-wide bid into industrial/logistics REITs on expectations of continued strong fundamentals (rent growth, high occupancy, and reshoring/nearshoring demand), with Terreno (TRNO) also getting a bump from positive leasing news and hitting/clearing its average analyst target price. EastGroup (EGP) is benefiting from multiple recent Buy reiterations (e.g., Wells Fargo on July 18) and anticipation of its upcoming earnings release, which is reinforcing the bull case on Sunbelt industrial exposure. STAG and FR are moving largely in sympathy as investors rotate into the industrial REIT group ahead of earnings and on the back of easing-rate and soft-landing positioning.

themes.name: AI Cloud GPU & Datacenter Colocation Platforms
themes.description: Auto-retired 2026-07-21: dropped during merge/absorption (no successor found) (prior stage Fading, 2 tickers).

themes.name: Financial Compliance & Regulatory Reporting SaaS
themes.description: WK’s strength in DFIN this week is being driven primarily by renewed interest in its AI-enabled disclosure and compliance tools, including the recently launched AI-powered iXBRL tagging capability within its Active Intelligence suite. Investors are also viewing DFIN as a beneficiary of the SEC’s proposed shift toward more flexible reporting (new Form 10-S), which highlights structural demand for its ActiveDisclosure platform. The combination of these regulatory and AI themes is catalyzing a rerating of the stock.

themes.name: Liquid Biopsy & Multi-Cancer Early Detection
themes.description: GH Research (ticker GH, and GHR on some feeds) is trading higher this week on M&A read-through from Bloomberg’s report that Eli Lilly is in advanced talks to acquire AtaiBeckley, whose lead psychedelic asset targets the same treatment-resistant depression indication as GH’s GH001. That news, combined with RBC highlighting GH Research as the top beneficiary of this deal dynamic and a supportive regulatory backdrop for psychedelic therapies, is driving incremental buying interest and a sharp move higher.

themes.name: U.S. Regional & Mid-Cap Commercial Banks
themes.description: The main catalyst is a broad regional-bank bid this week, helped by strong Q2 results and guidance from large regionals that reinforced the view that deposit costs are easing, net interest margins are stabilizing, and credit quality remains solid. FITB is also getting a specific boost from its Comerica acquisition/integration story after reporting a Q2 beat and stronger adjusted EPS, while USB and CFG are benefiting from analyst upgrades and sector spillover. BOKF, UMBF, and EWBC look to be moving mostly with the same sector rotation rather than a single company-specific headline.

themes.name: European Bank Stocks
themes.description: The move higher in these European and UK banks this week is primarily being driven by a more supportive rate and macro backdrop, with cooler US inflation and shifting Fed/BoE expectations improving the outlook for net interest margins and financials broadly. European banks are also catching a bid from the start of earnings season, with strong big-bank results in the US and anticipation of solid European prints, alongside ongoing investor focus on capital return (buybacks/dividends) in names like NWG. Geopolitical and oil-related volatility is a secondary factor, but the dominant catalyst for BCS, LYG, SAN, BBVA, NWG, and ING is the improved rates/earnings narrative for the sector.

themes.name: Global Crude & Product Tanker Fleet Operators
themes.description: The main catalyst is a strong bid for tanker and broader energy names on expectations of “spectacular” Q2 earnings and renewed institutional recommendations for the group, with Okeanis Eco Tankers (ECO), International Seaways (INSW), and Teekay Tankers (TNK) specifically highlighted as preferred plays. That sits on top of an already constructive tanker backdrop (tight effective supply, aging fleets, regulation, and geopolitical route disruptions) and strong forward bookings/earnings visibility for INSW and peers, which is keeping sentiment and multiples elevated. For TRMD and FRO, the move is largely read-through from the same tanker macro (high spot rates, fleet renewal, geopolitical disruptions, and anticipated support from recovering Middle East production and inventory restocking), rather than a single name-specific headline.

themes.name: Life Science Tools & Analytical Instruments
themes.description: The main catalyst is this week’s earnings setup: DHR reports July 21 and TMO reports July 23, and both names are trading with a pre-earnings bid. DHR is also getting support from continued strength in bioprocessing/biotechnology demand, while TMO has recent momentum from strong Q1 results, raised guidance, and AI/Clario-related optimism. MTD’s move looks more like sector sympathy and positioning ahead of the same life-sciences earnings window than a company-specific headline.

themes.name: Property & Casualty Insurance Underwriters
themes.description: The move looks mostly tied to broader insurance-sector strength plus company-specific analyst/earnings optimism: ACGL has a JPMorgan target raise, and its peers like THG, TRV, WRB, AXS, and SKWD are also being bid on expectations for solid pricing, underwriting margins, and favorable catastrophe-loss assumptions. For WRB specifically, the stock is also trading in a risk-on week where investors are leaning into insurers ahead of a packed macro/earnings calendar, which can amplify the group move. AXS is the outlier: unless you mean a different AXS ticker, the results here don’t show a clear insurer catalyst for Axie Infinity, so it may be a separate stock or a mistaken ticker.

themes.name: Financial Services Vertical SaaS
themes.description: JKHY is being helped by bullish analyst revisions and commentary on upside potential, alongside positive sentiment around its expanded Google Cloud AI security partnership for banks. GWRE is trading higher on strong fiscal Q3 results and ongoing news flow highlighting AI-driven insurance innovation and broader adoption of its platform. SSNC is benefiting from news about its push into tokenized funds and digital cash settlement using stablecoins and tokenized bank deposits, reinforcing a structural growth and innovation narrative.

themes.name: Private Mortgage Insurance
themes.description: The move looks driven mainly by a sector rerating in private mortgage insurers on stronger housing-market expectations, not a single company-specific headline. For ESNT specifically, JPMorgan recently raised its price target, while NMIH has gotten fresh positive coverage tied to mortgage-insurance growth and profitability; MTG and RDN are likely moving with the same group.

themes.name: Open-Air & Strip Mall Retail REITs
themes.description: The move is mainly interest-rate-sensitive REIT strength: investors are rotating into property names like KIM, REG, FRT, UE, SKT and KRG as Treasury yields ease and the market prices in a more dovish Fed path. For KRG specifically, the stock has also been hitting fresh highs on the back of positive REIT sentiment and momentum.

themes.name: Health & Wellness Consumer Nutrition Brands
themes.description: Auto-retired 2026-07-21: dropped during merge/absorption (no successor found) (prior stage Fading, 2 tickers).

themes.name: Custom AI Silicon & Chip Architecture Licensing
themes.description: ALAB and MRVL are being driven higher by a bullish UBS analyst call highlighting upside from CXL/AI data-center connectivity and raising price targets, alongside broader renewed buying in AI semiconductor and infrastructure names. ARM and CRDO are participating as part of the same AI hardware/high‑speed connectivity theme, with CRDO specifically benefiting from momentum around its 800G optical transceivers and Zacks Strong Buy rating, and ARM from rotation back into leading AI chip IP plays. Overall, the common catalyst is the rebound in AI hardware sentiment and increasing focus on companies leveraged to hyperscaler AI capex and data-center connectivity.

themes.name: Commercial Real Estate Brokerage & Advisory Services
themes.description: The main catalyst pushing JLL, CIGI, and NMRK higher this week is improving sentiment around commercial real estate services and capital markets activity, highlighted by visible deal and financing wins. For NMRK specifically, the stock is reacting to news coverage of its $515 million refinancing of a Midtown Manhattan office tower and its Asia-Pacific expansion via the Hong Kong project management leadership hire, which together support the undervaluation narrative and earnings growth runway. JLL and CIGI are benefiting in sympathy from the same theme of rising transaction/financing activity and global platform buildout, which investors see as signaling a cyclical recovery in brokerage, advisory, and property services.

themes.name: U.S. Passenger Airlines
themes.description: The main catalyst pushing SKYW, UAL, DAL, ALGT, ALK and LUV higher this week is relief on fuel-cost expectations driven by easing Iran/geopolitical risk and associated moderation in oil prices, which improves margin outlook across the group. Sector sentiment is further supported by strong summer travel demand and Delta’s better‑than‑expected Q2 print, which reinforces confidence in airlines’ ability to pass through fares and sustain revenue. Stock‑specific tailwinds (analyst upgrades, ALK/Hawaiian integration optimism, upcoming Q2 earnings) are amplifying the move but are secondary to the fuel/geo and demand backdrop.

themes.name: Semiconductor Wafer Foundry & Advanced IC Manufacturing
themes.description: The group is moving higher on a broad rebound in semiconductor equipment tied to renewed confidence in AI infrastructure demand and a recovery in memory and logic chip spending after the recent sector selloff. Investor sentiment has improved as major foundries and memory makers signal higher capex for advanced nodes, GAA and high‑bandwidth memory, reinforcing a view that the wafer fab equipment cycle is entering a sustained uptrend. Easing macro pressures from cooler inflation and a more benign rate outlook are also supporting high‑growth, capex‑levered tech and semi names this week.

themes.name: Coastal & Suburban Residential Rental REITs
themes.description: The move appears tied to a fresh analyst re-rating of the single-family rental group, led by Barclays lifting AMH’s price target, which is supporting sentiment across residential REITs. INVH and the apartment names EQR and ESS are likely trading higher in sympathy on the same sector rotation rather than on company-specific news.

themes.name: Molecular Diagnostics & DNA-Based Genetic Testing
themes.description: BLLN is moving higher on anticipation around its upcoming Q2 2026 earnings release, with the company announcing it will report results on August 5, which has already triggered a positive price reaction. QGEN’s strength this week is being driven primarily by renewed takeover speculation, with reports that multiple private equity firms are exploring a potential buyout around $50 per share.

themes.name: Antibody-Based Oncology & Autoimmune Biologics
themes.description: ABVX is trading higher as investors continue to react to the recent positive Phase 3 ulcerative colitis data for obefazimod, the $920M upsized equity raise that fully closed, and growing expectations for a U.S. launch and potential strategic interest or buyout. GMAB is being bid up on the back of its announced acquisition of Merus N.V., which adds the late‑stage, Breakthrough Therapy‑designated antibody petosemtamab to its pipeline, alongside recent positive EU approval and Phase 3 data for epcoritamab combinations in lymphomas. Together, those clinical and corporate developments are driving renewed bullish sentiment and rerating both names higher this week.

themes.name: B2B Data Intelligence & Professional Information Services
themes.description: NIQ TRI appears to be moving higher on broader market optimism tied to a busy macro week, with investors focused on U.S. CPI and the start of major bank earnings, which are expected to shape risk appetite. The main catalyst is not NIQ-specific in the results provided; it’s the inflation/earnings setup and stronger tech-led sentiment.

themes.name: Senior & Long-Term Care Healthcare REITs
themes.description: The main catalyst is a broad bid into healthcare REITs as investors rotate defensively and reprice senior housing/post‑acute operators on improving utilization and stable funding, benefiting LTC, OHI, and VTR together. Sector sentiment has been supported by aging‑demographic tailwinds, expectations for solid upcoming earnings (e.g., VTR’s Q2 release), and renewed interest from income investors as long rates stabilize. There is no single stock‑specific headline this week; it’s a sector move driven by macro and demographic themes.

themes.name: Golf Equipment & Apparel
themes.description: The main catalyst pushing CALY higher this week is the sharp upward revision in earnings estimates and the resulting Zacks upgrade to a Strong Buy rating. Consensus full-year earnings estimates have risen materially over the past 60 days, improving sentiment toward profitability and driving relative outperformance versus other consumer discretionary names. A cleaner balance sheet after full repayment of the term loan B is a secondary supporting factor, but the earnings estimate upgrades and Zacks re-rating are the core near-term driver.

themes.name: Orthopedic & Surgical Device Recovery
themes.description: Auto-retired 2026-07-21: dropped during merge/absorption (no successor found) (prior stage Fading, 2 tickers).

themes.name: Government & Commercial Management Consulting Services
themes.description: ICFI is being driven higher by a bullish sell-side writeup highlighting its launch of an AI-powered licensing, permitting and inspections accelerator built on Salesforce and DocuSign, which positions it well for government digital modernization. The same note also emphasizes increased capital return (share repurchase authorization lifted to $165M plus ongoing dividends) and a $14M Caltrans recompete win that reinforces long-term revenue visibility and contract momentum. HURN’s move is being supported by incremental institutional interest, including a newly disclosed $1.26M position by Y Intercept Hong Kong, alongside ongoing positive sentiment around its healthcare-focused M&A (RelateCare) and strong 2025 results.

themes.name: Genomic Medicine & Synthetic DNA Tools
themes.description: MRNA is being driven higher primarily by anticipation of the upcoming FDA decision on its seasonal flu vaccine mRNA-1010 (PDUFA date August 5) and, to a lesser extent, positioning ahead of its July 31 Q2 earnings call, both widely flagged as near-term catalysts. TWST’s strength is tied to improving sentiment on its growth and margin trajectory plus a recent price target hike from Canaccord Genuity to $120, which has reinforced the bull case despite mixed earnings and insider selling.

themes.name: Packaged & Shelf-Stable Food Manufacturers
themes.description: CAG (Conagra), CPB (Campbell's), GIS (General Mills), and KHC (Kraft Heinz) are all large packaged food conglomerates with high correlation (0.85) facing shared headwinds from consumer trade-down to private label, volume pressure, and commodity cost normalization. While currently at very low RS, this cluster could inflect simultaneously if consumer staples sentiment turns or M&A/restructuring activity emerges.

themes.name: AI Cloud GPU & Datacenter Colocation Platforms
themes.description: PENG is being driven higher by a cluster of post-earnings catalysts: a big Q3 beat (revenue up ~48% with EPS well above consensus), a raised 2026/2027 growth outlook, and multiple target hikes from analysts that pushed it to fresh 52‑week highs. On top of that, investors are bidding it up as an AI infrastructure winner after Nvidia named Penguin an AI Factory Specialized Partner, reinforcing the AI demand narrative.

themes.name: Canadian Big Banks
themes.description: The main catalyst pushing BMO, BNS, CM, RY, and TD higher this week is a combination of strong recent bank earnings driven by sharply lower credit loss provisions and ongoing dividend growth, which has reinforced the bullish narrative around Canadian banks’ profitability and income appeal. That fundamental backdrop is being amplified by a momentum-driven bid into the group, with several of these names and related bank vehicles printing new 52‑week highs and being highlighted as top dividend and “strong buy” value plays by brokers and media. A supportive macro backdrop (rate‑cut cycle already underway, credit fears fading, and regulators easing capital buffers) is further reducing perceived risk, encouraging investors to add exposure to the big Canadian banks.

themes.name: U.S. Domestic Steel Producers
themes.description: CMC, NUE, RS, STLD, and TX form a tight correlation cluster (0.82 corr) of domestic U.S. steel producers all moving in tandem. Potential tariff protection on imported steel, infrastructure bill spending on rebar/structural steel, and domestic manufacturing re-shoring are the shared policy catalysts. While current avg RS of 35 is low, the cluster coherence suggests early institutional positioning — price action is consolidating before any narrative breakout, and domestic steel historically leads industrials when trade policy tightens.

themes.name: Health & Wellness Consumer Nutrition Brands
themes.description: BRBR (protein shakes/nutrition beverages), VITL (pasture-raised eggs/butter), and SMPL (nutritional snacks/meal replacements) are all premium better-for-you food and nutrition consumer brands gaining shelf space and wallet share as consumers shift from processed food toward functional/clean-label products. The GLP-1 diet-consciousness wave and post-pandemic health prioritization are structurally expanding the addressable market for high-protein, clean-ingredient branded foods, and all three are showing multi-week RS acceleration from a similar consumer-defensive thematic rotation.

themes.name: Orthopedic & Surgical Device Recovery
themes.description: CNMD is trading higher on news that Conmed is formally exploring strategic alternatives, including a potential sale to private equity, after Bloomberg reported takeover interest and the stock jumped about 10% in after-hours trading. SYK’s strength this week is driven by improving sentiment around recovery from its March cyberattack, with investors positioning for deferred revenue to be recaptured in 2H 2026 and multiple sell-side upgrades/price target raises (notably BMO and Argus) reinforcing the medtech rebound narrative.

themes.name: For-Profit Education & Workforce Training Providers
themes.description: The common driver across these names is renewed bullishness on for‑profit and career-focused education tied to earnings revisions and analyst upgrades in the consumer/education space. CVSA, PRDO, STRA, and UTI are benefiting from strong enrollment trends, EPS estimate revisions, and fresh price-target hikes that have put them on “earnings revisions” and “high visibility” screens for investors, while LINC and LAUR are moving in sympathy as part of the same education-services trade. Sector rotation into non-essential consumer/education names with clearer profit visibility this earnings season is amplifying the move.

themes.name: Diversified Medical Device & Life Science Instrument Manufacturers
themes.description: Procedure volume recovery post-COVID, hospital capital expenditure normalization, and the return of elective procedures are lifting a broad cohort of medical device and life science instrument companies. These names share exposure to hospital/lab capital budgets and procedure-driven consumables revenue that is rebounding as health systems stabilize finances.

themes.name: Bitcoin Mining & Crypto Infrastructure Operators
themes.description: CIFR, CORZ, HUT, IREN, and WULF are all pure-play Bitcoin miners with correlated price action (corr 0.84) tied directly to Bitcoin price levels and mining economics. While currently at low RS levels, this cluster moves as a unit on crypto sentiment shifts, and any sustained Bitcoin rally or mining economics improvement would create simultaneous breakouts across all names.

themes.name: Minimally Invasive & Robotic Surgical Device Platforms
themes.description: ISRG (robotic-assisted surgery), BSX (cardiac & endoscopic devices), and PEN (endoscopy equipment) are all leaders in minimally invasive surgical technologies, sharing tailwinds from hospital capital spending recovery, expanding robotic procedure volumes, and growing evidence supporting minimally invasive approaches over open surgery. These companies serve similar hospital capital purchasing committees and benefit from the same procedure volume normalization cycle.

themes.name: U.S. Defense Prime Contractors
themes.description: Escalating geopolitical tensions across multiple theaters (Europe, Middle East, Indo-Pacific) are driving sustained U.S. and NATO defense budget expansion, benefiting the five major U.S. prime contractors that dominate large-platform procurement. Congressional budget momentum and allied re-armament spending underpin multi-year backlog growth for these names.

themes.name: Quantum Computing Hardware & Software
themes.description: IONQ, QBTS, QUBT, RGTI, and INFQ represent the publicly traded quantum computing ecosystem, moving with tight correlation (0.86) on quantum computing milestone news and government/enterprise investment announcements. While RS remains low, the cluster acts as a unit on quantum computing breakthroughs and policy catalysts, making it a watchlist rotation theme for when sentiment turns.

themes.name: Speculative Space & Defense New Space Ventures
themes.description: Government contracts from NASA, Space Force, and DoD are validating pre-revenue and early-revenue space companies across satellite broadband, lunar logistics, launch, and drone/autonomous defense systems. Risk-appetite for high-beta story-driven government-backed space and defense disruptors is turning on simultaneously as contract wins prove commercial viability.

themes.name: Precious Metals Miners & Royalty Streamers
themes.description: Gold prices breaking multi-year highs on central bank buying, de-dollarization flows, and safe-haven demand are lifting the entire precious metals mining and streaming complex simultaneously. The royalty/streaming model names (FNV, WPM, RGLD, OR, TFPM) provide leveraged exposure with lower operational risk, while the pure miners capture direct price upside. This is a broad rotation into real assets that is statistically coherent across 33 names.

themes.name: Bitcoin & Crypto Mining Infrastructure
themes.description: Auto-retired 2026-07-20: absorbed/superseded by 'Bitcoin Mining & Crypto Infrastructure Operators' (prior stage Nascent, 5 tickers).

themes.name: Non-US & Frontier Market Digital Financial Services Re-Rating
themes.description: Auto-retired 2026-07-20: dropped during merge/absorption (no successor found) (prior stage Fading, 2 tickers).

themes.name: Insurance Brokerage & Distribution Services
themes.description: AJG, AON, and BRO are moving higher mainly on a sector-wide rally in insurance brokers, helped by a fresh wave of bullish analyst commentary on AJG and improving sentiment around brokerage growth. For AJG specifically, upgrades and higher price targets from firms like Piper Sandler, RBC, Wells Fargo, Mizuho, Barclays, and UBS, plus acquisition activity, have reinforced the move. BRO is likely catching the same sympathy bid because these names often trade in lockstep with AJG.

themes.name: European Bank Stocks
themes.description: The move higher in these European and UK banks this week is primarily being driven by a more supportive rate and macro backdrop, with cooler US inflation and shifting Fed/BoE expectations improving the outlook for net interest margins and financials broadly. European banks are also catching a bid from the start of earnings season, with strong big-bank results in the US and anticipation of solid European prints, alongside ongoing investor focus on capital return (buybacks/dividends) in names like NWG. Geopolitical and oil-related volatility is a secondary factor, but the dominant catalyst for BCS, LYG, SAN, BBVA, NWG, and ING is the improved rates/earnings narrative for the sector.

themes.name: Copper Mining & Production
themes.description: The main catalyst is a broad copper rally: LME three-month copper jumped 1.7% to $13,851/ton, driven by strong China demand, falling inventories, and hopes for a ceasefire-related de-escalation that could tighten supply, which lifted FCX, SCCO, HBM, ERO, and TGB with it. TGB is also seeing stock-specific momentum, but the common driver across the group is higher copper prices.

themes.name: Urban Gateway Office REITs
themes.description: The main catalyst appears to be a broad office-REIT bounce, with investors rotating into names like BXP, DEI, VNO, and SLG on improving leasing/occupancy trends and more constructive sentiment toward premium office assets. SLG-specific strength is also being helped by recent financing and leasing progress, including its large One Madison refinancing and strong Manhattan leasing activity.

themes.name: Molecular Diagnostics & DNA-Based Genetic Testing
themes.description: QGEN is being bid up this week primarily on the disclosure that BlackRock has built a significant stake, crossing a substantial shareholding threshold and boosting institutional ownership and visibility. That’s being reinforced by positive coverage around rising Cyclospora testing demand highlighting QIAGEN’s QIAstat-Dx Gastrointestinal Panel 2 and infectious disease portfolio. BLLN’s move is more modest and tied mainly to company-specific updates like the recent appointment of a new VP of Medical Affairs and Clinical Development in Oncology, which modestly strengthens its leadership and clinical strategy narrative.

themes.name: Targeted Protein Degradation Oncology
themes.description: GLUE and KYMR are both trading higher this week primarily on continued momentum from recent positive clinical and pipeline developments, rather than a fresh single-day headline. For Kymera (KYMR), the key driver remains the late-June catalyst of accelerated completion of enrollment in the Phase 2b BROADEN2 trial of lead STAT6 degrader KT-621 in atopic dermatitis, which pulled forward the topline data timeline and de-risked the program, with follow‑through support from recent bullish research and July management/pipeline updates. For Monte Rosa (GLUE), the move is largely a sympathy/sector bid into targeted protein degradation names and positioning ahead of upcoming data/milestones, with no major new company‑specific announcement this week.

themes.name: Digital Payments Processing & Merchant Acquiring Networks
themes.description: FOUR is trading higher on continued follow‑through from last week’s upside earnings surprise and raised guidance, which has investors leaning into the name as a small‑cap payments winner ahead of broader fintech M&A chatter. PYPL is being driven almost entirely by the reported $53 billion, $60.50/share takeover offer from Stripe and Advent International, which has re-rated the stock toward the bid and fueled expectations for either a higher offer or competing interest. Both moves are being reinforced by traders positioning ahead of upcoming earnings and board responses to the PYPL bid.

themes.name: Digital Insurance Distribution & Marketplace Platforms
themes.description: The move in Porch Group (PRCH) and Goosehead Insurance (GSHD) this week appears to be driven more by technical momentum and renewed interest in the insurance/insurtech space than by a single, specific news catalyst. PRCH has seen a slight uptick in news volume versus its normal pace, but without a standout company-specific headline or earnings event this week, while GSHD’s recent strength is tied to a broader rerating as investors revisit its growth and valuation story. In other words, the catalyst is largely sector momentum and positioning rather than a discrete news item.

themes.name: Property & Casualty Insurance Underwriters
themes.description: The move looks mostly tied to broader insurance-sector strength plus company-specific analyst/earnings optimism: ACGL has a JPMorgan target raise, and its peers like THG, TRV, WRB, AXS, and SKWD are also being bid on expectations for solid pricing, underwriting margins, and favorable catastrophe-loss assumptions. For WRB specifically, the stock is also trading in a risk-on week where investors are leaning into insurers ahead of a packed macro/earnings calendar, which can amplify the group move. AXS is the outlier: unless you mean a different AXS ticker, the results here don’t show a clear insurer catalyst for Axie Infinity, so it may be a separate stock or a mistaken ticker.

themes.name: U.S. Regional & Mid-Cap Commercial Banks
themes.description: The main catalyst is a broad regional-bank bid this week, helped by strong Q2 results and guidance from large regionals that reinforced the view that deposit costs are easing, net interest margins are stabilizing, and credit quality remains solid. FITB is also getting a specific boost from its Comerica acquisition/integration story after reporting a Q2 beat and stronger adjusted EPS, while USB and CFG are benefiting from analyst upgrades and sector spillover. BOKF, UMBF, and EWBC look to be moving mostly with the same sector rotation rather than a single company-specific headline.

themes.name: Senior & Long-Term Care Healthcare REITs
themes.description: The main catalyst is a rotation into healthcare and senior-housing REITs as investors price in a more favorable long-term demand outlook for aging demographics and post-acute care, boosting LTC, NHI, OHI, and VTR together as a group. In addition, dividend stability/raises (e.g., VTR’s dividend increase) and upcoming earnings events are drawing incremental income-focused and defensively positioned capital into the space. There is no single stock-specific headline this week; it’s a sector move driven by yield, defensiveness, and demographics.

themes.name: Life Science Tools & Analytical Instruments
themes.description: The main catalyst is this week’s earnings setup: DHR reports July 21 and TMO reports July 23, and both names are trading with a pre-earnings bid. DHR is also getting support from continued strength in bioprocessing/biotechnology demand, while TMO has recent momentum from strong Q1 results, raised guidance, and AI/Clario-related optimism. MTD’s move looks more like sector sympathy and positioning ahead of the same life-sciences earnings window than a company-specific headline.

themes.name: Canadian Big Banks
themes.description: The main catalyst pushing BMO, BNS, CM, RY, and TD higher this week is the Bank of Canada holding its policy rate steady at 2.25%, which is supporting the broader TSX financials sector and easing concerns about further tightening. At the same time, investors are still reacting to very strong Q2 earnings from the Canadian banks, driven by sharply lower provisions for credit losses and solid capital markets revenue, which translated into notable earnings beats and dividend increases across the group. The combination of stable rates, improving credit trends, and dividend growth is fueling momentum buying in the sector.

themes.name: Open-Air & Strip Mall Retail REITs
themes.description: The move is mainly interest-rate-sensitive REIT strength: investors are rotating into property names like KIM, REG, FRT, UE, SKT and KRG as Treasury yields ease and the market prices in a more dovish Fed path. For KRG specifically, the stock has also been hitting fresh highs on the back of positive REIT sentiment and momentum.

themes.name: Packaged & Shelf-Stable Food Manufacturers
themes.description: CAG’s move is mostly a continuation of its post-earnings reaction and ongoing turnaround narrative under the new CEO after the recent dividend reset and impairment-driven loss; there is no fresh, stock-specific headline this week beyond digestion of those results and guidance. GIS is being supported by follow-through from its better-than-expected fiscal Q4 print and Jefferies’ positive cost-savings/efficiency commentary, which remains the main fundamental catalyst behind the recent strength. KHC’s strength looks more flow- and technical‑driven, aided by a bullish setup above key moving averages and lingering positive sentiment around its global restructuring and value/dividend case, rather than a new, discrete news item this week.

themes.description: The main catalyst pushing JLL, CIGI, and NMRK higher this week is improving sentiment around commercial real estate services and capital markets activity, highlighted by visible deal and financing wins. themes.name: Cloud Observability & AIOps Monitoring Platforms
themes.description: DT, PD, and DDOG are all benefiting from a renewed bid into observability/monitoring and incident-response names tied to AI infrastructure spend and cloud security, with Datadog in particular seeing momentum from strong Q1 results, rising revenue/EPS estimates, and aggressive AI product launches like Bits AI and GPU/LLM observability. The group is also catching support from recent analyst price target hikes and “Leader” recognition in observability, which reinforce the AI-driven growth narrative and institutional interest in the space. In the absence of stock-specific headlines this week for DT and PD, the move is largely a sympathy trade to DDOG’s AI/observability strength and positioning ahead of the next earnings prints.

themes.name: Specialty Catastrophe Property Insurance Underwriters
themes.description: The main catalyst driving Slide Insurance Holdings (SLDE) higher this week is its recent strong quarterly earnings beat combined with a newly authorized $100 million share repurchase program, which is lifting sentiment and attracting incremental institutional interest. Emerald Mutual Fund Advisers Trust’s disclosed share purchases are reinforcing the bullish narrative, while the stock’s technical setup near 52-week highs is drawing momentum and breakout traders.

themes.name: Financial Compliance & Regulatory Reporting SaaS
themes.description: WK’s strength in DFIN this week is being driven primarily by renewed interest in its AI-enabled disclosure and compliance tools, including the recently launched AI-powered iXBRL tagging capability within its Active Intelligence suite. Investors are also viewing DFIN as a beneficiary of the SEC’s proposed shift toward more flexible reporting (new Form 10-S), which highlights structural demand for its ActiveDisclosure platform. The combination of these regulatory and AI themes is catalyzing a rerating of the stock.

The same note also emphasizes increased capital return (share repurchase authorization lifted to $165M plus ongoing dividends) and a $14M Caltrans recompete win that reinforces long-term revenue visibility and contract momentum. HURN’s move is being supported by incremental institutional interest, including a newly disclosed $1.26M position by Y Intercept Hong Kong, alongside ongoing positive sentiment around its healthcare-focused M&A (RelateCare) and strong 2025 results.

themes.name: Genomic Medicine & Synthetic DNA Tools
themes.description: MRNA is being driven higher primarily by anticipation of the upcoming FDA decision on its seasonal flu vaccine mRNA-1010 (PDUFA date August 5) and, to a lesser extent, positioning ahead of its July 31 Q2 earnings call, both widely flagged as near-term catalysts. TWST’s strength is tied to improving sentiment on its growth and margin trajectory plus a recent price target hike from Canaccord Genuity to $120, which has reinforced the bull case despite mixed earnings and insider selling.

themes.name: Industrial Automation & Electrical Control Systems
themes.description: Cluster C — AME (Ametek, electronic instruments and electromechanical devices), HLIO (Helios Technologies, hydraulic and electronic motion control), HUBB (Hubbell, electrical products and grid infrastructure), and ROK (Rockwell Automation, industrial automation software and hardware) — are moving together with 0.82 correlation, all serving the industrial automation and electrical infrastructure build cycle. Re-shoring, factory modernization, and grid upgrades are driving multi-year capex in these systems. Average RS is currently modest (38) but the correlation cluster suggests early institutional positioning.

themes.name: AI Compute & GPU Data Center Hosting Operators
themes.description: The main catalyst is a broad rebound in AI data center / Bitcoin-miner-to-AI infrastructure names after multiple firms announced large, long‑term AI compute and data center lease deals, plus sector-wide optimism about AI demand and rising GPU pricing. Specifically, IREN’s jump is being driven by its disclosure of $2.8B in new AI cloud contracts and a higher year‑end ARR target above $4B, while APLD is reacting to fresh multibillion‑dollar hyperscaler lease agreements and debt financing that expand its contracted AI data center revenue; WULF and CORZ are moving in sympathy as part of the same former‑miner AI infrastructure cohort.

themes.name: Domestic Steel Producers
themes.description: CMC, NUE, RS, STLD, and TX form a tight correlation cluster (0.82 corr) of domestic U.S. steel producers all moving in tandem. Potential tariff protection on imported steel, infrastructure bill spending on rebar/structural steel, and domestic manufacturing re-shoring are the shared policy catalysts. While current avg RS of 35 is low, the cluster coherence suggests early institutional positioning — price action is consolidating before any narrative breakout, and domestic steel historically leads industrials when trade policy tightens.

themes.name: For-Profit Education & Workforce Training Providers
themes.description: The common driver across these names is renewed bullishness on for‑profit and career-focused education tied to earnings revisions and analyst upgrades in the consumer/education space. CVSA, PRDO, STRA, and UTI are benefiting from strong enrollment trends, EPS estimate revisions, and fresh price-target hikes that have put them on “earnings revisions” and “high visibility” screens for investors, while LINC and LAUR are moving in sympathy as part of the same education-services trade. Sector rotation into non-essential consumer/education names with clearer profit visibility this earnings season is amplifying the move.

themes.name: Diversified Medical Device & Life Science Instrument Manufacturers
themes.description: Procedure volume recovery post-COVID, hospital capital expenditure normalization, and the return of elective procedures are lifting a broad cohort of medical device and life science instrument companies. These names share exposure to hospital/lab capital budgets and procedure-driven consumables revenue that is rebounding as health systems stabilize finances.

themes.name: Bitcoin Mining & Crypto Infrastructure Operators
themes.description: CIFR, CORZ, HUT, IREN, and WULF are all pure-play Bitcoin miners with correlated price action (corr 0.84) tied directly to Bitcoin price levels and mining economics. While currently at low RS levels, this cluster moves as a unit on crypto sentiment shifts, and any sustained Bitcoin rally or mining economics improvement would create simultaneous breakouts across all names.

themes.name: Minimally Invasive & Robotic Surgical Device Platforms
themes.description: ISRG (robotic-assisted surgery), BSX (cardiac & endoscopic devices), and PEN (endoscopy equipment) are all leaders in minimally invasive surgical technologies, sharing tailwinds from hospital capital spending recovery, expanding robotic procedure volumes, and growing evidence supporting minimally invasive approaches over open surgery. These companies serve similar hospital capital purchasing committees and benefit from the same procedure volume normalization cycle.

themes.name: U.S. Defense Prime Contractors
themes.description: Escalating geopolitical tensions across multiple theaters (Europe, Middle East, Indo-Pacific) are driving sustained U.S. and NATO defense budget expansion, benefiting the five major U.S. prime contractors that dominate large-platform procurement. Congressional budget momentum and allied re-armament spending underpin multi-year backlog growth for these names.

themes.name: Traditional Staffing & Talent Placement Services
themes.description: MAN and RHI are both catching a bid off ManpowerGroup’s very strong Q2 print and above-consensus Q3 guidance, which is being read as a cyclical turn in staffing demand and a sector-wide re‑rating. On top of that, MAN’s outsized post-earnings move and heavy short-covering are amplifying flows into peer staffing names, while RHI is also benefiting from a positive news cycle around its sustainability, AI/Protiviti positioning, and workplace awards that is reinforcing the quality/ESG bull case.

themes.name: Franchise Auto Dealership Groups
themes.description: The main catalyst is the proposed Penske/Mitsui deal to take Penske Automotive Group (PAG) private via a $3.8 billion bid for the free float, which is lifting the entire auto retail group on takeout/speculation and valuation read-through. In addition, Seaport Research’s recent target hike on PAG and prior commentary that auto retail names like PAG, ABG, and SAH are well-positioned through digitization, acquisitions, and EV demand are reinforcing the move. Broader auto retail strength, with PAG showing strong technical “strong buy” signals and being viewed as undervalued versus history, is contributing to the sector bid.

themes.name: U.S. Petroleum Refining & Downstream Processing
themes.description: The group is being driven primarily by record-high U.S. refining margins, with the 3-2-1 crack spread spiking to all-time highs on the back of tight diesel and product supply, low inventories, and strong fuel demand. Geopolitical escalation between the U.S. and Iran and related disruptions to Middle Eastern and Russian product exports have further tightened global diesel markets, enhancing margin visibility for U.S. refiners. A supportive sell-side backdrop, including recent upgrades and buy ratings on VLO, PSX, DINO and MPC, is reinforcing the move as investors position for sustained strong Q3 refining economics.

themes.name: Enterprise Server, Storage & Data Infrastructure Hardware
themes.description: DELL, HPE and NTAP are being driven higher by a sector-wide rebound in AI infrastructure names after Super Micro Computer’s preliminary update showed sharply better gross margins, a record backlog and over $60 billion in new AI server orders, reinforcing confidence in the AI server demand cycle. This has prompted investors to rotate back into AI hardware and infrastructure suppliers, with Dell and HPE seen as key beneficiaries of stronger pricing and demand for enterprise AI buildouts, and NTAP rising on correlation and read-through to storage demand plus recent positive visibility events such as index inclusion and its NFL partnership.

themes.name: Powersports & Recreational Vehicle Manufacturers
themes.description: HOG is being driven higher primarily by Loop Capital’s upgrade to Buy from Hold with a higher $32 price target, coming just ahead of its Q2 earnings report and reinforcing a more optimistic outlook on the stock. For PII, the move is largely pre-earnings positioning within the powersports group, supported by Bank of America’s preview calling for results near the high end of guidance and highlighting solid fundamentals despite macro volatility.

themes.name: U.S. Apparel & Fashion Recovery
themes.description: CROX is higher mainly on a Baird upgrade to Outperform with a price target raise to $150, plus continued pre-earnings positioning ahead of its July 30 Q2 report; there’s no single fresh catalyst today.
